text.content: Operator authorized F2 going straight to LIVE + TUNABLE (overriding the shadow-first plan). Conviction now lifts the live suggested size; the former hard caps (TIER_BANDS ceiling, concentration cap, cap_room clamp, sell-gate-as-blocker, date gates) are removed as LIMITS and replaced with operator-visible, operator-tunable dials in src/sizing_tunables.json (conviction_size_slope ON by default; per-name/concentration soft-maxes OFF; sell_gate_blocks false; size_rounding_usd 100). Rationale: nothing auto-executes — the human places every trade, so a conviction-driven number is a recommendation to question, not money moving on its own; and no hidden caps the operator didn't set. Honesty rails preserved: lift fires ONLY on honest conviction (HIGH/MODERATE + >=2 converging independent groups + BUY + not conflicted) — correlated echoes, stale reads, LOW, and CONFLICTED get no lift; affordability is funding-aware (real cash + the sell-gate-ordered funding pool, ETF-excess only so a live single-name is never proposed as funding) shown as a NUMBER, never a block; all dollar amounts round to the nearest $100. Also shipped: F1 first-class CONFLICTED conviction posture (a genuine bull/bear split reads CONFLICTED/RE-CHECK, not a calm averaged NEUTRAL/LOW). Live on main: PR #73 (F1), #74 (F2 live+tunable), #75 (funding-aware), #78 ($100 rounding); each build->independent-verify->CI-green->merge off clean origin/main; pytest 1747 pass / 6 skip, golden drift-free. Supersedes the bounded-ceiling F2 design and the shadow-first plan. Companions: docs/sell_gate_and_sizing_

text.content: Operator-requested 2026-06-16, out of the live-dashboard UI critique. Elevates a sharper version of the Mission's passivity failure mode to a standing goal/principle and writes it onto the Boot Page (fetched every session). Core: passivity can be built into the system's OWN surfacing; a surface that nets out as 'here's why to wait' is a system failure, not neutral honesty. Standing job of every surface = faithfully amplify real edge into action (strength loud + one tap from action, weakness quiet, stakes loud, real opportunity most prominent, funding plumbing never outranks a real capital decision). Honesty guardrail (non-negotiable, what makes it safe): salience/framing of TRUE info only — never inflate weak evidence, manufacture conviction, hide risk, or push trades to hit a number; this STRENGTHENS the non-tunable honesty rails. Added a design test alongside the Mission drift test. Follow-up: mirror a one-line posture statement into the 'Remember our goals' recap + repo

text.content: Fresh Run 6/1. Oil futures spiked (WTI $94 +7.9%, Brent $97 +6.9%) on renewed Iran-US strikes, BUT the equity tape did NOT confirm a regime: SMH +1.9% to $607 (16% above Newton $525 Tenkan stop), XLE only +2.2% (lagging oil's +8%), URA -0.8%, gold -2.4%/silver -1.6%. Dual-edge (AI-multiple headwind / nuclear+defense tailwind) is theoretical, not priced. Iran narrative whipsawed de-escalation->escalation in ~12h -> wait for confirmation, don't trade the headline. DECISION: no repositioning today. TRIGGERS (the contingency/invalidation): De-risk AI if WTI >$95 sustained AND XLE confirms AND 10Y >4.75; OR SMH < $525 (Newton Tenkan) -> semis trend-break -> trim. text.content: Framework-wide (all sleeves)

rich_text.plain_text: Framework-wide (all sleeves)

text.content: Decided 2026-05-29. The framework was over-engineered for how the operator actually decides: it was convergent (per-session verdicts), ship-biased (must produce a rec each session), and exposed complexity the operator couldn't evaluate — so he trusted plausible local fixes that piled into rules that didn't work. His real operating style: accumulate conviction over time, stay light/flexible, react fast only when signals genuinely converge. ADOPTED: a 4-layer redesign — Collection (fetch + freshness check) → Sources (feeds with adjustable trust weights, extensible to Reddit/others, echo-chamber/independence guard) → Daily Analyst (reads paid sources daily, researches items of interest UNPROMPTED e.g. new Fundstrat Top 5, triages to priority few, NEVER decides) → Cockpit (simple readout: confidence read + plain-word drivers w/ why on drill-down + trajectory + bear-case + rotation + the rare clear decision). Two gears: Watch (default, ~95%) / Commit (exception, fired by a graduation event). Two labels per name: pipeline stage (Watching→Building→Ready→Committed) × risk class (Core/Tactical/Probe/Hedge; changes rarely). Graduation = a NAMED PATTERN (not a number), gated by a short adversarial memo (independence check, boring alternative, kill condition, expiry); fires a FRESH workup, never auto-trades. Conviction-direction read = logged event-driven trajectory + evidence-decay (absence of expected confirmation decays conviction). Rotation read = relative strength vs SPY + vs AI book, vocabulary anchored to data but never a trigger. Watch Ledger w/ bear-case tracked equally + expiration dates + attention cap. Premium-bleed guard. Honest-depth labeling on all autonomous research. Self-watching error/behavior log. DELETED the 'defer build to June 28 retrospective / operate-mode-blocks-build' rule — building now because the Trump-policy + AI opportunity window closes fast (~2026 midterms / new Congress Jan 2027); patient on entries, urgent on readiness+build. MODEL COUNCIL (ChatGPT + Gemini, no Perplexity Max) stress-tested and CORRECTED: do NOT collapse A/B/C into pipeline states — keep a separate risk class ('Ready for what?'); keep DOWNSIDE numeric triggers as hard circuit-breakers, soften only UPSIDE/entry triggers to flags; added over-watching guards (bear-case-equal, expiration, attention cap, evidence-decay, premium-bleed). Held against Council's two heaviest add-ons (full always-on short-thesis engine; hardcode-every-label) as recreating the bloat. Canonical docs: Notion 'Conviction Engine — System Philosophy Revision (Concept)' + 'Conviction Engine — Build Plan (staged)', both under Command Center. Build sequence: Stage 0 warm-start (seed cockpit from existing Notion + fresh pull, label old-vs-fresh) → Stage 1 lean v1 (all 4 layers thin) → Stage 2 grow. Pilot: critical minerals (patience half) + Fabrinet/FN (graduation/Commit half), optional BMNR (crypto/high-bar).

text.content: Operator decision 5/28/26. Repeatedly burned on crypto/ETH, nuclear, and critical minerals while the AI sleeve (high confidence) worked. This REFINES the 'under-sizing is the canonical failure / right-but-too-small' rule (memory #14): that lens applies to HIGH-CONFIDENCE sleeves (AI), not to the burned thematic sleeves. New posture for crypto/nuclear/minerals = MONITOR, not add. Their below-T1-floor sizing is now INTENTIONAL, not a gap to close — specifically BMNR (3.49% vs 8% floor) and LEU (4.89% vs 8% floor) are no longer flagged as conviction-sizing gaps. HOLD existing pieces; continuously monitor for the best re-entry. Add ONLY on (a) a genuinely high-confidence catalyst or macro regime-turn (dollar-WEAK turns minerals to tailwind; real-yields-DOWN turns crypto/nuclear to tailwind), or (b) a defined-risk asymmetric options play (capped loss). Implementation: memory #19 set; PENDING operator go — a CI behavioral rule (conviction-add suppression for MONITOR-stance sleeves; modifies P-WAKE-UP sustained-underdeployment trigger + conviction_sizing_calibrator output) plus a Live Theses Stance=MONITOR tag per affected ticker. Consequence: last session's Path B (BMNR/LEU conviction repair) is OFF the table; the breadth-rotation direction (Path A — financials/industrials/small-caps + a couple Top-5 picks) is the live plan.

text.content: CI bump v11.30 -> v11.31. Documentation-discipline release. No new principle (stays 18), no new script, no new command. Adds one behavioral standing rule: the Research Conviction Tag. PROMPTED BY a real blur in v11.28-v11.30 research output -- a HIGH-conviction structural model (the enabler depth-in-stack framework) and a set of LOW-conviction specific name picks (stale comparable prices, an empty screener pull, scan-depth only) were delivered in the same breath with no marker distinguishing their evidentiary strength, leaving a reader unable to tell solid research from a half-formed hypothesis. THE RULE: every research deliverable ending in an analytical conclusion (deepdives, multi-ticker scans, thesis write-ups, watch-list entries, comparative analyses, source syntheses) now closes with a RESEARCH CONVICTION block -- a HIGH/MODERATE/LOW rating + a Basis line (the concrete evidentiary reasons for the grade) + a Weakest Link line (one clear, complete, non-shorthand sentence naming the single thing most likely to make the conclusion wrong). The tag rates EVIDENCE QUALITY -- a separate, independent axis from the Two-Lens A/B/C opportunity tier (which rates how attractive the opportunity is) and from P-SOURCE-CALIBRATION (which scores the hit rate of external named sources). A Tier-A-looking opportunity carrying a LOW research-conviction tag is, by construction, a stop-and-recheck signal. REJECTED: a 19th principle (documentation discipline, not a gating principle with a forcing function -- same reasoning as v11.28's rejection of Inbound Synthesis as a principle); a numeric/1-10 conviction score (three named bands resist false precision); folding the tag into the Two-Lens A/B/C tier (the evidence-quality axis and the opportunity-attractiveness axis must stay independent). Canonical full text: Custom_Instructions_v11_

text.content: Operator flagged a recurring systemic issue: he tends to assume Claude operates with brain-like continuity — that Claude will autonomously do the logical next step and carry context forward — when in fact Claude only carries what is structurally encoded. Each chat is a fresh instance with no memory of prior chats except (a) what is written to Notion, (b) the ~30 memory edits, (c) the project files / CI. Within a chat Claude responds to the request plus whatever the CI/commands structurally direct; it does NOT autonomously pursue 'the obvious next step' unless that step is encoded as a CI rule, command step, auto-fire trigger, or a DB row a launcher reads. Un-encoded next steps evaporate at chat end. CONCRETE INSTANCE FOUND THIS SESSION: P-CLOSURE_v11_

CONCLUSION: quantum is a real, important frontier theme but NOT a high-conviction trade — classified soft Tier B SPECULATIVE (Asymmetric Sleeve), not Tier 1/Generational. Reasons it is not high conviction: no named research anchor (no Fundstrat/Meridian/Lee quantum pick — Two-Lens Cat 1 absent); sector +161% trailing 12mo (post-run entry); no valuation floor (~100-300x sales, negative EBITDA, beta ~3-4, ~75% drawdown Oct'25-Mar'26); 'wrong horse' risk (winner may be IBM/Google or a private). KEY 5/26 RESEARCH UPDATES: (a) the $2B Commerce Dept program announced 5/21 is letters-of-intent not completed deals, and IONQ is NOT a recipient — RGTI/QBTS are (~$100M each); (b) IONQ carries a Feb-2026 Wolfpack short-seller report alleging a $54.6M revenue 'black hole'; (c) Trump-admin ties present (Feinberg/Cerberus held ~$134M IONQ; FT flagged beneficiary-firm ties) — dual-edge, no active investigation; (d) UW options flow = retail-momentum froth, not smart-money conviction; (e) IONQ 13F ownership is passive-index/quant-dominated. text.content: tracker.py

rich_text.plain_text: tracker.py

text.content:
: detects when ONE source repeats ONE ticker inside a rolling window. Deliberately TIER-BLIND — it counts Tier D. Thresholds: 3-in-30d, or accelerated 2-in-14d if any mention is Tier A/B. Escalation gate is core-vs-non-core, NOT held-vs-unheld: a non-core ticker cluster fires LOUD (P-WAKE-UP + Signal Log row); a core-monitored name logs QUIET. HYPE was held as dust — a held/unheld gate would have suppressed it; the core gate escalates it LOUD.

STRUCTURAL LEARNING (the durable lesson). The tier ladder structurally under-weights crypto and long-horizon thematic content from Farrell and Meridian, because that content is mostly Tier C/D narrative ('should / favor / looking for') — unfalsifiable by design, therefore unscoreable. And the existing source-convergence wake-up (>=3 INDEPENDENT streams) is a chorus detector: it is deaf to a soloist. One analyst pounding one name for a month produces no convergence signal and no Cat 1 weight. The persistence detector is the mechanism that closes that gap — it watches for repetition by a single voice, tier-blind.

VERIFICATION. v11.29 shipped and uploaded to project: CI v11.29, source_call_

rich_text.plain_text:
: detects when ONE source repeats ONE ticker inside a rolling window. Deliberately TIER-BLIND — it counts Tier D. Thresholds: 3-in-30d, or accelerated 2-in-14d if any mention is Tier A/B. Escalation gate is core-vs-non-core, NOT held-vs-unheld: a non-core ticker cluster fires LOUD (P-WAKE-UP + Signal Log row); a core-monitored name logs QUIET. HYPE was held as dust — a held/unheld gate would have suppressed it; the core gate escalates it LOUD.

STRUCTURAL LEARNING (the durable lesson). The tier ladder structurally under-weights crypto and long-horizon thematic content from Farrell and Meridian, because that content is mostly Tier C/D narrative ('should / favor / looking for') — unfalsifiable by design, therefore unscoreable. And the existing source-convergence wake-up (>=3 INDEPENDENT streams) is a chorus detector: it is deaf to a soloist. One analyst pounding one name for a month produces no convergence signal and no Cat 1 weight. The persistence detector is the mechanism that closes that gap — it watches for repetition by a single voice, tier-blind.

VERIFICATION. v11.29 shipped and uploaded to project: CI v11.29, source_call_

(49/49 self-tests, up from 27), standalone P-SOURCE-CALIBRATION_v11_29 spec, updated Principles file. The 6 Farrell HYPE notes backfilled into the Source Call Log with Backfill=true and Outcome=Unscored — excluded from the scored hit-rate denominator to protect the no-retrofitting rule, but still fed to the detector. persistence_scan over the backfilled rows confirms: Farrell->HYPE fires 6x in 30d [LOUD], tier_mix {D:5, C:1}, any_tier_ab=false — i.e. six notes carrying zero scoreable weight still correctly trip a LOUD wake-up. Working as designed.

OPEN FOLLOW-UP (6/28 retrospective). The persistence thresholds (3-in-30d; accelerated 2-in-14d) are pilot defaults — recalibrate once the Source Call Log has more density. Also flag: a plausible index-macro false-positive mode — Newton's same-day batches of SPX/SPY/QQQ macro calls could cluster trivially; candidate fixes = index-ticker exclusion or same-day dedup. Principle count stays 18 (this was a repair, not a 19th principle).

text.content: Cross-project Notion audit (run from the Investing 2026 project, 5/27/26) found the Investing hub had accumulated three generations of databases — current-framework DBs layered over v7–v10-era legacy DBs that were never archived. The 💰 Finance page held ~22 databases (8 legacy) plus ~30 stale one-off pages. The CI 'Project Knowledge Pointers' table had drifted from reality: it claimed 16 Notion DBs, did not match actual IDs, omitted ~8 DBs physically present, and ~6 CI-canonical DBs were orphaned off the hub. ACTIONS: (1) created '📈 Investing 2026 — Command Center' (36dc5031-4bb6-8163-ad59-dc2fbfac6cad) as the new front door; (2) created '🗄️ Investing — Archive' (36dc5031-4bb6-813d-a5ff-d8bcb6b84b53); (3) moved 8 legacy databases (Trade Journal, Source Calls, Active Thesis Frameworks, Framework Drift Flags, Sleeve Targets, Briefing Tickers, Fundstrat Triage Log, Session Handoff Capsule) plus 26 stale pages into Archive. Databases were MOVED not trashed — cron-safe (workflows reference DBs by ID) and fully reversible. OPEN ITEMS: dedup the two Catalyst Calendar DBs (CI '35fc5031...81c5' vs Finance-hub 'a3576fb6...'); reparent orphaned DBs (13F Deltas, Active Recommendations Digest) under the Command Center; ship CI v11.30 with corrected pointer table + count. Life OS and Work workspace cleanup handed off to their own projects via paste-ready handoff prompts.

text.content:
UW MCP options-flow debug session 5/26/26. Three tool issues researched against UW API docs (get_public_api_docs) + support KB (get_support_info), reproduced, and tested live.

(1) 'No approval received' on get_flow_alerts + get_stock_screener — NOT a UW API error (term does not exist anywhere in UW's system per docs/KB). It is a transient CLIENT-SIDE MCP connector-approval gate. RESOLVED: both endpoints re-tested 5/26 and respond normally. If it recurs: retry once; if persistent, re-approve the UW connector in Claude Settings > Connectors. Not a code or parameter fault.

(2) get_stock_screener returned empty []. ROOT CAUSE: the ticker param does NOT accept pipe-delimited input. Tested 5/26 — 'IONQ|RGTI|QBTS|QUBT|QTUM' returns []; 'IONQ,RGTI,QBTS,QUBT,QTUM' returns full data for all five. FIX: use COMMA-separated tickers. This corrects the stale session-open note that says 'pipe-delimited ticker string' — that note is the bug.

(3) get_option_trades returned wrong tickers (INTC/IWM when IONQ was requested). ROOT CAUSE: the ticker_symbol filter is fully non-functional — the MCP server ignores it and returns the market-wide tape regardless. Confirmed via clean test 5/26 (ticker_symbol=IONQ, no sort, no other filters -> still only INTC/IWM). The existing CI 'Patch G' note is WRONG: the bug is NOT the order=premium combination; removing the sort does not fix it. FIX/WORKAROUND: use get_flow_alerts for per-ticker options flow — its ticker_symbol filter works (verified single-ticker AND comma-separated multi-ticker 5/26). Per-contract tape also available via UW REST endpoint /api/option-contract/{id}/flow. Treat get_option_trades as market-wide-only; never use for a single name.

CI PATCH-BACKLOG (fold into next CI version): (a) replace Patch G text with the corrected get_option_trades note; (b) correct the session-open 'pipe-delimited' note to comma-separated; (c) add the corrected UW tooling notes to Operational_Reference_-_

text.content: BUILT TODAY (5/19/26 evening session). 18th principle. Addresses operator concern that pundits broadcasting widely develop language patterns partially unfalsifiable by design (survivorship bias + hedge-and-timeframe selection). Framework's existing source-correlation defense protects against being wrong about source weight but does NOT measure whether each source actually has edge. Solution: pre-register every Newton/Lee/Meridian/Farrell call at the time it is made, tag by structural quality tier (A/B/C/D), score Win/Loss/Push at window end, compute rolling hit rate by source×tier, discount signal weight in Two-Lens Cat 1 when n≥15. text.content:
P-MACRO-CONTEXT added as 17th principle. Forces systematic macro reads (Treasury curve, dollar, credit, commodities, vol regime) before every Tier A/B capital action and on session-open.

TRIGGER: every session-open + before any Tier A/B capital action.

REQUIRED READS:
- US Treasury curve (2Y, 10Y, 30Y + 2s10s + 10s30s spreads)
- Inflation expectations (5Y5Y breakevens, TIPS)
- Credit spreads (LQD vs UST, HYG vs UST OAS)
- Dollar (DXY level + 5d change via UUP)
- Vol regime (VIX + MOVE)
- Commodities (WTI via USO, gold via GLD, copper)
- Fed/policy (Fed funds futures, 10Y real yield)

SURFACE FORMAT (pre-flight line):
MACRO: 10Y X.XX% (delta Nbp 5d) | 2s10s +Xbp | DXY XX.X | VIX XX.X | MOVE XX | WTI $XX.XX | Fed Jun cut prob X% | Real 10Y X.XX%
[ALERT triggers: 10Y >4.75% / 2s10s flips / MOVE >120 / DXY weekly delta >2% / VIX >25 / WTI ±5% in 5d / Real 10Y delta ±25bp in 5d]

DECISION FEED LOGIC:
- Long-LEAPS / high-P/E growth: 10Y rising = headwind to multiple
- Cyclicals / financials: curve steepening = tailwind
- Critical minerals: DXY weakness = tailwind, oil/inflation strong = tailwind
- BMNR / crypto: real 10Y yields rising = headwind
- AI hedge sizing: MOVE elevated = upsize hedge
- Global-export names: DXY strong + yen unwind = headwind

IMPLEMENTATION: macro_pulse_

text.content:
P-DEEPWORK added as 16th principle (after P-IV-CONTEXT). Multi-turn deepwork workflow for major decisions.

TRIGGER (any fires): capital action ≥$25K notional / Generational Lane entry-upsize-trim / source-cluster ≥3 named sources / framework principle change / operator says 'deep work' or 'multi-turn' or 'plan it out'.

5-PHASE STRUCTURE:
- Phase 0: Setup (confirm scope, sequence, success criteria)
- Phase 1: Source Intake (reads only, no recs)
- Phase 2: Data Layer (UW pulls, scripts, structured data only)
- Phase 3: Analysis (P-REASONING-ARCH 5-component inline: Devil's Advocate -> Counterfactuals -> Kelly -> Coherence -> CoVe)
- Phase 4: Synthesis + Recs (with explicit exit triggers per P-INTEGRITY symmetry)

FORCING FUNCTIONS:
- Phase 1 ends with no recommendations
- Phase 2 ends with structured data, not qualitative calls
- Phase 3 surfaces devil's advocate BEFORE Kelly
- Phase 4 must include exit triggers
- Cross-decision interaction effects surfaced explicitly
- Phase 3+4 NEVER foldable for Tier A entries (allowed for time-bound exit decisions per operator override)

CALIBRATION EVIDENCE: Session 5/18/26 SMH/BMNR/LEU cluster decision exceeded single-turn capacity. Operator approved multi-turn structure mid-session. Existing single-turn Two-Lens + P-REASONING-ARCH design assumed bounded scope; framework needed explicit multi-turn pattern for cluster decisions.

WHY PRINCIPLE NOT WORKFLOW: gates real decisions about how to allocate session structure for major capital events. Failure mode is silent — operator gets single-turn analysis on Tier A decision that needed multi-turn rigor, doesn't realize until after fill.

SESSION VERIFICATION 67 (added v11.24): Multi-turn deepwork structure triggers automatically on $25K+ Tier A entries; phase gates respected; cross-decision integration explicit in Phase 4.

IMPLEMENTATION: workflow principle, no script required. Operator's 'deep work' command word triggers; otherwise auto-fires on objective thresholds.

PRINCIPLES COUNT: 15 -> 16.

text.content: Operator asked: 'are we over engineering? will this slow the system down too much or one time work by you for ongoing benefit? i only want the latter. and i dont have time to constantly be monitoring and making trades. that's why i want fewer but very high value but need high confidence. if we need some major architectural changes, now's the time. separately, is there an existing system i can buy that would do me better?' Honest answer: yes partially over-engineering, the 15 P-codes + 15+ Notion DBs carry cognitive overhead even if Claude maintains it, most CI 'patches' are operational tweaks not principles, pre-flight surfaces 10+ lines on every session-open when 80%+ should be silent. Nothing meaningfully better to buy — the value here is personalization + memory + source-library integration that no commercial tool matches; supplement with Fidelity Active Trader Pro (free, you already have account) for execution + alerts + mobile push, optionally add Seeking Alpha Premium for 3rd-stream source breadth ($240/yr). Then: parallel-chat sent 4-phase build proposal (MOP-v1 + Sleeve Decelerator + Factor Stress-Test + Risk Governor over 2 weeks). Correctly identified 2 real risks (asymmetric tail risk + system unavailability during stress) but proposed solutions: (a) duplicated existing capability (factor stress-test = portfolio_factor_

text.content: Operator said 'go' again. Picked Gap D (portfolio factor exposure) from remaining queue. Selected because: (1) closes meaningful gap in portfolio coherence visibility — framework explicitly cares about factor stacking via P-FACTOR and P-REASONING-ARCH Component 4; (2) supports source-independent sleeve discipline by making epistemic correlation risk measurable; (3) pure AEM Cat A safety; (4) surfaces an actually-useful insight on operator's portfolio (the Meridian-CM stack). Design: factor membership is binary not weighted (avoids false precision); positions contribute full MV to each factor (conservative); thresholds 25%/40% align with P-REASONING-ARCH C4; source-factor stack requires ≥3 positions matching both (matches insider cluster threshold). Diversity via HHI + effective-N (1/HHI, interpretable). Operator-curated theme_tags is canonical — no auto-inference (staleness/bias risk). Untagged positions explicitly surfaced — forces operator to maintain Live Theses Theme Tags. REJECTED: auto-rebalancing on warning, beta-weighted exposure, canonical factor taxonomy in script, auto-Theme-Tag inference from ticker.

text.content: Operator said 'go' — picked highest-leverage remaining gap from System Architecture analysis queue. Gap E (insider activity batch scan) chosen because: (1) HIGHEST user-pref alignment — prefs explicitly require recent insider buys with dates including Trump-ally buys for every stock conversation; (2) pure AEM Cat A safety (code + tests, no Notion DB creation, no operator decisions needed); (3) immediate Monday pre-flight value (fires on next session-open); (4) closes real gap (per-ticker exists in deepdive_runner T1 item, portfolio-wide periodic scan didn't). Architecture mirrors outcome_logger / position_sizer pattern: pure-logic core, synthetic-fixture tests, CLI for ad-hoc + integration point with session_open_preflight. Constants explicitly traced to memory rule #28: BULLISH_BUY_THRESHOLD_USD = 500_000 (memory: 'buys >$500K = bullish'), SIGNIFICANT_SALE_THRESHOLD_USD = 500_000 (memory: 'under $500K = noise'), CLUSTER_MIN_INSIDERS = 3 (memory: '3+ direction same Q'), CATALYST_PROXIMITY_DAYS = 30 (memory: 'sale 30 days pre-catalyst = flag'). Edge cases tested: 10b5-1 filtering, RSU grant filtering, option exercise filtering, invalid date handling, same-insider-3x NOT cluster, $500K boundary firing, mixed signals flagged. REJECTED: auto-fire Two-Lens on insider alone (it's Cat 5 of 8, one signal not full trigger), per-trade dollar tracking (event-driven scan is sufficient), insider sentiment scoring (false-confidence risk), auto-Trump-ally classification (staleness + bias risk).

text.content: to prove the diff-detection works. Script takes two portfolio snapshots + optional Active Rationales + Live Theses payloads, infers 4 event types (FULL_EXIT, PARTIAL_EXIT, SIZE_INCREASE, NEW_POSITION), prorates cost basis on partial exits, aggregates across accounts to avoid false-positive exits from account transfers, looks up source/tier/lane from Active Rationales + Live Theses (Theses preferred for tier), generates Notion DB payloads. Edge cases handled: missing cost basis (flag, leave PnL null), no matching rationale (flag), tier conflict (Theses wins), stock splits / mergers (round_trip_suspected flag). 27 unittest cases verify: event type detection, prorated PnL math (LEU 500→300 shares producing exactly $8K realized correctly), cross-account aggregation (transfer is not an exit), realistic operator-portfolio simulation (ANET fully exited at -$900 loss correctly inferred with Fundstrat Top 5 source pull-through). Schema design (in /mnt/user-data/outputs/) lists 17 properties with auto-vs-manual classification. Operator-only fields: Outcome Tag (subjective, optional, can batch-tag quarterly) and Operator Notes (rich text, optional). All other fields auto-populated. Once 1-2 quarters of data accumulate, the calibrator can compute: win rate by Tier, win rate by Source, realized 'right but too small' dollar amounts, hold-duration analysis, source-cluster coherence audit. Approval question posed to operator: (1) create Notion DB with designed schema, (2) wire outcome_

text.content:
Absolute IV figures ('85% IV') are uninformative without context. The framework's v11.12 IV Context Overlay already codifies IVR-based cheap/normal/expensive classification, but execution gap meant Claude was characterizing IV using absolute figures, recommending structures that inverted the overlay's prescription.

Specific calibration failure 5/15/26: BMNR at 75-90% IV labeled 'expensive' → recommended verticals over LEAPS. Live pull showed BMNR IVR=3.3 (bottom of 52-week range) → framework actually prescribed CHEAP/LEAP_CALL/+15% sizing. Same gap would have inverted recommendations on LEU (IVR 13.4, IV-RV spread -28 vol pts) and MP (IVR 22.1).

P-IV-CONTEXT makes the rule a forcing function: IV Context Block (header + 3 substantive lines covering IVR, IV-vs-RV, Term/Composite/Structure/Sizing) must surface before any options structure advice. Banned phrases enforced ('IV is high/elevated/rich/expensive/low/cheap' without paired IVR).

Composite classification: IVR primary, IV-vs-RV refines edge cases. NORMAL IVR + DISCOUNT IV-vs-RV → CHEAP composite. NORMAL IVR + PREMIUM IV-vs-RV → EXPENSIVE composite.

Reframes existing v11.12 overlay (uw_iv_

text.content:
renders 16-item Tier 1 + Tier 2 checklist at top of every Deepdive response before any analytical content. Closes the gap where ANET deepdive (this session) hit 5/16 items before operator pushback surfaced the miss. Modes: tier1 (8), deepdive (15), deepdive+tier_a (16), full+tier_a (20).

PATCH C — Runner-coverage validation meta-principle as P-SIMPLICITY extension. Every script-shipping patch must verify 4 legs: (1) path, (2) self-test passes, (3) CI references the script in relevant launcher step, (4) end-to-end fire on trigger condition demonstrated. Calibration: ASTS re-entry zone alert miss 5/14/26 — script shipped + CI referenced but leg 4 missing; stock went $63.85 → $84.27 with operator under-sized at 0.28%.

PATCH D — MU above-Street consensus framing rule. Held position >25% above analyst consensus PT for >60d fires mandatory re-check covering factor-correlation audit, thesis-source re-read, hard-stop adequacy. NOT auto-trim. Calibration: MU +22.4% above consensus on 4/27/26 — gap closed via Street PT raises (Street was wrong, not price); a re-check on 4/27 would have produced actionable framework hygiene without forcing mean-reversion trade.

PATCH E — P-STAGE token budget staging as 14th principle (operator-confirmed P-code 5/4/26, never landed until now). Thresholds: 1 deep dive OR 2 mid-depth OR multi-ticker scan-depth comparison (4+ names) per session. When operator requests 3+ deepdives, P-STAGE fires FIRST with staging proposal + Research Queue write before any deepdive begins. Calibration: this session's request for ANET+CRS+FIX+LITE+IESC+STRL deepdives.

text.content: Asymmetric Sleeve (Lane 4); Speed Lane (Bottom 5 funding-source reframe); all sleeves where Fundstrat-named picks were treated as multi-year anchors.

rich_text.plain_text: Asymmetric Sleeve (Lane 4); Speed Lane (Bottom 5 funding-source reframe); all sleeves where Fundstrat-named picks were treated as multi-year anchors.

text.content:
Operator surfaced Fundstrat's official methodology Overview for the Large Cap and SMID-Cap Top/Bottom Ideas lists. TWO FINDINGS INVALIDATE PRIOR CI FRAMING:

(1) Newton's monthly ranking is ~1-month timing, not multi-year conviction. Per Fundstrat methodology: Lee builds the universe (long-term favorable reward/risk); Newton ranks within universe on ~monthly basis for ~1-month outperformance vs benchmark. CI v11.17 ranked 'Fundstrat Top 5 Large-cap or SMID with stop + target' as #2 in named-anchor strength (just below Meridian, above Lee Granny Shots ETFs). This conflated Newton's monthly timing with multi-year thesis anchor strength. Tier A requires multi-month named thesis; Top 5 standalone does not meet that bar.

(2) Bottom 5 are STILL favorable long-term. Per methodology: 'Both Top and Bottom Ideas are viewed favorably over the long term.' Bottom 5 = Newton's near-term underperformance call within long-term-favorable names. Intended use case: 'where to allocate new capital or which existing positions to trim to raise cash.' This is a funding-source flag, NOT a thesis-break signal. v11.15 Bottom-5 cross-check remains useful but reframes intent.

DECISIONS:
- Top 5 (Large-cap or SMID) standalone = Tier B-eligible named anchor (1-month tactical timing layer)
- Top 5 + multi-month thesis anchor on same ticker (Meridian-named, operator-validated with external corroboration, Lee Granny Shots top weight, hyperscaler-anchor contract) = Tier A becomes available; Top 5 layer adds Newton's near-term timing confirmation
- REPEATED-ENDORSEMENT MULTIPLIER: ticker that survives >=3 consecutive monthly Top 5 reviews -> eligible for Tier A standalone anchor (Newton's repeated re-endorsement across multiple re-reviews = conviction signal across Lee+Newton)
- Bottom 5 reframes from 'trim/sell signal' to 'funding-source flag.' Surface continues at session-open per v11.15 Rule 1, but auto-fire trim recommendations remain explicitly NOT triggered. Operator-decision-point preserved.
- Monthly adds/drops between consecutive months tracked as separate signal stream - name dropped from Top 5 = 'Newton no longer expects near-term outperformance,' NOT thesis-break. Adds = new Newton near-term endorsement worth a fresh Two-Lens.

SOURCES (full citation): Fundstrat 'Large Cap Top & Bottom Ideas' and 'SMID-Cap Top & Bottom Ideas' Overview methodology pages (operator-shared screenshots, 5/15/26). Cross-references: Fundstrat_Allocation_

text.content:
RE-SURFACED ASYMMETRIC OPPORTUNITIES UNDER RECALIBRATED FRAMEWORK (will validate over time):

- ANET ($177.73 4/22 -> $144.86 5/15 = -18.5%, still on Top 5 list) = cleanest re-entry-below-naming setup. Setup was invisible under prior framing because Top 5 inclusion looked stale; under new framing, it's an active Newton-endorsed timing call you'd be entering BELOW Fundstrat's add price.

- LITE & FIX (both added 2/6/26, on list 5/15 = 3+ monthly reviews survived) -> newly elevated as repeated-endorsement candidates eligible for Tier A standalone.

- AMD ATH-pursuit setup unchanged (Tier B with options expression only; was already AVOID-EQUITY-EXPRESS-VIA-OPTIONS).

- LEU LEAPS unchanged (anchored by Meridian multi-year HALEU thesis, not by Fundstrat Top 5).

- Bottom 5 hits cross-check continues firing in pre-flight but reframed surface language.

v11.18 PATCH CANDIDATE (separate work, not part of this DL entry):

rich_text.plain_text: BMNR options (asymmetric sleeve); BMNR underlying (Generational lane)

text.content: Sources: Fundstrat Tom Lee (Block 5/14 Senate Banking 15-9; Farrell 5/14 1:36pm ET) + Own Analysis + News/Current Events. On 5/14/26 PM session, operator returned to BMNR options request (had deferred AM session to 5/15 9am). Two-Lens run: 4/7 firing (Cat 1 named/external-corroborated, Cat 3 CLARITY <60d, Cat 4 Lee 6-pillar macro, Cat 7 Tier-1 13F). Tier A classified. Game B IV overlay fired CHEAP (IVR 10.5) — LEAP_CALL structure recommended across all horizons, +15% sizing modifier clamped at Tier A 15% heat cap = $4,500, then AMBER discount -20% (theme overlap with existing BMNR underlying) = $3,600 effective ceiling. 3-ticket draft framework built: Aug21 $30C×5 SHORT, Jan'27 $40C×7 MID in Roth, Jan'28 $50C×2-3 LONG. Operator iterated through alternatives ($95c rejected as too-tail, $60c rejected at $90 crossover analysis, $50c selected). Operator separately floated selling 100 BMNR shares (~$2K) to fund Jan'28 options. CLAUDE REJECTED with three-part logic: (a) aggregate would breach RED cap ($5,500 vs $4,500 hard ceiling); (b) 100 shares = $2,200 underlying delta replaced by Jan'28 $50C×4 ≈ $9,200 delta = 4x leveraging at the exact T1 trigger that says ADD to underlying not subtract; (c) underlying is Generational core, options are convex layer — selling core to fund layer inverts structure and caps base-case outcome to fund higher-variance one already exposed to. MATERIAL LEARNING: when a named T1 upgrade trigger fires on a Generational name, the correct directional move on underlying is ADD, not convert to leverage. The convex options layer is additive, not substitutive. No fills executed this session. P-NO-FILL-NO-FACT: all three tickets remain DRAFTS pending 5/15 9am ET re-engagement.

text.content:
Live UW data snapshot taken late PM 5/14/26. Documents IV regime state for retrospective calibration of Game B IV Context Overlay (shipped v11.12 Patch 1).

=== CHEAP regime (LEAP_CALL +15% modifier) ===
LEU $192.32 — IVR 19.34, ATM IV 82.7%, vs 30d mean -12.3%. Both signals fire.
BMNR $22.00 — IVR 8.71 (extreme), ATM IV 80.8%, vs 30d mean -6.5%. 52w range $4-$161 makes this noteworthy.

=== EXPENSIVE regime (DIAGONAL/VERTICAL, -20% modifier) ===
AMAT $440.56 — IVR 95.18 (extreme), earnings TONIGHT postmarket. Implied move ±$55 (±12.5%). Defer LEAP_CALL until post-earnings IV crush.
MU $776.01 — IVR 89.92, ATM 92.8%, +26.6% vs 30d mean. Earnings 6/24.
SMH $578.34 — IVR 87.82, +20.8% vs mean. Basket cap; v11.10 SMH-default hedge put-spread sized correctly for this state.
NVDA $235.74 — IVR 76.58, +41.4% vs mean. Earnings 5/20 (6 trading days).
AVGO $439.79 — IVR 70.45, +28.7% vs mean. Earnings 6/3.

=== NORMAL regime ===
ASTS $83.01 — IVR 56.42 (+11% today, hit W8 move trigger; W8 scan found 0 stale strikes — chain repriced).
RKLB $132.55 — IVR 65.68, +7.3% vs mean.
GS $968.96 — IVR 31.58 (borderline cheap, 1 tick from threshold).
CRCL $123.88 — IVR 31.26 (borderline cheap).
AEM $192.66 — IVR 51.40, -13.0% vs mean (1-2 IV points from recent-crush reclassification → CHEAP).

=== Three substantive takeaways ===
1. LEU and BMNR are the only outright-LEAP candidates by IV state today. Framework cleanly differentiates them from the AI complex.
2. AMAT prints tonight at IVR 95. Post-earnings IV crush could reclassify CHEAP if conviction holds — pull get_stock_screener tomorrow AM.
3. AI complex uniformly expensive — adding outright LEAPs at IVR 70-95 here is the over-paying-for-premium failure mode. Wait or use diagonals.

=== W8 move-trigger scan ===
ASTS +11%/1d hit W8 trigger. Jan 2027 chain scanned (19 calls + 10 puts via v1.1 both-side): 0 stale strikes. Active trading, chain repriced. Clean negative confirms tool discriminates noise from signal correctly.

=== Pre-trigger watch ===
AEM at -13% vs 30d mean (recent-crush threshold is -15%). One more 2-3 point IV slip flips to CHEAP. GS at IVR 31.58 — one tick from CHEAP threshold.

text.content: Operator framing pre-trip assumed deal-day risk could trigger precipitous critical-minerals retraction (per Meridian Jan 23 note: 'deal with China = sector retraction near-term'). Research 5/14/26 reframes: Oct 2025 Busan truce already had Beijing agreeing to effectively eliminate REE export controls (Foreign Policy 5/13 + Al Jazeera 5/14 joint-statement coverage). That absorption is why MP is off 40% from $100 52w high and LEU is off 59% from $464 52w high since Oct 2025. The Trump-Xi 5/13-15 summit is NOT a fresh deal-day binary on critical minerals — it is a re-affirmation or status-quo extension. Pentagon 5/14 announcement (Bloomberg) launching plan to break China grip = structural reshoring confirmation NOT decoupling reversal. Asymmetric setup as of 5/14 evening: cheap IV on de-rated survivors (LEU IVR 19.3, MP IVR 23.0, UUUU IVR 18.6) with structural theses intact. Game B IV overlay (v11.12): cheap IV → LEAP_CALL recommended, +15% sizing modifier. LEU least exposed to summit headlines (HALEU anti-Russia, not anti-China); MP/UUUU still face Day-2 Friday 5/15 joint-statement binary headline risk. Forward principle for next analogous setup: when a named deal-day catalyst approaches, FIRST check whether a prior truce/agreement has already partially absorbed the move — sector positioning may already reflect the binary.

text.content:
CapEvaluation dataclass with 3 new fields (iv_context, iv_adjusted_max_debit, iv_adjusted_max_contracts), added iv_context parameter to evaluate_position(), wired CLI flags (--ivr / --atm-iv / --atm-iv-30d-mean / --atm-iv-back / --skew-slope / --target-dte / --catalyst-within-dte), added IV CONTEXT section to format_text_report() with clamp annotation. Backward-compatible — calls without IV flags behave identically to v11.10/v11.11.

Operational addition: multi-cap-breach warning. CI spec mandates hard clamp at Tier RED ceiling (heat cap) only — other caps (theme, delta, theta, vol) can be exceeded by +15% modifier when they were the binding constraint. Previously silent footgun (overall_gate computed pre-overlay shows GREEN even when iv_adjusted breaches theme by 15%). Now surfaces explicit warning note when modifier > 1.0 AND iv_adjusted_max_debit > max_additional_debit AND binding != position_heat. Spec-compliant (heat clamp unchanged); operator now sees the breach instead of relying on stale overall_gate.

Verified LEU canonical: IVR 20.35 + ATM IV 87.7% (Jan 2027) + 30d mean 94.25% classifies CHEAP, recommends LEAP_CALL, +15% modifier, $2050 iv_adjusted_max_debit CLAMPED at Tier A RED ceiling — matches CI v11.12 validation item 30.

=== TEST COUNT FINAL: 201/201 ===
- re_entry_zone_scan: 26
- tier_promotion_scan: 39 (test 12 rewritten to use deterministic ref_date — was tautological)
- 13f_quarterly_pull: 61 (+9 dedup tests)
- v11.12 existing suite: 75 (+1 multi-cap-breach test)

=== DEFERRED (not shipped) ===
- Notion property parser refactor (_select_name / _number / _rich_text / _title duplicated across 4 files). Pure cleanup. Defer until schema migration forces.
- Parabolic-screener composition strengthening: accept dict {ticker: (phase, score)} instead of set. Minor surface polish; caller can synthesize.

=== KEY LEARNING ===
CI changelog claims about test counts and ship status need code-verification, not just spec-write-up. The v11.12 'shipped 50/50 pass' claim survived because no one ran the test suite end-to-end after the changelog was written. Backstop: when CI changelog claims tests pass, the verifying session should actually run the suite. Cheap, catches drift.

Verified LEU canonical: IVR 20.35 + ATM IV 87.7% (Jan 2027) + 30d mean 94.25% classifies CHEAP, recommends LEAP_CALL, +15% modifier, $2050 iv_adjusted_max_debit CLAMPED at Tier A RED ceiling — matches CI v11.12 validation item 30.

=== TEST COUNT FINAL: 201/201 ===
- re_entry_zone_scan: 26
- tier_promotion_scan: 39 (test 12 rewritten to use deterministic ref_date — was tautological)
- 13f_quarterly_pull: 61 (+9 dedup tests)
- v11.12 existing suite: 75 (+1 multi-cap-breach test)

=== DEFERRED (not shipped) ===
- Notion property parser refactor (_select_name / _number / _rich_text / _title duplicated across 4 files). Pure cleanup. Defer until schema migration forces.
- Parabolic-screener composition strengthening: accept dict {ticker: (phase, score)} instead of set. Minor surface polish; caller can synthesize.

=== KEY LEARNING ===
CI changelog claims about test counts and ship status need code-verification, not just spec-write-up. The v11.12 'shipped 50/50 pass' claim survived because no one ran the test suite end-to-end after the changelog was written. Backstop: when CI changelog claims tests pass, the verifying session should actually run the suite. Cheap, catches drift.

text.content: Operator asked whether JPMorgan's JLTXX tokenized money market fund news moves the needle on BMNR or is already factored in. Claude's initial response leaned on stale memory text ('CLARITY Act: Passed House but NOT enacted law — do not treat as enacted catalyst') and surfaced 'CLARITY past committee' as a FUTURE gate to watch. In fact the Senate Banking Committee had passed CLARITY markup 15-9 on 5/14, firing the canonical BMNR T1 upgrade trigger #1, and the Live Theses BMNR row had been updated same day to authorize T1 partial step-up to 7% with Tranche 1 $12K execution scheduled 5/15-5/16. The CI failure modes engaged: (a) v11.14 Rule 1 Position-Held auto-route requires Live Theses + Latest Portfolio fetch before answering named-ticker questions on held positions — Claude skipped the Live Theses read on a thesis-impact question; (b) P-READ-OR-PUNT trigger fired (ticker mentioned, sector/research-source stance involved) but Claude treated memory text as canonical instead of the Live Theses row. Pattern: when memory contains a fact about a named ongoing catalyst (legislation, regulatory milestone, named-binary outcome) that's older than the most recent session that referenced that catalyst, the memory fact must be treated as stale until Live Theses / web_search / Fundstrat Inbox confirms current state.

text.content:
OPERATOR REQUEST: 'Check it again with fresh eyes.'

QA METHODOLOGY: Read full 558-line file cold, scanned for (a) internal contradictions, (b) broken references, (c) undefined terms, (d) duplicated spec, (e) UUID format consistency, (f) numeric threshold consistency.

BUGS FIXED (6):

1. GRNY threshold inconsistency. Line 164 said ≥2.40%, line 192 said ≥2.50%. Direct contradiction on same threshold. FIX: Normalized to ≥2.40% (matches v11.7 patch + Launcher Step 4).

2. Tier A/B sizing table contradicted v11.10 sizing-to-caps math. Table on line 185 still showed legacy '$3-5K, LEAPS 12mo+' and '$1-3K, 2-6mo spreads' patterns. Line 415 explicitly retired these: 'size to maximum allowed by caps, NOT historical $3-5K Tier A debit pattern.' FIX: Replaced table sizing column with 'sizing per caps math (max allowed by 4 caps, NOT $3-5K legacy)' pointing to caps math section.

3. Bottom-5 surface contract violated. v11.15 Rule 1 (line 306) said 'Surface in pre-flight as Bottom-5 hits: N tickers held' but pre-flight format block (lines 254-265) omitted this line. FIX: Added 'Bottom-5 hits: B5 tickers held (v11.15 Rule 1)' between Promotion candidates and 13F deltas lines. Also added validation item 37 for it.

4. Catalyst Calendar UUID format mismatch. Line 53 had 35fc50314bb681c5ae90d8a84919999b (no dashes); all other UUIDs in the file used dashed format. FIX: Normalized to 35fc5031-4bb6-81c5-ae90-d8a84919999b.

5. Footer line-count claim wrong. Line 557 said 'Full text: ~680 lines' but actual was 557. Self-contradicting footer. FIX: Removed the incorrect line-count claim entirely.

6. Validation list mixed two categories. Items 31-35 verified shipped-and-passing code (already done this release). Items 36-40 verified next-session behavior. Mixing made operator's mental load worse. FIX: Separated into 'Shipped-and-verified' paragraph (the one-time work) + numbered 'Session-verification' items (every fresh-run). Cut from 10 numbered items to 7 numbered items + summary paragraph for the 5 implementation tests.

EFFICIENCY WINS (3):

7. Trump-admin Cat 8 stated 3 times (P-ASYMMETRIC Cat 8 + v11.14 Rule 2 + Tier A surfacing template). FIX: v11.14 Rule 2 is now a pointer ('see P-ASYMMETRIC Cat 8 (line in 8-category list). Canonical spec lives there.'). Saves ~3 lines, eliminates risk of future drift between three statements.

8. v11.13 stated twice (P-READ-OR-PUNT extension + standalone Behavioral Standing Rules section). FIX: Behavioral Standing Rules v11.13 section is now a pointer ('See P-READ-OR-PUNT for full spec. Trigger: ... Mandatory reads: web_search + Fundstrat Inbox 7-day + conversation_search.'). Saves ~2 lines.

9. 'Continuation-autopilot turn' used on lines 251, 311, 317 but never defined. Operator may not know what triggers it. FIX: Added inline definition at first use ('any turn the operator's input is an explicit continuation cue — "continue," "go," "next," "ok," or implicit when prior turn ended with a pending action queue').

NET RESULT:
- Line count 557 → 559 (slightly larger by 2 lines despite consolidations — the validation reorganization + continuation-autopilot definition added net content)
- Removed: ~15 lines of duplicated spec
- Added: ~17 lines of (a) corrected Bottom-5 surface, (b) Tier table caps-math pointer, (c) clear validation separation, (d) continuation-autopilot definition
- Zero broken internal references remaining
- All numeric thresholds consistent (GRNY 2.40%, GRNJ 2.00%, Tier A caps not $3-5K)
- All UUIDs in dashed format

NON-FIXES (intentional — judged not worth changing):
- Line 84 'continuation-autopilot' definition does NOT contradict line 251 first-use definition; left as is
- 'Sleeves' terminology undefined inline but well-known in operator framework; pointer to Trading_Lanes_-_

text.content: check.py

rich_text.plain_text: check.py

text.content:
(~280 lines)
   - Operationalizes v11.16 Patch 3 Position-Size Verification
   - Pure-logic core: parse_memory_baselines (regex extraction with false-positive filtering), compute_drift, cross_reference
   - Heuristic memory parser catches explicit pct ('BMNR at 5%') and tier inference ('BMNR T2' → 5.5% midpoint)
   - False-positive filter excludes AI/ETF/USD/CI/etc. that look like tickers
   - CLI takes memory text + portfolio JSON; outputs drift table with ALARM_DRIFT flags at >25% drift
   - BACKTEST PASSES: BMNR memory 5% vs actual 3.87% (72793/1879245) correctly flags UNDERSIZED with P_UNDERSIZE_CANDIDATE and drift -22.5%

text.content:
(~330 lines)
   - 45 unit tests, 100% passing
   - Covers: ticker extraction, freshness banding, duplicate detection, OCC parsing, intrinsic computation, moneyness classification, action band classification, memory parsing, drift computation, alarm flagging, false-positive filtering
   - 3 explicit backtest cases: IVES today, SMH hedge position, LEU LEAPS long-dated, BMNR drift today

SMOKE TESTS PASS:
- recommendations_digest with 6-entry synthetic set: correctly groups FRESH/MEDIUM/OLDER, detects CRCL duplicate, flags 'New page' untitled entry
- options_expiry_preflight with IVES + LEU options: correctly identifies IVES ACTION_REQUIRED and LEU OK (600+ DTE)
- position_drift_check with 3-position memory + portfolio: correctly identifies BMNR UNDERSIZED (-22.5%) and SMH OVERSIZED ALARM_DRIFT (+28.2%)
- session_open_preflight unified summary output matches expected v11.17 format

INTEGRATION WORKFLOW (operator-side):
1. Claude (via Notion MCP) fetches Active Trade Rationales DB → exports to JSON
2. Claude (via Latest Portfolio Notion page) exports portfolio state to JSON
3. Claude reads operator memory text
4. Runs: python session_open_

text.content:
OPERATOR DIRECTIVE 5/14 late PM: 'Yes - autopilot - go' — authorizing autopilot execution on Path A interpretation + cleanup queue + missing rationales.

PATH A ACTIVATION:
- P-AI-MOMENTUM-EXIT trigger #3 confirmed fired (3-of-4 hyperscaler Q1 2026 beats with negative post-print: AMZN -3%, MSFT -4%, META -7-9%; GOOGL +7-10% lone positive)
- P-AI-MOMENTUM-EXIT trigger #2 interpreted under '2 hyperscalers showing gap ≥20pp' (autopilot recommendation) → MSFT 45pp + AMZN 71pp = satisfied
- 2-of-4 threshold MET → pre-committed AI complex trim ACTIVATED
- MAGS trim 5% selected (highest mega-cap-AI beta, not single-stock thesis dependent)
- SMH hedge upsized 5→7 contracts for elevated risk

FINAL PLAN FOR 5/15 EXECUTION (Path A):

PHASE A (5/15 open onward):
1. IVES sell-to-close 4 contracts (forced expiry action) → -$2,700 inflow
2. MAGS -318 sh trim @ ~$27 → -$8,600 inflow
3. BMNR Tranche 1 +547 sh equity @ ~$21.91 → +$12,000
4. LEU Jan 2028 $300 LEAPS 1 contract @ $55 → +$5,500
5. SMH May 22 $560/$520 put spread 7 contracts @ $0.90 → +$6,300

Net deployment: +$23,800 buys - $11,300 inflows = $12,500 net cash deployed
Funding source: MAGS trim + IVES close = $11,300; remaining $1,200 from cash buffer
Cash buffer impact: $1,075 starting - $1,200 = need additional ~$125 from somewhere

ADJUSTMENT NEEDED: Original Phase A had IVES + XLF + GRNY = $28,590. With MAGS trim swapping for XLF + GRNY, total inflow drops to $11,300, but deployment is $23,800. Shortfall ~$12,500.

RESOLUTION: Either (a) execute partial XLF trim -240 sh @ $51 = $12,290 to bridge gap, OR (b) reduce one buy. Recommend (a) — XLF trim is Newton-validated separately from MAGS trim; running both is consistent with overall defensive posture.

REVISED PHASE A FUNDING:
- IVES close: -$2,700
- MAGS -318 sh: -$8,600
- XLF -240 sh: -$12,290
- Total inflow: $23,590
- Total deployment: $23,800
- Cash buffer post: ~$1,075 starting - $210 net = ~$865 (tight but operational)

ALTERNATIVE: Increase MAGS trim to -400 sh ($10,800) and skip XLF trim. Cleaner single-source funding. Recommend this if operator prefers.

CLEANUP QUEUE EXECUTED (12 entries updated):
- CRCL 5/11 'DAT regulatory' → Cancelled (superseded by 5/14 Aug spread)
- XLF 5/2 → Cancelled (superseded by 5/8 entry)
- XLF 5/8 → Cancelled (Newton 5/11 conflicts; trim recommendation now active)
- BWXT 5/4 pre-earnings trim → Executed (insider sales already documented)
- ELBM 5/4 → Expired (11 days, no recent thesis update)
- GEV trailing stop placement 5/2 → Executed (process item completed)
- SOXX 5/8 → Cancelled (redundant with SMH 9.61% holding)
- SMH 5/8 → Cancelled (thesis tracker not buy rec; belongs in Live Theses)
- JPM 5/8 → Cancelled (Newton 5/11 financials rotation-OUT conflicts)
- SPHB 5/8 duplicate → Cancelled
- IBB 5/8 duplicate → Cancelled
- BWXT 5/8 → Executed (post-earnings sell-the-news ack'd)

UNTITLED PAGES RETITLED (2):
- 'New page' 35fc5031-4bb6-814f → 'SIL/SLV — Hedge sleeve silver allocation' + Status Executed
- 'New page' 35fc5031-4bb6-81a8 → 'Hedge sleeve gap analysis (pre-trade state)' + Status Executed

MISSING RATIONALES WRITTEN (3):
- SMH May 22 2026 $560/$520 put spread 7x (361c5031-4bb6-818e-9a7f-d7d7732815f0) — Tier A hedge
- MAGS P-AI-MOMENTUM-EXIT pre-committed trim (361c5031-4bb6-8172-a07d-e9a884e2e901) — SELL action
- HYPE Hyperliquid scale-up (361c5031-4bb6-8112-ac08-e92dc00fb5ac) — Phase B deferred

LEFT FOR OPERATOR REVIEW (5/8 batch ambiguous entries kept Active):
- GS 5/8 — operator holds; likely active
- IBB 5/8 (other entry) — status check
- IGV 5/8 — superseded if Phase B add-on confirmed; left Active for now
- INFL 5/8 — status check
- MU 5/8 — do-not-trim memory; rec direction unclear
- PBW 5/8 — status check
- SPHB 5/8 (other entry) — second duplicate left for operator decision on which to keep

FRAMEWORK STATE: CI now v11.17. v11.17 patch active — 📋 Active Recommendations Digest auto-refresh page operational.

text.content: Newton 5/14 Daily Technical: NVDA closing in on Fibonacci-based alternative projection target $242 (from April 2024 low base); DeMark 9-13-9 exhaustion may complete Monday 5/18 (first official exhaustion since March 2026 lows); ascending trendline connecting last two major swing highs at $240-245; RSI overbought; 7-week +70-point parabolic surge. NVDA earnings 5/20 + Newton-flagged 5/15-5/19 cycle inflection window align. Newton posture: hold long into early next week, watch for trend reversal starting Tuesday. CONTEXT against operator's intentional AI/semi overconcentration per P-AI-MOMENTUM: technical resistance call, NOT a P-AI-MOMENTUM-EXIT trigger. The 4 named exit triggers are (1) NVDA DIO >120 for 2 consecutive Qs, (2) hyperscaler capex/rev gap >=20pp 2x, (3) post-EPS-beat negative >=3 of 4 hyperscaler prints, (4) NVDA fwd GM cut >=150bps QoQ. None currently fired. Operator framework explicit: do NOT trim on parabolic moves / hedge-language notes. However EWY rebalance forced selling Samsung+SK Hynix by end of May is structural overhang on semi complex regardless. Operator should review trigger 1-4 status PRE-earnings, not post.

text.content: Per operator memory under 'On the horizon': BMNR T1 upgrade gate explicitly defined as 'CLARITY past committee OR flywheel re-engaged at premium-to-mNAV'. Senate Banking Committee pass on 5/14 = gate #1 explicitly fired. This is a NAMED trigger from the operator's framework, not interpretation. Source-convergence across multiple FS streams over 14d window: Lee 5/11 Macro Minute, Farrell daily notes 5/11-5/14, Block US Policy 5/11+5/14, Newton flashes 5/13-5/14. P-WAKE-UP source-convergence threshold met. Tier upgrade decision is operator-level (P-NO-FILL-NO-FACT). Action items: (a) verify mNAV status to determine if gate #2 also fired, (b) operator decides BMNR T2 5% → T1 10% baseline sizing per memory upgrade path. Per P-WAKE-UP, push hard at exceptional moments — this is one. text.content: Three concurrent macro inputs: (1) Senate confirmed Kevin Warsh as Fed Chair 54-45 on 5/13/26 by party-line vote; Powell's chairmanship ended Fri 5/15/26 today; Powell staying as Governor through 2028. Warsh chairs June FOMC. Warsh on record skeptical of Core PCE as inflation gauge. Lee notes 'first year of new Fed chair tends to be accompanied by market uncertainty and drawdown of at least 10%' (10 of last 13 chairs). (2) April Core CPI +0.38% MoM (Supercore highest since January); April PPI +1.4% MoM / +6% YoY (largest since Dec 2022); Core PPI accelerated to +4.4% YoY. (3) 30Y Treasury yield hit 5% at auction 5/13/26, first time since Aug 2007. 10Y at 4.477%, highest since July 2025. Newton sees TNX breakout above 4.60% completing multi-year symmetrical triangle. Fed funds futures imply non-zero probability of HIKES by Dec 2026. Lee 5/13: median PE of SPX lower than EOY 2019 — partial counter to bear case. Operator implications: (a) rates pressure on long-duration growth, (b) AI-momentum still working but valuation tailwind shrinking, (c) source-independent sleeve (VXUS/EFV/SGOV/BRK.B) buildout more relevant under hawkish-Fed regime, (d) Lee's mid-year drawdown 15-20% expectation reinforced. Sources: Tom Block US Policy 5/13 + Lee Macro Minutes 5/12-5/13 + Newton Daily Technical 5/12-5/14.

)
3. Detect duplicates (same ticker, multiple entries) → flag for merge or supersede
4. Detect superseded entries (older entry on same ticker contradicted by newer entry) → flag Status update
5. Identify missing rationales (recommendations made in chat but not written to DB)
6. Synthesize confidence-ranked execution queue across all Active entries
7. Update digest page '📋 Active Recommendations Digest' with refreshed snapshot
8. Surface in pre-flight: 'Active Recommendations Digest: N entries (X FRESH / Y MEDIUM / Z OLDER / W STALE). Cleanup queue: M duplicates, P superseded, Q untitled, R missing-from-DB.'

PRE-FLIGHT FORMAT v11.17 (full):
'Pre-flight: N Active rationales (M flagged). Live Theses: X positions, Y decay findings. Zone alerts: Z tickers. Promotion candidates: P tickers. 13F deltas: Q new Tier-1. Parabolic AUTOFIRE: R Phase 1/2. Cash: $A (B%). FUNDSTRAT INBOX 7-DAY AUDIT: I entries, J held-touching, K named-source. OPTIONS EXPIRY: S options <30 DTE (T <5 DTE). POSITION DRIFT: U checked, V drift-flagged. RECOMMENDATIONS DIGEST: W FRESH, X MEDIUM, Y OLDER, Z STALE; W2 duplicates, W3 superseded, W4 untitled, W5 missing.'

LOGIC FLOW PER FRESH RUN:
1. notion-search Active Trade Rationales DB filtered Status=Active
2. For each entry, parse Recommended Date → compute days_old
3. Group by freshness band (≤2 / 3-7 / 8-14 / >14)
4. Per-ticker grouping: if multiple entries on same ticker, flag for merge/supersede review
5. Cross-reference recent chat session for recommendations not yet in DB
6. Compose confidence-ranked execution queue (action / cost / confidence / timing / status)
7. Update digest page content (overwrite or append-with-timestamp)
8. Surface summary metrics in pre-flight

COST: ~10-15 seconds per Fresh Run. Adds to total pre-flight time but well within budget.

- Patch 2 (v11.16): Options expiry surface — forced-action items appear in digest execution queue
- Patch 3 (v11.16): Position drift verification — drift findings can trigger new rationales

text.content:
AUTOPILOT RESEARCH FINDING 5/14: P-AI-MOMENTUM-EXIT TRIGGER ANALYSIS

Per operator memory: 'P-AI-MOMENTUM-EXIT (5/4/26): trim only when 2-of-4 fire in 60d — (1) NVDA DIO >120 for 2 consecutive Qs, (2) hyperscaler capex/rev gap ≥20pp 2×, (3) post-EPS-beat negative ≥3 of 4 hyperscaler prints, (4) NVDA fwd GM cut ≥150bps QoQ.'

Q1 2026 HYPERSCALER PRINT REACTIONS (all reported 4/29 AH; reactions per multiple sources):
- GOOGL: BEAT (rev $109.9B; cloud $20B +63%) → +7% to +10% AH = POSITIVE
- AMZN: BEAT (EPS $2.78 vs $1.64; AWS $37.6B +28%) → -3% AH = NEGATIVE (capex concern)
- MSFT: BEAT (EPS $4.27 +$0.21; Azure +39% / +31% CC) → -4% AH = NEGATIVE (margin pressure)
- META: BEAT (EPS $7.31 vs $6.79; rev +33%; ad +33%) → -7% to -9% AH = NEGATIVE (capex raise to $125-145B)

TRIGGER #3 STATUS: FIRED. 3-of-4 hyperscaler beats had negative post-print reactions (AMZN, MSFT, META). Threshold satisfied unambiguously.

TRIGGER #2 STATUS: LIKELY FIRED pending '2x' interpretation:
- MSFT capex +84% YoY vs Azure rev +39% = gap ~45pp (above 20pp threshold)
- AMZN Q1 capex $44.2B (annualized ~$200B FY) vs Q1 revenue $155.7B annualized ~$623B → capex/rev ~32% vs prior year ~21% = gap shift; capex growth +84% YoY vs revenue growth ~13% = gap ~71pp
- META capex $19.84B Q1 (range $125-145B FY26 from prior $115-135B) on $56.31B Q1 rev → annualized capex/rev approaching 60%
- INTERPRETATION QUESTION: Does '2x' mean (a) 2 hyperscalers showing the gap in one period, or (b) 2 consecutive quarters showing the gap? If (a), trigger #2 ALREADY FIRED in Q1 2026 alone. If (b), need to verify Q4 2025 capex/rev gaps to confirm.

TRIGGER #1 STATUS: UNKNOWN. NVDA DIO data needs verification across last 2 Qs.

TRIGGER #4 STATUS: UNKNOWN UNTIL 5/20. NVDA fwd gross margin guide must be vs prior Q guide; only knowable post-print.

NET POSITION: 1 trigger CONFIRMED FIRED (#3); 1 trigger LIKELY FIRED but interpretation-dependent (#2). If both fired, P-AI-MOMENTUM-EXIT is ACTIVATED → operator pre-committed trim signal on AI complex.

NVDA OPTIONS FLOW TODAY validates the setup:

text.content: BMNR equity (Tranche 1); LEU LEAPS (new options structure on existing Generational); SMH put spread (AI hedge); BK equity deferred; CRCL options deferred

rich_text.plain_text: BMNR equity (Tranche 1); LEU LEAPS (new options structure on existing Generational); SMH put spread (AI hedge); BK equity deferred; CRCL options deferred

text.content:
OPERATOR DIRECTIVE 5/14 late PM: 'I rather make fewer high value trades.' Per memory's canonical failure mode ('under-sizing is the canonical failure mode — right but too small'), this is the correct strategic pivot. Better to concentrate conviction than spread it.

ORIGINAL 5-TRADE PLAN:
1. BMNR Tranche 1 +547 sh equity = $12K (HIGH ~80%)
2. BK +30 sh equity = $4.08K (MED-HIGH ~70%)
3. CRCL Aug $130/$170 spread x1 = $0.95K (MED-HIGH ~65%)
4. SMH put spread hedge x5 = $4.5K (HIGH ~80%)
IVES sell-to-close: -$2.7K
Total net deployment: $18.83K across 4 buys + 1 close

SIMPLIFIED 3-TRADE PLAN:
1. BMNR Tranche 1 +547 sh equity = $12K (HIGH ~80%) — unchanged
2. LEU Jan 2028 $300 LEAPS 1 contract = $5.5K (HIGH ~78%) — NEW, swap for BK+CRCL
3. SMH put spread hedge x5 = $4.5K (HIGH ~80%) — unchanged
IVES sell-to-close: -$2.7K
Total net deployment: $19.3K across 3 buys + 1 close

SWAP RATIONALE (LEU LEAPS over BK + CRCL):
(a) FACTOR DIVERSIFICATION: BK + CRCL both CLARITY-cluster bets; LEU is independent nuclear thesis. Portfolio no longer concentrates 4-of-4 forward bets on single regulatory outcome.
(b) SOURCE-STRENGTH UPGRADE: LEU = Meridian (rank #1) + Lee Granny Shots (rank #3) dual-source vs BK = single Lee, CRCL = Lee + Farrell (same firm = correlated).
(c) MEMORY ALIGNMENT: Operator memory explicitly designates LEU Jan 2028 $300 calls as 'primary options structure for LEU' — framework-mandated structure.
(d) IV CONTEXT: CRCL IV 95% EXPENSIVE; LEU IV 86% also expensive but operator memory grants exception for designated structure on 600+ DTE.
(e) ASYMMETRIC R/R: LEU LEAPS pays 1.82x at $400, 6.4x at $650; CRCL spread caps at $3K (3.19x); BK equity linear.
(f) CAPITAL EFFICIENCY: $5.5K (LEU LEAPS) similar to $5.03K (BK + CRCL combined).
(g) EXECUTION SIMPLICITY: 3 trades vs 5 = lower decision overhead.

BK + CRCL NOT KILLED: Active Trade Rationales remain Active in database; deferred to Phase B funding cycle if operator decides to add. Rationale documents include 'deferred per 5/14 simplification' context. If CLARITY floor passes ahead of schedule or operator wants additional CLARITY exposure, both rationales remain executable.

ADJACENT VALIDATION: BMNR institutional flow today (UW $184.5K put-selling at $20 strike + $44K Jan'28 LEAPS calls + $22K Jul'17 $30 calls) confirms institutional bullish positioning matching operator's Tranche 1 conviction. Direct external corroboration per Cat 7 institutional flow signal.

FRAMEWORK COMPLIANCE: All 3 trades pass v11.10 sizing-to-caps (GREEN gates), v11.12 IV overlay (LEU exception granted per memory; SMH expensive IV → vertical structure correct; BMNR equity unaffected), v11.16 patches (Inbox audit complete; options expiry surface clean; position drift surfaced).

FUNDING CONFIRMED: Phase A (IVES close + XLF -400 + GRNY -200) raises $28.59K capacity. Deploy $22K leaves $6.59K cash buffer (0.35% portfolio; still <2% triggers v11.15 Rule 2 funding-sequence-required flag but materially safer than $1.2K).

CONFIDENCE: All 3 trades HIGH (~78-80%). Average ~79% vs prior 5-trade plan average ~73% — net conviction upgrade.

text.content:
FAILURE MODE 5/14: Memory stated 'BMNR T2 at 5% baseline.' Actual operator position was 3,322 sh × $21.91 = $72,793 = 3.87% of portfolio. 23% drift from memory baseline. Initial autopilot sizing math used memory baseline for the T1 step-up target ($94K = 5% of $1.88M). This was wrong by ~$21K of capacity calculation. Operator surfaced manually; should have been auto-flagged.

RULE TEXT: On every session-open turn AND every continuation-autopilot turn, Claude MUST cross-reference position-size baselines stated in operator memory against actual Live Theses Position % values and Latest Portfolio computed %s. Any drift >10% from memory baseline triggers pre-flight surface.

IMPLEMENTATION: Pre-flight check format extends to:
'Pre-flight: N Active rationales, M flagged. Live Theses: X positions, Y decay findings. Zone alerts: Z. Promotion candidates: P. 13F deltas: Q. Parabolic AUTOFIRE: R. Cash: $A (B%). FUNDSTRAT INBOX 7-DAY AUDIT: I entries scanned, J touching held tickers, K named-source highlights. OPTIONS EXPIRY: S options <30 DTE (T action-required). POSITION DRIFT: U positions checked, V drift-flagged.'

DRIFT-FLAGGED FORMAT:
'TICKER memory baseline N% / actual M% / drift -X% → [UNDERSIZED at conviction / OVERSIZED concentration risk]'

DIRECTION SEMANTICS:
- Drift NEGATIVE (actual < memory) = UNDERSIZED → surface as potential canonical-failure-mode firing (P-UNDERSIZE)
- Drift POSITIVE (actual > memory) = OVERSIZED → surface as concentration check (may be intentional drift; verify intent)
- Drift >25% in either direction = additional flag for operator review

LOGIC FLOW:
1. Parse memory text for explicit position-size claims: 'TICKER at N%', 'TICKER T1/T2/T3', 'baseline X%'
2. Cross-reference against Live Theses Position % field
3. Cross-reference against Latest Portfolio computed % (position $ / total wealth $)
4. Compute drift: (actual - memory_baseline) / memory_baseline
5. Output drift table for positions where |drift| >10%

MEMORY-BASELINE EXTRACTION RULES: Only extract memory claims with explicit numeric/tier baselines. Don't infer baselines from vague language. If memory says 'BMNR T2 5% baseline,' that's extractable; if memory says 'BMNR active position,' not extractable.

COST: ~3-5 seconds per turn for memory-text scan + Live Theses lookup + Latest Portfolio cross-check. Minimal overhead.

COMPOSITION: Patch 3 cross-validates the OPERATOR INTENT BASELINE (what was decided) against the EXECUTED REALITY (what's in the portfolio). Catches:
- Under-deployment vs decision (P-UNDERSIZE failure mode)
- Position drift from price action without re-balancing
- Memory-state staleness where operator already adjusted but framework didn't catch up

BACKTEST: Would have caught BMNR today. Would also catch LEU drift if existing position vs Generational Lane spec drifts >10%; would catch IVES if equity grew above target via price appreciation; would catch any held position where intended sizing and executed sizing diverged.

CI bump v11.16-rev2 → v11.16-rev3 (in-place patch addition, no version-rebase).

ATM (delta 0.35-0.65) + <5 DTE = Close or Roll (gamma risk high)
  * OTM (delta <0.20) + <5 DTE = Let expire OR close for tax loss

text.content:
8 days of Fundstrat Inbox content audited per v11.16 Patch 1. Findings:

1. CRCL AGENTIC FINANCE THESIS DEEPENED (Farrell 5/11 22:10 ET): CRCL +17.63% on the day. Circle launched agentic infrastructure stack — agent wallets, micropayments, marketplaces, tooling around x402 standard. Launched ARC token tied to ARC blockchain; Circle retains 25% of supply = significant balance sheet optionality. Farrell explicitly re-added to Fundstrat crypto equities portfolio 5/1. Risk flag: Circle and Coinbase 'increasingly appear competitive' — strategic overlap rising. Implication: CRCL Live Theses updated with deeper agentic-finance thesis; CRCL Aug 130/170 spread rationale strengthened; risk of Coinbase competitive overlap noted.

2. STRC EX-DIV 5/15 WINDOW = BTC FLOW SIGNAL (Farrell 5/11 + 5/12): STRC volume reaccelerating — 'highest T-4 volume observed so far (~$431M)' on 5/11 = strongest pre-ex-div flow in months. Suggests BTC-related inflows continuing into ex-dividend window tomorrow 5/15. STRC has historically driven incremental BTC/MSTR buying via yield-seeker mechanics. Implication: BMNR Tranche 1 execution 5/15-5/16 hits during favorable STRC ex-div flow window; do NOT delay Tranche 1 if STRC volume holds through tomorrow.

3. EWY FORCED REWEIGHTING = US SEMIS RISK (Newton 5/12): EWY (iShares MSCI South Korea ETF) end-of-month rebalance forces selling in Samsung + SK Hynix. Newton: 'might ripple through US Semiconductors between now and early June.' Implication: 41.9% AI complex exposure faces month-end Korean semis selling pressure as additional mechanical risk on top of NVDA earnings catalyst. STRONG additional validation for Path 2 SMH put spread hedge.

4. NEWTON ROTATION THEME 5/15-5/19 (5/11 19:55): 'NYA Cycle Argues Cap-Weighted Stallout Approaching.' Rotation OUT of: Consumer Discretionary, Consumer Staples, Healthcare, FINANCIALS. Rotation INTO: international equities (CQQQ/FXI), Silver, China, commodities. Newton explicitly named XLF/financials as rotation-OUT target. Implication: XLF trim recommendation validated by Newton named-source. GDX/AEM hold validated by Newton silver-precious-metals named breakout. CQQQ remains operator-SKIP per cash constraint but Newton consistent.

5. ZEC COMPELLING BUY-ON-DIPS (Farrell 5/8 20:55): ZEC +5.95% on the day. Farrell named privacy-coin thesis. Operator does NOT currently hold ZEC. New opportunity candidate — added to Research Queue. Not Fundstrat-Top-5-named; rank #6 inbox digest level. Defer to Phase B funding cycle.

6. IGV SOFTWARE BREAKOUT CONFIRMED (Newton 5/7 20:10): IGV +3.39% on Newton's named breakout. Operator currently holds IGV 4.6% portfolio ($86,468 per Latest Portfolio). Newton named-source = thesis CONFIRMED on existing holding. Add-on candidate when cash freed; not urgent given existing position is healthy size. Surface as positive holding-tracker, not new recommendation.

7. LEE MACRO MINUTE 5/13 23

title.plain_text: 7-day Fundstrat Inbox sweep findings (5/7-5/14): 8 high-priority signals missed across 3 prior autopilot turns — synthesized for position management

text.content: All (framework-level)

rich_text.plain_text: All (framework-level)

text.content:
OPERATOR DIRECTLY ASKED: 'Do I have to manually tell you to check the Fundstrat Direct?' — the answer should be NO. v11.13 P-READ-OR-PUNT extension WAS supposed to address this for binary-catalyst questions but did not fire correctly across continuation turns. v11.16 Patch 1 closes the gap completely.

RULE TEXT: On every session-open turn AND every operator-prompted 'continue / autopilot / keep going' turn, Claude MUST execute Fundstrat Inbox 7-day audit BEFORE any analytical output or new research. The audit reads ALL Inbox entries with timestamps within last 7 calendar days. The audit MUST extract subject lines, identify entries touching held tickers (cross-reference fs_holdings.json) and entries from named-source authors (Lee / Newton / Farrell / Block / Singh), and surface any missed signals in pre-flight section of response.

IMPLEMENTATION: Pre-flight check format extends to: 'Pre-flight: N Active rationales, M flagged. Live Theses: X positions, Y decay findings. Zone alerts: Z. Promotion candidates: P. 13F deltas: Q. Parabolic AUTOFIRE: R. Cash: $A (B%). FUNDSTRAT INBOX 7-DAY AUDIT: I entries scanned, J touching held tickers, K named-source highlights, L missed-since-prior-turn'. Where L counts entries posted since last continued-autopilot turn (timestamp delta).

INBOX ENTRIES AUTO-SURFACE WHEN: (1) authored by Lee, Newton, Farrell, Block, Singh, or any other named Fundstrat analyst; (2) subject line contains held ticker per fs_holdings.json; (3) subject line contains 'Sector Allocation', 'Top 5', 'Bottom 5', 'Signal From Noise', 'Crypto Comments', 'Daily Technical Strategy', or 'Weekly Roadmap'; (4) any explicit BUY/SELL/ADD/TRIM language on named ticker; (5) any binary catalyst date mentioned within 60 days.

COST: ~3-5 seconds per turn for 7-day Inbox scan + subject filtering. Trivial relative to value of catching missed signals (today's audit found CRCL agentic finance update, STRC ex-div flow window, EWY semis reweight risk, Newton rotation theme, ZEC opportunity, IGV breakout confirmation — all missed across 3 prior autopilot turns despite being directly relevant to operator positions).

FAILURE MODE DOCUMENTED: 5/14 session evolution — v11.13 fired on initial CLARITY question but did NOT re-fire on subsequent 'Continue on autopilot' messages, treating the in-session state as 'still current' when in fact 8 days of Inbox content had not been audited. v11.16 fires on EVERY continuation, regardless of session state staleness assumption.

BACKLOG: v11.16 Patch 2 (Options-expiry pre-flight surface) and v11.16 Patch 3 (Position-size verification vs memory baseline) remain held pending operator design input. v11.16 Patch 1 ships immediately as critical-gap closure.

CI bump: v11.15 → v11.16 (Patch 1 only).

text.content:
Late-day 5/14 5:40 PM ET drop in Fundstrat Inbox includes 3 notes directly relevant to active positions and today's framework decisions.

HYPE MATERIAL THESIS UPGRADE (Sean Farrell 5/14 11:44 AM ET): Hyperliquid AQAv2 partnership with Coinbase and Circle announced. Under the new structure Coinbase becomes treasury deployer for USDC, shares 'vast majority' of reserve yield revenue with Hyperliquid protocol. Effect: Hyperliquid evolves from trading venue to protocol with meaningful participation in stablecoin reserve economics. Farrell's run-rate earnings estimate: $70-150M annually for tokenholders. Move also consolidates ecosystem around USDC as canonical quote asset, reducing liquidity fragmentation. Operator currently holds HYPE $752 (0.04%, classified as Watchlist-passive per zombie rule). Open question Farrell flagged: whether yield shared is gross T-bill reserve yield or net pass-through. ACTION: HYPE position thesis materially upgraded. Promote from Watchlist-passive to active Tier C tracking. Add Live Theses entry. Cash-constrained, so add-on deferred to Phase B funding cycle if operator wants larger position. Per v11.11 promotion rule: HYPE now fires (a) named-source Fundstrat 5/14 + (b) regulatory de-risk indirect via CLARITY beneficiary stablecoin clarity = 2 of 4 criteria. AUTO-PROMOTE to Tier B Two-Lens eligible per v11.11.

CLARITY FLOOR-FIGHT REAL (both Farrell + L. Thomas Block 2:05 PM ET): 3 Dems (Gallego, Alsobrooks, third unnamed) supported committee but reserved right to vote against on floor. Bill needs 60-vote bipartisan vote on Senate floor as Dems threatened filibuster. Committee bill must be merged with Senate Agriculture Committee crypto bill before floor vote. Leadership targets July 4 break. Polymarket odds rebounded to 70%. VALIDATION: Today's BMNR partial-step-up (5% to 7%, NOT full T1 to 10%) decision was correct given floor-fight uncertainty. Reaffirms staging Tranches 2-3 at lower price levels rather than full deployment immediately. Reaffirms CRCL spread vs outright calls structure. Reaffirms CEPT starter size vs full position.

FIGR + HOOD FLAGGED AS DIRECT BENEFICIARIES (Farrell 1:36 PM ET): Crypto financial services equities responding most favorably post-vote: COIN, HOOD, FIGR. COIN already in plan. HOOD not held (research-queue add). FIGR ticker not in operator's universe per current sleeves — research-queue check for company identification and sleeve fit. ACTION: Add FIGR + HOOD as Research Queue items (defer to next session due to cash constraint).
