text.content: ). If ticker appears on either Bottom 5 list → KILL recommendation regardless of Wall Street consensus or other forward signals. GLXY caught today: project knowledge fetch revealed GLXY on Fundstrat Bottom 5 SMID-cap (4/22/26 add date, price $26.80; current $32.20 = badly underperforming Fundstrat bear thesis); my analyst-rating data showed all-BUY consensus (Canaccord $50, Rosenblatt $39, Piper $36, Chardan $35); without this cross-check rule, I would have recommended GLXY as HYPE-replacement equity. Lee/Newton Bottom 5 IS a named-source AVOID list per source-strength ranking (parallel to Top 5 = source-strength rank #2). Implementation: Launcher Step 1 (Fundstrat ranker sweep) extends to cross-check candidate tickers against active Bottom 5 lists from latest monthly allocation .md before generating any recommendation surface. Cost: 1 extra .md scan per session start, near-zero. Backtest: would have caught GLXY today; would also catch Carrier Global (CARR), elf Beauty (ELF), Array Tech (ARRY), Kratos (KTOS) currently on the Bottom 5 SMID-cap list. RULE 2 CASH-CONSTRAINT PRE-FLIGHT: At session open, surface cash as % of portfolio in the pre-flight check output. If cash <2% of total wealth, flag every new-position recommendation with a funding-sequence required disclosure. If cash <5%, flag in the recommendation output. Today: built a $10K execution plan without checking cash; portfolio fetch later revealed cash at $1,075 (0.06% of $1.88M portfolio) = recommendation plan was unexecutable as proposed. Implementation extension: pre-flight format extends to: Pre-flight: N Active rationales, M flagged. Live Theses: X positions, Y decay findings. Zone alerts: Z tickers. Promotion candidates: P. 13F deltas: Q. Parabolic AUTOFIRE: R. Cash: $A (B%) [🚨 FUNDING-SEQUENCE-REQUIRED if <2% / ⚠️ LOW if <5%]. New rationales auto-include funding-sequence-required tag if cash <2% at write time. Cost: 1 number lookup per session start, zero. CI bump v11.14 → v11.15. Both rules close systemic blind spots, not edge cases. Held v11.15 Rules 3+4 (position-size verification + options-expiry pre-flight) pending operator design input.

rich_text.plain_text: ). If ticker appears on either Bottom 5 list → KILL recommendation regardless of Wall Street consensus or other forward signals. GLXY caught today: project knowledge fetch revealed GLXY on Fundstrat Bottom 5 SMID-cap (4/22/26 add date, price $26.80; current $32.20 = badly underperforming Fundstrat bear thesis); my analyst-rating data showed all-BUY consensus (Canaccord $50, Rosenblatt $39, Piper $36, Chardan $35); without this cross-check rule, I would have recommended GLXY as HYPE-replacement equity. Lee/Newton Bottom 5 IS a named-source AVOID list per source-strength ranking (parallel to Top 5 = source-strength rank #2). Implementation: Launcher Step 1 (Fundstrat ranker sweep) extends to cross-check candidate tickers against active Bottom 5 lists from latest monthly allocation .md before generating any recommendation surface. Cost: 1 extra .md scan per session start, near-zero. Backtest: would have caught GLXY today; would also catch Carrier Global (CARR), elf Beauty (ELF), Array Tech (ARRY), Kratos (KTOS) currently on the Bottom 5 SMID-cap list. RULE 2 CASH-CONSTRAINT PRE-FLIGHT: At session open, surface cash as % of portfolio in the pre-flight check output. If cash <2% of total wealth, flag every new-position recommendation with a funding-sequence required disclosure. If cash <5%, flag in the recommendation output. Today: built a $10K execution plan without checking cash; portfolio fetch later revealed cash at $1,075 (0.06% of $1.88M portfolio) = recommendation plan was unexecutable as proposed. Implementation extension: pre-flight format extends to: Pre-flight: N Active rationales, M flagged. Live Theses: X positions, Y decay findings. Zone alerts: Z tickers. Promotion candidates: P. 13F deltas: Q. Parabolic AUTOFIRE: R. Cash: $A (B%) [🚨 FUNDING-SEQUENCE-REQUIRED if <2% / ⚠️ LOW if <5%]. New rationales auto-include funding-sequence-required tag if cash <2% at write time. Cost: 1 number lookup per session start, zero. CI bump v11.14 → v11.15. Both rules close systemic blind spots, not edge cases. Held v11.15 Rules 3+4 (position-size verification + options-expiry pre-flight) pending operator design input.

text.content:
(in-place replacement of v1.0):

(1) F2 EPS data quality — score_eps_surprise_streak() now returns 3-tuple (score, note, data_quality) where data_quality ∈ {FULL, PARTIAL, MISSING}. No silent zero-fallback on missing estimates. ScreenResult gained data_quality_flags dict. New --show-data-quality CLI flag. Live-validated against LEU's real earnings history: FULL/PARTIAL/MISSING branches all classify correctly.

(2) F5 low-variance metric — score_price_base() rewritten using coefficient of variation (stdev/median) + absolute-low check. CV<0.20 AND median<$25 → 1.0 (VIAV pattern). CV<0.25 AND median<$50 → 0.7. Else degrades. Fixes the v1.0 miss on flat-low bases like VIAV.

(3) Phase reset detector — classify_phase() now checks post-earnings reaction via _compute_earnings_reaction() helper. If base_phase=Phase 3 AND most recent earnings reaction ≥50% in 3 days → reclassifies as 'Phase 1.5 (reset cycle, +N% post-earnings)'. Catches AEHR-style restart cycles.

=== W8 Stale-LEAPS Scan v1.0 → v1.0.1 ===

New file /mnt/user-data/outputs/stale_leaps_

text.content: RULE 1 POSITION-HELD AUTO-ROUTE: When evaluating any named ticker, FIRST step = check Latest Portfolio (Notion 35ac5031-4bb6-81fc-b792-e50bf86d63f4) or operator existing holdings before research workflow. If HELD: route to Add-on/Two-Lens for incremental size + reference existing thesis. If NOT HELD: route to full new-position P-REASONING-ARCH. Closes gap from today where add-on candidate decision was implicit, not structural. RULE 2 TRUMP-ADMIN-TIE TWO-LENS CATEGORY 8: User preferences explicitly require rationale to buy or not (including Trump administration corruption ties) as standing consideration. Promote from ad-hoc to structural Two-Lens Cat 8: (a) direct ties (cabinet, family business, donor network) score +1 for upside catalyst access AND +1 for political/investigation risk, net effect context-dependent; (b) indirect ties (banker/SPAC sponsor adjacency, e.g. Cantor Fitzgerald/Lutnick) flag with same dual-edge framing; (c) check Senate investigation queue (Warren/Wyden/Van Hollen letters), DOJ inquiries, ethics-rule challenges. Cat 8 firing does NOT auto-veto or auto-promote; ensures dimension is in the tally and surfaced explicitly. Closes gap from today CEPT analysis where Cantor/Lutnick tie was caught manually. RULE 3 NAMED-TRIGGER POSITION-MANAGEMENT AUTO-ROUTE: When a memory-encoded position trigger fires (e.g. CLARITY past committee = BMNR T1 upgrade trigger), auto-route to SEPARATE position-management stream from the catalyst research, BEFORE completing the catalyst research synthesis. Today session caught BMNR trigger inline near end; should have been flagged structurally at top as parallel work-stream. Implementation: maintain explicit trigger registry (CLARITY committee passage → BMNR T1 evaluation; mNAV>1.0 for 3 sessions → full T1 upgrade; STRC volume drop >50% MoM → MSTR thesis check). Surface as Separate Stream callout at top of response. CI bump v11.13 → v11.14.

text.content: GAP DIAGNOSED 5/14/26: Operator asked which equities benefit from CLARITY Act passage. Claude failed to fetch Fundstrat Inbox (CI rule violated), failed to web-search current CLARITY status (committee passage was THAT DAY), failed to run conversation_search despite operator referencing prior chats + screenshots, and treated stale userMemories CLARITY status statement as canonical instead of baseline-to-verify. Material misses: (1) Fundstrat-named beneficiaries COIN/HOOD/CEPT/CRCL from Sean Farrell 5/13 flash; (2) CRCL +17.63% on 5/11 agentic finance event = v11.8 thesis-strengthening event; (3) Tom Lee 5/11 explicit Macro Minute on CLARITY-ETH thesis; (4) BMNR T1 upgrade trigger fired today per memory rule. FIX: P-READ-OR-PUNT extended with NEW TRIGGER: Named ongoing catalyst is question premise (regulatory bill, court case, scheduled vote, FDA decision, election outcome, named binary). When question premise = current status of named event, MUST verify before substantive analysis: (a) web_search event name + current month/year status; (b) Fundstrat Inbox fetch if event keyword is in fs_holdings.json thesis_keywords or macro_themes; (c) conversation_search if operator references prior chats/screenshots/as-we-discussed. STALE-STATUS DETECTOR: when userMemories or project knowledge contains a status statement about ongoing event (X passed / Y pending / Z scheduled), treat as starting point, NOT canonical. Corollary to P-NO-FILL-NO-FACT: never claim event state from memory. EFFICIENCY SAFEGUARDS: does NOT fire for (a) historical/settled events, (b) catalyst mentioned but not question premise, (c) follow-ups within session where verify already ran. Cost when fires: 2-3 tool calls (~10-15 sec). Operator chose hold patch / apply mentally for the originating session but explicitly directed autopilot mode upgrade afterward. Patch shipped to Decisions Log per Notion write discipline (framework change threshold met). CI bump to v11.13 from v11.12.

text.content: AI Complex / Speculative Options

rich_text.plain_text: AI Complex / Speculative Options

text.content: Closed 4× IVES May 15 2026 $30 Call (IVES260515C00030000) at $6.60 mid-point limit for $2,640 gross in Parents Fidelity Rollover IRA. Position context: calls entered OTM as leveraged AI-complex bet, rallied deep ITM as IVES went from ~$32 entry zone to $36.99 today. Decision basis: (1) AI complex concentration already 41.9% portfolio-wide — no source-named signal (Lee/Newton/Ives) supporting MORE IVES exposure specifically. (2) Duplicative exposure to existing IVES (1,855sh equity), SMH, MAGS, GRNY, GRNJ, IGV (just added 188sh 5/7-5/14). (3) Cash conservation — portfolio cash was $1,075 total; exercise would require $12,000 cash outlay. (4) Same-IRA capital redeployment opportunity — freed $2,640 in Parents Fid Rollover where LEU Jan-2028 $400/$600 vertical entry ($1,793 debit) also lives. Net intra-account: -$2,640 IVES → +$1,793 LEU → ~$847 cash freed, zero tax friction. (5) Theta at 1 DTE essentially zero, no premium left to harvest. LEARNING (material, durable): Theo $6.98 vs intrinsic $6.99 vs actual market mid $6.60 = $0.39/share discount below intrinsic. UW theo field is a model output; midpoint is the order book. For deep-ITM (delta >0.95) options at ≤2 DTE with same-day volume = 0, default limit anchor is MIDPOINT not theo. Initial recommendation of $6.95 limit was too aggressive — would have sat unfilled or chased to mid eventually. New rule logged to options_structure_decision_

Named source pick fires INDEPENDENT of Fundstrat: JPMorgan $460 + 'Top Overall Pick 2026' (4/30), Goldman $450 (4/30), Mizuho $460 (5/6), Citizens $515, BMO $410, Scotiabank $450. Source-cluster discount does NOT apply across Fundstrat-vs-sell-side boundary — independent named sources. Institutional flow = 1: Berkshire new $4.3B stake Q3 2025, TCI raised 3%→5% Q1 2026 AND cut MSFT, Whale Rock made GOOGL largest holding +930K shares. Three tier-1 funds with conviction-sized AI-leadership rotation. Macro/technical = 1 (record close $402.62 on 5/13, inside Newton's $377-402 Fib zone). Momentum = 1 (YTD ~+27% vs SPY +9.16%, MAGS +6.93%, XLC +1.27%). Disqualifiers: (1) Q2 print 7/22 = 69 days out (just outside 60d gate); (2) no qualifying open-market discretionary C-suite buy; (3) at top of Newton's Fib target — decision zone, not blind-chase zone; (4) DOJ remedies appeal live (gov+states appealed 2026-02 seeking stronger remedies, Chrome divestiture path still on table); (5) capex/revenue gap WORSE than AMZN — 22.7% → 38% (15-point step-up vs AMZN's 7-point). Q1 GAAP EPS flattered by $36.9B equity-securities gain (+$2.35 EPS) — forward P/E 28.1x is the honest read (vs S&P 21x, sector 20.5x, GOOGL own 10y avg 22x). Cloud 63% Q1 = fastest hyperscaler. Trump nexus = lobbying/access not corruption-protection. RE-EVALUATION TRIGGERS: (A) confirmation breakout >$402.50 (close+hold) → Jan 2027 $420/$500 call spread, $1500-2000 (AMBER discount for MAGS overlap), (B) pullback to $377-385 → Jan 2027 $380/$460 call spread same sizing, (C) Google I/O 5/19-20 monetization signals, (D) Q2 print 7/22 — re-run at T-7 = 7/15. PRIORITY: GOOGL > AMZN for next entry attempt. Reminders + calendar entries created 5/14. Optional next step: run Gemini Adversarial (Prompt 2) for DOJ appellate + AI-eats-Search stress test before triggers fire.

text.content: A.1 prompt). Verdict: NOT-NOW-WITH-TRIGGER. Score 2/7 post-discount. Positive findings ChatGPT surfaced independently: strong sell-side cluster (56 buys/3 holds/0 sells, $313 consensus PT, post-Q1 PT raises BNP Exane $345, Goldman $325, New Street $350, Benchmark $370). Capex/revenue gap = 26.9% vs 19.9% prior year (+700bps), AWS AI revenue run-rate only >$15B annualized vs $57.6B incremental spend. AWS grew 28% Q1 = fastest in 15Q but Azure 39% / GCP 63% — largest hyperscaler not winning relative race. Operator-added pharma thesis (AMZN Pharmacy + One Medical + GLP-1 distribution): noted as operator-validated thesis ADDITION but lacks external corroboration (no top-tier analyst initiation specifically on pharma thesis, Pharmacy <1% of revenue) — does NOT elevate to Tier A. Kelly sizing math on +5.75% EV with 23% bear-case loss = $300-600 at quarter-Kelly, well under symbolic Tier B $1K floor. Portfolio coherence AMBER (MAGS overlap, AI complex concentration). Reminders + calendar entries created 5/14.

text.content: Audited bearish analyst flow on LEU (Zacks Strong Sell 4/17, Citi PT $224→$218 5/8, UBS PT $245→$195 3/5) to determine whether bears have info edge or are stale. Findings: (1) Zacks Rank is mechanical algo driven by EPS estimate-revision direction, NOT a thesis call. Algo flagged falling Q1 consensus ($0.54→$0.41 over 60d), then LEU reported $1.05 adj EPS = 156% beat. Algo lagged reality. (2) Citi anchored on Q4 miss. Big cut was Feb 18 ($292→$225); May 8 trim ($224→$218) is cosmetic. PT $218 still implies +13% upside from $192 current — not actually bearish, just Neutral with mild caution. Bagri staying ~$220 range pre+post-Q1. (3) UBS is the only substantive bear thesis: BWXT enrichment competition. But BWXT operates conversion (not enrichment) commercially today; their enrichment plans target naval/tritium, not commercial HALEU; realistic timeline for BWXT commercial HALEU competition is 2028-2030+. Timing-mismatched to Jan-2028 trade window which targets 2026-2027 NOFO/offtake/Piketon catalysts. (4) Bull counter-flow post-Q1 is the real signal: Roth Capital raised PT $137→$230 (+68%) on 5/7 with 'HALEU-Fueled Growth' framing; Northland maintained Outperform calling Feb pullback a 'buying opportunity'; B. Riley maintained Buy at $295 trimming only on expansion costs; consensus PT $271.83 implies +42% upside. (5) Tinelli CFO $62K sale 5/11 = noise; below $500K signal threshold. STRUCTURE DECISION: Jan 21 2028 expiry wins on IV term structure (70.87% vs Jan-27 80.20%, 9 vol pts cheaper); Dec-2027 is functional equivalent. Jan-2027 is trap (peak vega + Rule 3 review fires July 2026). Primary entry: 2× Jan-2028 $400C/$600C bull call vertical, debit $1,793/contract = $3,586 total, R/R 10.2:1, position_sizer GREEN at 12% sleeve heat under Tier A 15% cap. Lotto ($400/$640 vertical) deferred pending dip to $180-185. Scale-in plan: 1c today, 1c on dip or pre-Aug-4 earnings. BWXT-licensing exit trigger added to Exit Triggers DB. Live Theses LEU row updated to Tier A, Anchor Source Multiple (Meridian + Lee + Q1), Signal Count 5/7.

(3) Strategy slide: WHAT TO OWN that benefits from stronger 2026 = MAG7 ($MAGS), Ethereum ($IBIT $ETHA), Software ($IGV) explicit BOTTOM IN call (YTD relative to SPX: -1.8% / -24.9% / -32.9%). Plus XLI industrials, XLF + KRE financials, IWM small-caps, XLE/XLB energy/materials.nnIntegration: (a) Held names MAGS/IBIT/ETHA/IGV/XLF — reaffirmed, no sizing action, no signal-count bump (source-cluster discount: this is same Lee source as existing GRNY/GRNJ/inbox signals on these names). (b) Net-new candidate: KRE — added to Research Queue. (c) Other unheld picks (IWM, XLI, XLE, XLB) — deferred to next-chat full refresh after broker PDF upload. (d) Macro PE thesis is supportive context for held AI/ETH/software complex on any drawdown.nnSource tag: Fundstrat Tom Lee 5/13 Macro Minute (high-conviction, named tickers, dated). Not a hedge call.

text.content: P-ASYMMETRIC framework Cat 7 (Institutional flow verification); UW-as-judgment Tier 1 auto-pull spec; P-AI-MOMENTUM-EXIT trigger set

rich_text.plain_text: P-ASYMMETRIC framework Cat 7 (Institutional flow verification); UW-as-judgment Tier 1 auto-pull spec; P-AI-MOMENTUM-EXIT trigger set

text.content:
Ran UW post-mortem on six 500%+ 12-month winners that fit operator themes: BE (+1,413%), CRML (+650%), LITE (+1,283%), STX (+675%), WDC (+908%), APLD (+711%). Tested whether UW endpoints would have predicted the moves retrospectively.

Cross-cutting findings:

(1) UW options + dark pool data are NOT historically queryable at Basic tier — 30-trading-day window only. These are forward-looking real-time signals, not post-mortem tools. Framework must accept this constraint: options/DP signals must be monitored continuously on watchlist, not retrospectively pulled on winners.

(2) Insider open-market buys (P-code, 10b5-1 excluded) were ZERO across all six winners. Insider buying is rare but high-quality when present. Existing rule validated: 'C-suite open-market buys >$500K = bullish; absence is growth-co default, not bearish.' AI-infrastructure names have insiders sitting on legacy grants who sell into strength — net-seller pattern does NOT predict tops.

(3) Congressional trades: weak signal absent CLUSTER. BE had Cisneros accumulating Sep-Nov 2025 single-politician (got it right); LITE/CRML/APLD had nothing or chase-late; STX/WDC mixed. Proposed rule: single-politician buys are informational; 3+ politicians same direction in 30 days = actionable.

(4) 13F institutional flow is the HIGHEST-quality retrospectively-verifiable signal at Basic tier. Pattern that worked: hedge-fund-tagged initiations (tags: ["hedge_fund"]) with first_buy in last 2 quarters, position >100K shares OR >0.1% of OS. Filing lag is ~45 days but signal is real-time-detectable on each filing cycle. Specific wins documented: Graticule Asia Macro on BE Q3 25 at $48.93 avg; WT Asset Management on LITE Q2 25 at ~$80; WCM Investment Management on STX Q3 25 at $171 AND on WDC Q3 25 at $84 (cohort initiation by same fund across thematic peers).

(5) Meridian-style micro-cap names (CRML): UW endpoints essentially predict nothing. Zero congressional, zero insider, no active hedge-fund accumulation visible. Meridian research IS the alpha source; UW signals are confirmation-only or absent for this name class. Framework should acknowledge this explicitly.

(6) Index addition events generate false-positive 13F signals: Vanguard family Q1 2026 added 3 entities with identical position size + identical avg price + identical first_buy quarter across BE, BMNR, LITE, STX, WDC, MU — mechanical index rebalance, not conviction.

Proposed framework integrations against current CI v11.7 (5 additions):

(A) Tier 1 13F filter spec — refine existing 'institutional 13F' auto-pull bullet to specify: filter hedge_fund tagged + first_buy in last 2 quarters + size threshold (>100K shares OR >0.1% of OS) + surface as cohort-initiation when same fund hits 2+ thematic peers same quarter.

(B) Quality active manager lens — Baillie Gifford-class growth investors not hedge_fund tagged but signal-bearing. Maintain manual list (Baillie Gifford, Capital World, Capital Research/International, Primecap, T. Rowe Price growth funds).

(C) Strategic anchor lens — tags: ['public_companies'] initiations from major operating companies (Alphabet on ASTS) or strategic equity stakes (Rakuten on ASTS, SBI Holdings on CRCL). Partially absorbed by v11.7 Patch 4 (hyperscaler-contract-anchor F0 override) but the UW data path differs.

(D) Cohort initiation detector — when same fund initiates 2+ thematically-linked names in same quarter (WCM on STX+WDC), surface as higher-confidence cohort signal. New rule.

(E) Institutional distribution warning on held names — when 2+ top-tier active managers trim >30% from peak position over 2 consecutive quarters on a held AI-momentum name (MU pattern: Capital World 70M→42M and PRIMECAP 37M→22M Q3 25-Q1 26), surface as P-AI-MOMENTUM-EXIT 5th trigger candidate. NOT auto-exit, but informational warning that supplements the existing 4 triggers.

(F) Index addition negative filter — when Vanguard family or BlackRock subsidiaries show 3+ entities with identical position size + price + first_buy quarter, label as index event, deprioritize as conviction signal.

These additions address a failure mode the v11.7 patches do NOT cover: names that fit operator themes but lack Lee/Newton/Meridian endorsement (the six post-mortem winners had no Lee ETF inclusion, no Fundstrat naming, no hyperscaler contract anchors). The signal that DID exist was institutional flow. v11.7 patches address NBIS pattern (named-source SMID); these additions address theme-fit but source-unnamed winners.

text.content: UW Post-Mortem 5/13/26: Hedge-Fund 13F Initiations = Highest-Quality Retrospective Signal at UW Basic Tier

title.plain_text: UW Post-Mortem 5/13/26: Hedge-Fund 13F Initiations = Highest-Quality Retrospective Signal at UW Basic Tier

text.content: BMNR (T2 baseline 5% per 5/3/26 Generational Lane reclassification, T1 upgrade gate pending CLARITY past committee or flywheel re-engagement at premium-to-mNAV)

rich_text.plain_text: BMNR (T2 baseline 5% per 5/3/26 Generational Lane reclassification, T1 upgrade gate pending CLARITY past committee or flywheel re-engagement at premium-to-mNAV)

text.content:
Ran UW get_institutions on BMNR as part of live test of hedge-fund-13F-initiation scanner. Findings strongly validate the 5/3/26 Generational Lane reclassification:

Hedge-fund-tagged initiations in last 2 quarters:
— ARK Investment Management (Cathie Wood): first_buy Q3 2025, scaled 7.47M → 9.46M → 9.70M shares at $48.09 avg
— AMOVA Asset Management Americas (Nikko Asset Management, Japanese): first_buy Q3 2025, 4.91M shares at $48.52 avg (holding steady)

Major non-hedge-fund institutional initiations in last 2 quarters:
— BlackRock first_buy Q3 25, scaled 3.4M → 9.05M → 11.08M shares at $40.63 avg (notable since this is active accumulation by BLK on top of passive baseline)
— Sumitomo Mitsui Trust Group (Japanese): NEW Q4 2025 with 4.91M shares at $40.58 avg
— California Public Employees Retirement System (CalPERS): NEW Q1 2026 with 711K shares at $23.53 avg — real US pension money entered
— Jane Street Group: NEW Q3 25, scaled to 3.88M shares
— Mozayyx Management: NEW Q4 2025 with 3.33M shares at $40.58 avg
— Bank of Montreal: NEW Q3 25, scaling
— IMC-Chicago (prop): NEW Q4 25, scaling
— LPL Financial: scaling
— Vontobel Holding (Swiss): NEW Q4 25, scaled 35x in one quarter
— BIT Capital GmbH (German): first_buy Q3 25
— Exchange Traded Concepts: scaled 25K → 861K Q4 25

Pattern: 12+ distinct institutional entities initiated or substantially scaled BMNR positions Q3 2025 - Q1 2026, including two hedge-fund-tagged names (ARK, AMOVA), one major US pension (CalPERS), one Japanese trust bank (Sumitomo Mitsui), and the largest passive holder (BlackRock) actively accumulating beyond passive baseline.

This is precisely the cohort/multi-source institutional adoption pattern that the post-mortem analysis identified as the highest-quality retrospective signal. The pattern is independently validating — multiple unrelated institutional types entering same name same window without coordinating.

Comparison vs MU (also in AI-momentum complex): MU showed OPPOSITE pattern same window — Capital World 70M → 42M (-40%), PRIMECAP 37M → 22M (-40%), zero hedge-fund initiations Q3 25 - Q1 26. Active managers distributing into strength. BMNR institutional setup is materially earlier-cycle than MU's.

Framework implication: validates T2 baseline 5% sizing and supports T1 upgrade gate consideration when (a) CLARITY past committee, OR (b) flywheel re-engages at premium-to-mNAV. The institutional adoption pattern is now layered on top of: (a) Tom Lee operator-validated ETH/stablecoin/RWA thesis, (b) ETH staking yield mechanics (~72% of ~3.5M ETH staked), (c) flywheel mechanics (accretive issuance at premium-to-mNAV).

No immediate capital action required. Position stance reaffirmed.

Pattern: 12+ distinct institutional entities initiated or substantially scaled BMNR positions Q3 2025 - Q1 2026, including two hedge-fund-tagged names (ARK, AMOVA), one major US pension (CalPERS), one Japanese trust bank (Sumitomo Mitsui), and the largest passive holder (BlackRock) actively accumulating beyond passive baseline.

This is precisely the cohort/multi-source institutional adoption pattern that the post-mortem analysis identified as the highest-quality retrospective signal. The pattern is independently validating — multiple unrelated institutional types entering same name same window without coordinating.

Comparison vs MU (also in AI-momentum complex): MU showed OPPOSITE pattern same window — Capital World 70M → 42M (-40%), PRIMECAP 37M → 22M (-40%), zero hedge-fund initiations Q3 25 - Q1 26. Active managers distributing into strength. BMNR institutional setup is materially earlier-cycle than MU's.

Framework implication: validates T2 baseline 5% sizing and supports T1 upgrade gate consideration when (a) CLARITY past committee, OR (b) flywheel re-engages at premium-to-mNAV. The institutional adoption pattern is now layered on top of: (a) Tom Lee operator-validated ETH/stablecoin/RWA thesis, (b) ETH staking yield mechanics (~72% of ~3.5M ETH staked), (c) flywheel mechanics (accretive issuance at premium-to-mNAV).

No immediate capital action required. Position stance reaffirmed.

text.content: Postmortem on April-May 2026 misses. INTC $50 (Apr 8) → $129 (May 11) = +158% / 33 days. All 6 forward signals visible in own session work (TeraFab Apr 7, US govt 10% stake, Nvidia $5B + Musk anchors, Q1 binary <60d, Lee/Newton tech double-OW, +76% YTD by Apr 15, insider buys across 47 recent transactions). Verdict on Apr 8 was 'AVOID. Max 2% position size — speculative only.' Zero exposure taken. GEV $911 → $1,182 (+30%) and AMD $285 → $440 (+54%) followed the same pattern — 5/6 forward signals, deferred via 'wait for pullback' that never came. Two failure modes diagnosed: (1) binary 'AVOID' vocabulary eliminated the asymmetric-expression slot; (2) 'wait for pullback to $X' as default produces zero entry in trending environments. Fix: Two-Lens Test inline forces forward-signal count; 4+ mandates asymmetric expression option with pre-built order ticket. P-AI-MOMENTUM extended for source-named-pick post-naming runs (>15%/5d). Three standing rules formalize Asymmetric Sleeve + Catalyst Calendar + FMP-as-judgment. Total scope: 12 principles up from 11, three new behavioral rules, five new Workflow D bypass-detection signals.

1. P-ASYMMETRIC (12th principle) added. Two-Lens Test: count forward signals across 6 categories (named source pick / strategic-government overlay / binary catalyst <60d / Lee-Newton macro / insider activity / momentum confirmation) against trailing-financials bear case. Output discipline: 4+ forward signals → asymmetric expression option REQUIRED in output, with pre-built order ticket. 2-3 signals → mention not mandate. 0-1 → conventional verdict.

2. Vocabulary discipline: single-word AVOID retired. Decomposes to AVOID-EQUITY-EXPRESS-VIA-OPTIONS / NOT-A-FIT / NOT-NOW-WITH-TRIGGER.

3. P-AI-MOMENTUM extended: source-named picks (FS NEW Top 5 / Meridian-flagged / equivalent) running >15% in 5 trading days post-naming with no thesis-break → ATH-pursuit decision surfacing in Launcher Step 1, Two-Lens auto-runs, scale-in 1/3+1/3+1/3 cap 2% or Asymmetric Sleeve options expression. Generalizes beyond AI-only scope.

4. Asymmetric Sleeve formalized as behavioral standing rule: $10-30K rolling, 4-8 positions max, $1-5K per position, defined-risk only (call spreads/LEAPS, never naked premium that goes to zero), source-tagged at entry (which catalyst, named backer, X/6 forward count), 30-day re-evaluation, funded from realized gains or Speed Lane cash.

5. Catalyst Calendar Notion page + Launcher Step 3: every launcher scans binaries <30d on watchlist + held names, Two-Lens auto-runs on hits. Pending operator: create the Notion page and provide UUID.

6. FMP-as-judgment standing rule (no formal tier promotion gates — Claude judges depth that fits decision):
  - Two-Lens 3+ signals → auto-pull Senate trades + 13F top-25 changes + 8-K last 90d + OpenInsider link
  - ≥$25K notional / Deepdive / 5+ signals → fundamentals depth (Piotroski + Altman + DCF + 3y/5y growth + 8Q earnings surprises + financial-estimates revision pace + sector PE peers + segments + short interest + analyst PT cluster timestamps)
  - ≥$50K / ≥2 accounts / Generational Lane → full depth (statements + exec comp + M&A + peer table + transcript + 5y insider history)
  - Output table populates BOTH trailing AND forward lens metrics (confirmation-bias mitigation)
  - ETFs gracefully degrade: F-score/Z-score/DCF N/A; substitute fund flows / expense ratio / top-10 turnover / premium-discount NAV / AUM trend

Triggered by postmortem on April-May 2026 misses: INTC $50→$129 (+158%, 33 days, 6/6 forward signals, v11.3 verdict 'AVOID max 2% spec'); GEV $911→$1,182 (+30%, 5/6, verdict 'wait $800-850 pullback'); AMD $285→$440 (+54%, 5/6, verdict 'defer <$320'). Pullbacks never came; exposure zero or deferred.

Diagnosis: two failure modes. (1) Binary AVOID vocabulary eliminated the asymmetric-expression slot — no place for 'trailing veto on equity but forward catalyst warrants defined-risk options.' (2) 'Wait for pullback to $X' as default produces zero entry in trending environments; P-AI-MOMENTUM scoped to AI-momentum names only, didn't generalize.

Replay validation: INTC under v11.4 → Two-Lens 6/6 → asymmetric expression mandatory → Jan 2027 $50 calls $3-5K from Asymmetric Sleeve → would have returned $25-50K. Different and better than v11.3 output.

Validations #24-29 added. Workflow D extended with 5 new bypass-detection signals plus empirical calibration target on the 15%/5d threshold.

Open items / on horizon:
- Catalyst Calendar Notion page creation + UUID provisioning
- First-session validation pass under v11.4
- Empirical calibration loop on the 15%/5d threshold (next 10 FS NEW Top 5 namings)

text.content:
Operator-reported failure 5/11/26: uploaded fresh broker PDFs earlier today expecting Latest Portfolio to update; it did not. Latest Portfolio still dated 5/8 12:16 ET.

DIAGNOSIS: v11.0 'Latest Portfolio write-on-update' standing rule said writes happen 'any time fresh broker PDFs are uploaded AND processed.' The 'AND processed' clause was the weak point — depended on Claude in each session actively running extractor + Notion write as a side-effect of operator's stated task. Three failure modes:
(1) PDFs are chat-scoped — files uploaded in Chat A invisible to Chat B (separate containers). Acknowledged as 'framework patch candidate' in chat cd7561d9 on 5/6, never patched.
(2) Trigger too weak — if operator uploaded PDFs while asking unrelated question (RKLB, ZEC, options, DAT thesis — all today's chats), Claude focused on the stated question and skipped the extract+write. Last successful Latest Portfolio write was chat 3823f43e 'Account positions update' on 5/8 — chat whose explicit task was the update. Chat 0bb923fc 'Complete refresh' on 5/8 evening extracted PDFs in-chat but did NOT write to Latest Portfolio.
(3) Memory edit #12 referenced /home/claude/lean_

text.content: Replaces memory edit #28 (third-party PE verification) with a consolidated Data Verification Methodology. Driven by RGTI/OKLO deep-dive on 5/11/26 — operator pushed back on naive 'shares disposed = bearish' read. Original mistake: flagging aggregate insider sales without filtering for transaction type (RSU cover, 10b5-1, exercise-and-sell), % of insider stake, or coordinated catalyst timing. Operator's framework correction: '1% of total stock sold is not a strong negative signal.' Refined framework filters to discretionary C-suite sales, uses % of remaining stake (not absolute count), and flags pre-catalyst coordinated sales as highest signal. Memory #28 holds compressed rule; this page holds full spec with worked examples (OKLO co-founder $28M coordinated dump 4d pre-earnings = moderately bearish; RGTI directors trimming 23-63% over 6mo with C-suite mostly noise = mild bearish, no catalyst timing).

text.content:
Sources: Own Analysis + Gemini Research audit.

Three-failure-mode session drove the patch:

(1) Within-thread oscillation on IGV/PBW/DRIV/SNSR: Path C (IGV+PBW+cash) → Path C no cash (Turn 4 silent cash-floor drop) → 2:1 split → all-four basket (Turn 12 framing flip) → all-IGV (Turn 14 toughness flip) → Path E all-four conviction-weighted (Turn 16 capitulation to articulate but mixed-evidence pushback). 180° round-trip with intermediate stops. Only consistent throughline was the conviction-tier framing.

(2) PBW Active rationale logged at Path C and never updated through subsequent flips — frozen mid-flight DB state, would have shown up corrupted in next session.

(3) Operator stated friction with verbal [FILL CONFIRMED] reconciliation ritual — doesn't execute recs as scripts, takes them as input and adapts. Demanding line-by-line confirmation creates friction that gets bypassed.

Gemini audit confirmed independently: identified Constraint Erasure (Turn 4 cash-floor drop), Reactive Reversal (Turn 12 question-as-directive), Total Erasure of Prior Rationale (each flip was all-or-nothing, no synthesis), Source-Hierarchy Collapse. Proposed Reconciliation Table as primary structural fix. Explicitly self-identified susceptibility to same failure mode — evidence the pattern is structural across LLMs not Claude-specific.

v11.2 changes:

P-NO-FILL-NO-FACT REWRITE: Verbal [FILL CONFIRMED] retired. PDF upload IS the truth event. Active Rationales DB Status field gains 'Acted Upon' (position moved in recommended direction, exact form may differ from script). Reconciliation runs mechanically at PDF processing time. Recs framed as input not as scripts.

P-INTEGRITY NEW (10th principle): Holds analytical position absent new evidence. Symmetric — catches both agreement-bias (cave to pushback) AND toughness-bias (over-correct in opposite direction). Within-session reversals require Claude to name what new evidence triggered the reweigh. Synthesize-don't-replace template ('you're right about X, but Y') preferred over wholesale flip.

P-RATIONALE-PERSIST WRITE-TRIGGER REFINEMENT: Writes batch at commit/pivot/PDF/long-thread-auto-save, NOT per-turn. New 🧠 Held tag (queued-for-write) vs 🧠 Logged tag (written this turn). Addresses operator concern about excessive Notion writes during conversation flow + prevents in-thread state corruption.

RECONCILIATION TABLE behavioral rule: trades ≥$50K notional or ≥2 accounts produce structured Action / Source Basis / Framework Compliance table before execution. 'Conflict' entries explicit not blocking — makes framework violations visible at decision time, operator override acknowledged not silent. Direct fix for Turn 4 silent cash-floor drop + multi-leg source-tagging bundling.

FRAMING-ACCEPTANCE GATE behavioral rule: when operator imposes a framing ('deploy all' / 'all four picks'), surface trade-offs explicitly before complying. Earlier-recommended elements NOT silently dropped when constraint changes.

Validations #19-22 added. Workflow D Architecture review extended with 5 new bypass-detection signals.

Project file Custom_Instructions_v11_

text.content:
Delta-only entry per CI standing rule. Triggered by parallel-chat session: Claude produced substantive framing on Newton's May 2026 drop of SPHB ('drop ≠ sell, here's how to think about whether your thesis still holds for your $11K PCRA position') — useful rationale that would have evaporated at session end because P-RATIONALE-PERSIST as scoped only fired on 'high-conviction trade rec doesn't get placed in-session.'

Two coordinated changes: (1) Surface expansion — capture rationales regardless of whether produced via launcher / deepdive / conversational chat / source-stance pass-through, regardless of execution state. (2) Substantiveness filter replaces conviction gate — same 'find it in 6 months' test that v10.3 applies to Decisions Log entries; conviction tier captured inline in Rationale text rather than as a binary gate.

Why substantiveness vs. all-recs preserved: removing both filters would push Active DB from ~5-10 entries to 50+, slow pre-flight (P-SIMPLICITY 20-min test), dilute 'needs my attention' signal, make W-D bypass-detection noisy, and re-introduce v10.3 anti-pattern. First draft was over-broad; operator pushback narrowed to substantiveness-filtered scope.

New asymmetric inline tag (🧠 Logged: TICKER · ...) fires when entries logged, silent on clean runs — different signaling than Step 1/2 launcher rules because this rule fires across every conversational surface; 'none in scope' announcements on every routine response would re-introduce v10.3 noise. W-D handles audit from the other direction (substantive stance present without log tag = bypass candidate).

Open question on substantiveness filter resolved with Option A (judgment + W-D audit) over Option B (keyword matching) — matches v10 trust model used by P-WAKE-UP triggers; keyword matching brittle on nuanced stance language ('the case for keeping SPHB has weakened').

DB schema unchanged — existing Active Trade Rationales columns (Action / Source / Lane / inline-conviction-text) cover broader scope.

Follow-up needed: full principle text in Principles_-_

text.content:
Delta-only entry per CI standing rule. Driven by two same-day failure modes:

(1) Bundled source-tagging violation: Top 5 #1 packaged 'Newton 5/7 + Lee 5/6 + Farrell 5/5 → trim FS-deletes / fund BMNR' as if all three sources backed BMNR. Newton 5/7 only supported the trim leg; Lee 5/6 was a generic 'scarce things' Macro Minute video with no tickers; Farrell 5/5 named CIFR not BMNR. Multi-leg disaggregation sub-rule added: each leg of a multi-leg rec carries its own source tag; bundling across legs is a violation.

(2) Cross-chat state loss: new chat asked 'which accounts hold INFL,' couldn't answer because all extracted position data lived in prior chat's container (dashboard.jsx, slim.json) and was wiped between sessions. No durable Notion store existed. New 📊 Latest Portfolio page created (overwrite-on-update, parent 💰 Finance, page id 35ac5031-4bb6-81fc-b792-e50bf86d63f4). Per-ticker aggregation includes per-account quantity breakdowns. P-READ-OR-PUNT trigger #2 added: cross-account ticker question → Latest Portfolio read.

(3) Latest Portfolio write-on-update rule: any time fresh broker PDFs are uploaded and processed, Claude writes Latest Portfolio. Trigger is the PDF-upload event itself, not launcher invocation (operator-clarified 5/8/26) — casual-session uploads outside Sprint / Where-do-I-stand still update the page. Forcing-function shape mirrors P-NO-FILL-NO-FACT.

(4) Explicit-named-action surfacing: Newton 5/7 IGV breakout ('add Software exposure on the breakout above $89, stop $87, target $97-100' — explicit ADD with full risk frame) was demoted to prose mention while operator-rebalancing items took Top 5 slots. New rule: explicit named ADD/BUY/TRIM/SELL with stop+target = default Top 5 candidate.

P-READ-OR-PUNT triggers renumbered: was 1-10, now 1-11. Workflow D Architecture review extended to flag (a) PDF uploads that failed to write Latest Portfolio regardless of launcher context, (b) explicit-named-action items demoted to prose, (c) multi-leg recs bundling sources across legs. Validations #16-19 added.

Full v11.0 file at /mnt/user-data/outputs/Custom_Instructions_v11_

text.content: Framework / methodology

rich_text.plain_text: Framework / methodology

text.content:
Triggered by operator request 5/7-5/8 for ongoing scan of 'opportunities to invest alongside Thiel' on a curated public watchlist. Earlier in same session: stored Thiel-Network Exposure Tracker entry in 📚 Research Queue with thesis synthesis + watchlist tickers (PLTR, RKLB, COIN, CRCL, HOOD, MSTR public; SpaceX, Anduril, Stripe, OpenAI, Anthropic, Erebor, Panthalassa private/IPO watch) + 6 trigger rules + reference prices captured 5/7 intraday. Operator opted into 'option 2' — extend launcher behavior to scan watchlist on every visit — over status quo (reactive only) and full Pipedream build (deferred).

New rule mirrors Launcher Step 1 (FS ranker sweep) architecture: every launcher button runs Step 2 after Step 1, before composing response. Pulls live FMP batch-quote on watchlist, computes drift from reference prices, surfaces inline ONLY if a trigger fires per Research Queue entry's rules:
1. Price drift >10% from reference (rolling 30-day window)
2. Thiel/Founders Fund news involving watchlist name (passive — only if news context being pulled this turn anyway)
3. IPO catalyst on private name (SpaceX, Anduril, Stripe, OpenAI, Anthropic, Erebor, Panthalassa)
4. Regulatory move (CLARITY Senate, OCC trust grant to Stripe/Coinbase/Circle/Ripple/Paxos, SEC tokenization rule)
5. Source convergence — Lee/Newton/Meridian/Farrell flags watchlist ticker

Key divergence from Step 1: quiet-day silence acceptable (Step 1 always surfaces tier-ordered hits). Operator's stated need is 'alert when there are opportunities,' not constant noise on quiet days. Forcing-function shape preserved: act of reporting 'Thiel scan: clean' or 'Thiel scan: triggers fired on X' forces the run; silent omission is a v10.9+ violation.

Source classification: Thiel-network research is operator-prompted, not Lee/Newton/Meridian/Farrell — source-tagging gate does not formally apply. Capital actions stemming from Step 2 triggers still go through normal sizing/conviction discipline.

What changed in the actual file:
- Title: v10.8 → v10.9
- Project Knowledge Pointers: added Research Queue row
- Launcher table: Step 2 reference added to Where do I stand / Top 5 actions / What changed / Deepdive entries
- Behavioral Standing Rules: new 'Launcher Step 2 — Thiel-Network watchlist scan' bullet (~250 words, mirrors Step 1 structure)
- Workflow D Architecture review #2: extended with Step 2 bypass + false-positive flagging
- Workflow D Sources review #3: extended with Thiel-watchlist hit-rate tracking for v11 source-hit-rate scoring
- Validation header: v10.8 → v10.9
- Validation #15 added covering Step 2 forcing-function shape
- 'What's Cut and Added': v10.9 line added
- Changelog: v10.9 entry added at top

P-SIMPLICITY check: passes 20-min usability (zero operator effort during launchers), passes forcing function (deterministic on launcher visit), bypass cost is real (operator misses opportunity windows like CRCL -7.8% on 5/7 without proactive surface). Strictly mechanical — no judgment burden on operator.

Deferred: full Pipedream weekly cron build for true proactive (off-launcher) alerts. Not built. Defer until Workflow B (Fundstrat triage) is fully operational. Logged in Research Queue entry's automation spec section.

text.content: TRIGGER: Workflow A briefing went dark 5/4 15:30 ET → 5/7 15:50 ET (~3 calendar days, 2 full trading days, ~16 missed scheduled runs). Pipedream Event History showed every step green ✓ across all runs. Root cause: Notion SDK warned with 400 validation_error (table row width mismatch caused by Pipedream's markdown parser collapsing 3 tables into 1) but the action wrapper logged $summary='Appended blocks successfully' regardless. $return_value was null but no step inspected it. Heartbeat step also failed downstream of the broken write, but heartbeat freshness wasn't being externally monitored — so the runbook line 'if heartbeat >12hrs old on weekday, cron is dead' had no enforcement. text.content: parent.page

rich_text.plain_text: parent.page

text.content:
_id bug fixed 5/6, but only caught because operator noticed missing emails).

PRINCIPLE (memorialized): Every automated workflow that writes to Notion or sends notifications must have an external watchdog verifying its outputs are fresh. Watchdog reads RESULTS, not status flags. 'Step succeeded' is not 'outcome happened.' This is operationally the same shape as P-NO-FILL-NO-FACT — verify outcome, not the friendly summary message — applied to the workflow layer instead of the trade-fill layer. Any new automation gets added to the watchdog's check list before it ships.

IMPLEMENTATION: Workflow E — Watchdog. Daily 7am ET cron. Reads 🫀 Pipedream Heartbeat, 📡 Latest Briefing, and 📧 Fundstrat Inbox directly via Notion API. Three checks: (1) heartbeat <4h old, (2) briefing 'Generated:' timestamp <4h old, (3) Fundstrat Inbox newest entry <1.5d old (skip on weekends). Special check: heartbeat fresh + briefing stale = SILENT WRITE FAILURE flag (the exact 5/4-5/7 pattern). Sends Pushover priority-1 alert if any check trips. Spec'd in chat 5/7 evening; deployment pending.

LINEAGE: Patches Workflow A compose step also shipped this session (escape pipes, switch tables to plain-text paragraph blocks with double-newline separation to dodge Notion's 100 rich_text segment cap). Workflow B was separately fixed 5/6 per memory. Watchdog covers both going forward + any future workflows (Workflow C deferred, Workflow D when deployed).

NOTE: Workflow E is itself a single point of failure. Mitigation: Pipedream sends account-level email if a workflow errors repeatedly, so if E itself dies the platform tells you. Acceptable residual risk.

text.content: All (framework / governance)

rich_text.plain_text: All (framework / governance)

text.content:
Workflow D Sources axis (per CI v10.4 source-tagging gate) needs an outcome ledger to do hit-rate analysis by source. Without it, the Sources axis ships blind — only Triage Log false-positive flagging works, no calibration on which source confidence levels (hedge / high-conviction / structural) actually correlate with outcomes.

DB design (14 properties):
- Call (title), Ticker, Direction, Sources (multi-select: Lee/Newton/Meridian/Farrell/Polymarket/Council/Operator/Other), Source Tags (free-text per-source confidence + recency), Approved/Executed/Closed dates, Entry/Exit prices, Status (Approved-Pending/Open/Closed/Aborted/Stopped-Out), Outcome Label (Worked/Mixed/Wrong/Aborted/Pending), Outcome Notes, Related Rationale (one-way relation to Active Trade Rationales).

Design decisions:
- Multi-select Sources (Active Rationales has single-select; multi needed for proper attribution when a call cites Newton + Meridian + Polymarket together)
- Free-text Source Tags rather than per-source structured fields (P-SIMPLICITY: API can parse 'Newton 5/4 (hedge): brief context' format at analysis time)
- One-way Relation to Active Rationales (non-invasive — doesn't add a new column to Active Rationales DB)
- Clean date property names — no 'date:' prefix in the name itself (avoids the embedded-syntax bug Active Rationales has)

New logging discipline (in effect immediately): when an Approved Rationale gets [FILL CONFIRMED], Claude creates a Source Calls row. When the position closes, Claude updates Status → Closed, fills Exit Price + Outcome Label + Outcome Notes. Aborted Approved Rationales also get rows (Status=Aborted) so the system tracks which source confidences produce calls operator never pulls trigger on.

No backfill performed — starting clean from today. Workflow D Sources axis will report 'pending data accumulation' for first ~4 weeks until Closed entries accumulate.

DB URL:

text.content:
_id bug marked Resolved 5/6/26; schema cache + Sender field still Open).
2. Council Phase 3 write-back corrected to Decisions Log + Exit Triggers DB (was Prompt Library, killed in v10.3). Council prompt delivery clarified as inline paste-ready code blocks, never auto-saved.
3. Workflow D spec rewritten: explicit numbered Notion query list (steps 1–13, buildable as-is), 20K-token input cap, N-threshold anti-noise rule (<3 instances → 'watch' not 'suggestion'), 4-week suggestion repeat dampener, retrospective loop on prior 4 weeks, JSON output schema specified. text.content:
+ fs_holdings.json. Ranker scans inbox entries via SIX paths: (1) cashtag $TICKER, (2) explicit 'Tickers in Report:' headers, (3) verb+ticker context (buy/sell/add/trim NVDA), (4) ticker+noun context (NVDA stock/shares/calls), (5) whitelist match against held + watchlist, (6) company-name aliases (Nvidia→NVDA, Micron→MU). Plus per-position thesis keywords (BMNR→ETH/stablecoin/CLARITY) and macro themes (scarce_thesis, ai_power, crypto_regime).

Blacklist filters 200+ all-caps abbreviations (CEO/AI/USD/ETF/GDP) so bare-caps regex doesn't false-fire.

Tier ordering: HELD > THESIS-HELD > WATCHLIST > NAMED-NOT-HELD > MACRO. Rolled up into Sprint launcher (any of: Where do I stand, Top 5 actions, What changed, Fresh run).

Validation on today's 3 unique inbox entries: Hardika Apple piece elevated to HELD (caught Micron+Nvidia by alias); Farrell crypto note ranked THESIS-HELD via BMNR/MU/SMH thesis hits; Tom Lee macro video correctly elevated from MACRO to THESIS-HELD via BMNR(ETH) thesis + scarce_thesis macro theme. Without thesis layer, Tom Lee would have ranked last. Without aliases, Hardika would have ranked last.

Known v1 gaps queued for v1.1: action-verb weighting (so passing mention beats real call), recency decay, source-tagging gate auto-detection (hedge vs high-conviction language per CI v10.4), pull held list from live portfolio state (currently in fs_holdings.json).

Files: /mnt/user-data/outputs/fs_

text.content: Infrastructure (Pipedream / Notion 📧 Fundstrat Inbox)

rich_text.plain_text: Infrastructure (Pipedream / Notion 📧 Fundstrat Inbox)

text.content:
GAP IDENTIFIED 5/6/26: 📧 Fundstrat Inbox page existed since v9.1 spec but Workflow B as deployed had no Notion append step — emails were classified, triaged, and marked read with no durable archive. Discovered while doing AMD post-mortem (4/30 FS allocation update added AMD as Top 5 LC at $303.46 → $420 today, +38.5% in 10 trading days; 5/1 dash punted on 'wait for pullback' under pre-v10 doctrine). Inbox check showed page empty since 5/2.

FIX DEPLOYED: New Pipedream Node.js step 'archive_to_notion' inserted between log_triage and mark_read. Implementation: recursive Gmail payload walk + base64URL decode, prefer text/plain → fall back to HTML-stripped, drop link href URLs (was leaking unsubscribe tracking blobs), trim Fundstrat-specific footer markers (PS / Reg AC / Conflicts of Interest / disclaimers / Tickers in this video), chunk body into 1900-char paragraphs (Notion rich_text cap is 2000), wrap in toggle block with title format '[ts] [analyst] [type] — [subject]'.

VALIDATION: Tested on Sean Farrell Crypto-Comments email (5/5/26). Final result: 71953 raw HTML chars → 4055 cleaned chars. All 4 article sections preserved (Clarity Act / BTC Breaking Out / Positioning / STRC Dynamics + Bottom Line). Zero boilerplate, zero tracking URLs.

OPEN ITEMS (not blockers, deferred):
1. TICKERS field showing 'none' — triage step output schema doesn't expose tickers under field names archive_to_notion reads. Needs $return_value inspection to map field names. ~5 min next session.
2. Subject extraction works for FS emails but earlier '(no subject)' entries suggest some sender variants don't populate via the same header path. Self-healing as new email types arrive.
3. Push leg organic validation still pending — the test email scored Inbox-Silent so no Pushover expected. Awaits next Actionable-tier email with held ticker.
4. Workflow C (iOS share-sheet capture) DEFERRED INDEFINITELY — Fundstrat iOS app has no Share button, killing original spec. Optional fallback: Safari-only manual capture for FS articles encountered outside the app (~10 min build, low priority).

WHY THIS MATTERS: Closes one of two channels in the AMD-class miss pattern. Now FS email content is durably searchable in Notion alongside the structured Triage Log DB metadata. Together they give: structured signal (Triage Log: verdict, urgency, suggested action) + full source content (Inbox: original prose). Sprint Step 1 inbox read now actually returns content.

DOES NOT FIX: the AMD miss itself was a P-AI-MOMENTUM translation failure (saw the signal, applied 'wait for pullback' doctrine that v10 was added to correct), not an intake gap. Intake hardening is a separate, independent improvement.

text.content: Independent macro thesis — pre-Fundstrat baseline (May 6, 2026)

title.plain_text: Independent macro thesis — pre-Fundstrat baseline (May 6, 2026)

text.content: Source: r/ethtrader thread analysis 5/5/26 (multi-comment synthesis). Operator earlier in session articulated a 'binary $150+ vs zero by 2028' framing with '2-5 explosive spikes' triggered by CLARITY passage and institutional adoption. Reddit comments (Serenaded, ben2885, king_escobar, EdgeByContext) push back on the SPIKE PATTERN specifically, not the directional thesis. Mechanism reality: (1) Bitmine $10B ETH staked = 4.3% supply, accumulation flows via OTC desks not exchange order books — supply genuinely removed from circulation but exchange tape doesn't reflect it as price spikes; (2) ETH derivatives volume is 5-10x spot — paper markets absorb spot tightness for extended periods (oil analogy applies), divergence between futures and spot can persist; (3) HODLer conviction at multi-year highs (ETH Reserve Risk multi-year low) + ETH CEX reserves 14.5M (lowest since 2016) + 5/4 ETF inflows $61.3M (BlackRock $54.8M) all VALIDATE the supply drain side of the thesis. Synthesis with existing thesis: Lee 6-pillar thesis intact; BMNR accretive flywheel intact; mNAV gate framework intact. What CHANGES: the timing/shape expectation. Underlying mechanism produces slow grind, not spikes. NEWS catalysts (CLARITY, institutional adoption announcements) can superimpose sentiment-driven spikes on the grind, but the underlying supply→price translation happens slowly via derivative capitulation to spot scarcity. Implications for capital deployment: (a) Favor longer-dated options (2028 > 2027) — slow grind rewards duration over convexity; (b) Equity adds in weakness remain favored — grind benefits the holder; (c) REJECT weekly rolling calls — mechanism does not produce consistent timeable spikes; (d) Tier 3 gate (a) — premium-to-mNAV >1.05 sustained 60d — likely takes longer to develop given OTC accumulation pattern (no immediate tape-driven premium expansion). Thesis-break watchpoint: sudden CEX deposit spikes from 2017-era legacy whales = redistribution signal. Currently not firing; flag to monitor in mNAV/ETH tracker reads. Confidence: r/ethtrader is anecdotal not Lee/Newton/Meridian/Farrell, BUT the OTC-flow and derivatives-absorption mechanisms are mechanically sound and consistent with how institutional crypto accumulation has worked historically (MSTR BTC playbook 2020-2024). Recency: cited data is current week. Action items: (a) Memory updated with mechanism nuance (memory #29); (b) Prior $10K options allocation discussion — 50/50 split 2027/2028 — should nudge toward 35/65 or 40/60 favoring 2028 if operator wants to operationalize this update; (c) parents account hybrid (30/70 2027/2028) is already aligned with this nuance, no change needed.

text.content: Framework / methodology

rich_text.plain_text: Framework / methodology

text.content:
DELTA from v10.3.1:

1. NEW behavioral standing rule — 'Source-tagging gate (confidence + recency)'. Before citing Lee/Newton/Meridian/Farrell in any rec touching capital action, must tag in response BOTH (a) confidence (hedge-language vs high-conviction) and (b) recency (when news broke vs when source published). Format examples included. Hedge-language → rec magnitude must match. Stale repackaging → flag 'no new info.' [FILL CONFIRMED]-style forcing function — visibility at point of use forces the check.

2. REMOVED 'Newton technicals weighted only when Newton flags high-confidence' bullet — subsumed by the general source-tagging gate. Newton discipline preserved but enforced via visible tagging instead of memory-only rule.

3. Workflow D self-evolution targets updated to include source-tagging-gate violation tracking + hedge-vs-high-conviction outcome correlation.

4. Validation #10 added.

TRIGGER: two source-weighting drift instances surfaced in single session 5/5/26 morning. Newton 5/4 hedge-language framed as actionable; Farrell 5/5 stale 5/1 catalyst framed as today's signal. Different surface forms (confidence vs recency), same root cause: not interrogating source before citing. Memory-only enforcement (CI standing rule existed) failed to fire at point of use. Promoted to CI durable rule with explicit format requirement.

Bumped to v10.4 (not v10.3.2) because addition is structural — new standing rule with its own forcing-function mechanism, not a same-day patch to an existing one.

Full canonical text in project file Custom_Instructions_v10_

text.content: Framework / methodology — affects all sleeves indirectly via recommendation quality

rich_text.plain_text: Framework / methodology — affects all sleeves indirectly via recommendation quality

text.content:
Two source-weighting drift instances surfaced in a single session, both translating into capital-action recommendations that exceeded what the underlying source actually supported.

INSTANCE 1 — Newton confidence over-weighting. Newton 5/4 PM piece used hedge-language: 'use any further strength to lighten exposure for those with a shorter-term time horizon (3-5 weeks).' That is a horizon caveat, not a high-confidence directional call. I framed it as 'Newton tactical caution' and translated to 'reasonable window to free 3-5% cash if you want dry powder for the consolidation.' Magnitude of recommendation didn't match magnitude of underlying signal. CI standing rule says weight Newton only when he flags high-confidence; rule lived too far from point-of-use to fire when needed.

INSTANCE 2 — Farrell stale-news repackaging. Farrell 5/5 published note on CLARITY Act with Polymarket at 65%, framed as today's signal triggering BMNR Tier 3 gate (c) reconsideration. Actual catalyst was Friday 5/1: Tillis/Alsobrooks compromise text dropped, Polymarket jumped 46%→65% intraday. By 5/5 the move had already happened, partially faded, and was an open question on hold-vs-slip. I quoted Farrell at face value as 'high-conviction structural' without checking news recency. Operator caught it.

ROOT CAUSE — both failures share the same shape: not interrogating the source before citing it. Different surface forms (confidence laundering vs. recency laundering), same drift mechanism. Source-weighting rules in CI v10.3 are memory-only enforcement, no forcing function at point-of-use.

CORRECTIVE — added memory entry (#27, replaces prior version): 'Source tagging (Investing): before citing Lee/Newton/Meridian/Farrell in any rec touching capital action (trim/add/cash/size), tag BOTH (a) confidence — hedge vs high-conviction, and (b) recency — when news broke vs when source published. Format: Newton 5/4 (hedge) / Farrell 5/5 (reporting 5/1 catalyst, no fresh data) / Lee (high-conviction). Hedge → rec magnitude must match. Stale repackaging → flag no new info. [FILL CONFIRMED]-style forcing function.'

Mechanism mirrors P-NO-FILL-NO-FACT [FILL CONFIRMED] tagging: visibility forces the check. Act of writing the tag forces the evaluation before the citation lands.

META — there was a third drift instance in the same conversation: I offered an FDF DB entry to track this pattern, forgetting that v10.3 explicitly killed the FDF DB. The fix to a drift conversation contained another drift. Pattern is real; recurrence will be tracked via this DL entry rather than via FDF.

NEXT — if pattern recurs (third instance in any future session), trigger CI patch v10.3→v10.4 to add source-tagging gate as behavioral standing rule with explicit format enforcement. Until then, memory entry holds.

Session ref: 5/5/26 morning.

text.content: Framework / workflow

rich_text.plain_text: Framework / workflow

text.content:
Triggered by same-session staleness failure mode: SMH trim drafted at 1 PM May 4 became inconsistent with P-AI-MOMENTUM-EXIT added at 3 PM via Council write-back; staleness only caught when operator asked Q late evening.

Three additions to Behavioral Standing Rules section of CI:

1. Recommendation freshness & approval state. Drafts vs. Approved are distinct states. Drafts auto-invalidate on framework change in same session, >24hr without approval, or market regime shift. Claude must distinguish 'here's what I think' from 'here's the plan you approved' in language. Drafts logged to Notion get explicit DRAFT tag.

2. Pre-flight check on session open. Auto-runs before launcher menu / Where do I stand / What changed / Fresh run. Scans Active Trade Rationales DB + recent Drafts for stale references, price drift >5%, age >24hr without approval, past Valid Until. Surfaces as one-line flag if found.

3. In-session re-check on framework change. When Claude adds memory edit / CI patch / Council write-back later in same session than a Draft was made, Claude re-checks earlier Drafts against new rule before next reference. Flags conflicts explicitly.

Validation #9 added. Behavioral rules, not P-codes (don't gate trading judgment, govern Claude bookkeeping). P-SIMPLICITY passes: 20-min usability ✓ (zero operator effort), forcing function ✓ (deterministic on session open + framework change), bypass cost real (May 4 SMH/AI-MOMENTUM-EXIT failure is the proof).

Project file: Custom_Instructions_v10_3_

Triggered by same-session staleness failure mode: SMH trim drafted at 1 PM May 4 became inconsistent with P-AI-MOMENTUM-EXIT added at 3 PM via Council write-back; staleness only caught when operator asked Q late evening.

Three additions to Behavioral Standing Rules section of CI:

1. Recommendation freshness & approval state. Drafts vs. Approved are distinct states. Drafts auto-invalidate on framework change in same session, >24hr without approval, or market regime shift. Claude must distinguish 'here's what I think' from 'here's the plan you approved' in language. Drafts logged to Notion get explicit DRAFT tag.

2. Pre-flight check on session open. Auto-runs before launcher menu / Where do I stand / What changed / Fresh run. Scans Active Trade Rationales DB + recent Drafts for stale references, price drift >5%, age >24hr without approval, past Valid Until. Surfaces as one-line flag if found.

3. In-session re-check on framework change. When Claude adds memory edit / CI patch / Council write-back later in same session than a Draft was made, Claude re-checks earlier Drafts against new rule before next reference. Flags conflicts explicitly.

Validation #9 added. Behavioral rules, not P-codes (don't gate trading judgment, govern Claude bookkeeping). P-SIMPLICITY passes: 20-min usability ✓ (zero operator effort), forcing function ✓ (deterministic on session open + framework change), bypass cost real (May 4 SMH/AI-MOMENTUM-EXIT failure is the proof).

rich_text.plain_text: New sleeve: International Value, Short-Duration FI, Contrarian Manager (~$180K target across VXUS/EFV, SGOV, BRK.B). Funded primarily from FSLook/FSTactical/SMH/MAGS trims.

text.content:
Council Phase 1 (Nemotron 3 Super, Gemini 3.1 Pro Thinking, GPT-5.4 Thinking) on Q2 source independence split: 5% / 15% / 15%. Operator chose 10% as compromise.

Mechanism (Council majority): not portfolio-theory rebalancing. Two converging arguments:
- Capital adequacy in stress (Gemini): a structurally orthogonal sleeve generates organic liquidity precisely when primary thesis (Fundstrat/Meridian) fails, preventing forced liquidation of core holdings
- Behavioral discipline (GPT): 5% is easy to ignore when it disagrees with you; 10-15% is large enough to force engagement with disconfirming evidence. 5% rejected as performative.

Proposed composition (initial $180K target):
- International value (VXUS or EFV): $80K (4.5% portfolio)
- Short-duration fixed income (SGOV): $60K (3.4%)
- Contrarian framework manager (BRK.B): $40K (2.2%)

Funding: too large for BWXT residual + ETF wrapper micro-trims. Material Fundstrat-traceable trims required: GRNY 1,000-1,500 sh (~$27-40K), GRNJ 800-1,200 sh (~$24-35K), SMH +50-80 sh (~$25-40K), MAGS +500-800 sh (~$33-53K), FSTactical trims (~$20-30K). Total trim capacity ~$130-200K.

Pace: 8-12 weeks. Slowest of the four current buildouts (BMNR / XLF / hedge book v0 / source-independent) due to size, not urgency. Quarterly review on its own terms, not through Fundstrat lens.

This is the largest single structural change to portfolio shape since v9 framework redesign in late April.

text.content:
Council Phase 1 unanimous on Q3: Tier 2 NOT Tier 3, via three different mechanisms (genuine edge, not groupthink).

Three different mechanisms:
- Nemotron 3 Super: mNAV mechanic. Accretive issuance flywheel requires sustained premium-to-mNAV. BMNR currently at-or-below mNAV → flywheel off. Stock down 59% over 6 months despite holding 4.875M ETH demonstrates market not currently rewarding scale alone.
- Gemini 3.1 Pro Thinking: correlation amplification. BMNR Tier 3 (15%) on top of 55% AI = ~70% of portfolio correlated to single risk-off event via crypto-Nasdaq beta channel. ETH historically trades as high-beta Nasdaq proxy in severe selloffs — 'Generational Lane' label may mask that BMNR adds risk rather than diversification.
- GPT-5.4 Thinking: stablecoin moat erosion. $7B+ USDT left Ethereum in Q1 2026 (largest single-quarter outflow on record). Direct challenge to Pillar 1 of Lee 6-pillar ETH thesis (stablecoin settlement dominance). Doesn't break thesis (ETH still ~60% global stablecoin supply) but adds timing uncertainty.

Tier 3 (15% cap = $267K) gating conditions — ALL THREE must close simultaneously:
1. BMNR premium-to-mNAV >1.05 sustained for 60 days
2. ETH stablecoin supply on Ethereum stabilizing or growing QoQ
3. CLARITY Act advancing past committee vote

Until all three close, BMNR cap stays at Tier 2 ($178K = 10% of portfolio).

This SUPERSEDES the May 3 reclassification language that left Tier 3 sizing 'pending Council clearance' with implicit positive framing. Council cleared the lane reclassification but explicitly NOT the Tier 3 cap. Buildout to $80K initial → $178K Tier 2 max proceeds. Tier 3 conversation reopens only when gates close.

Monitoring cadence: weekly check on premium/mNAV ratio (use BMNR market cap vs ETH holdings * ETH price). Monthly review of stablecoin supply trends. CLARITY Act tracked separately in policy tape.

text.content:
Council Phase 1 (Nemotron 3 Super, Gemini 3.1 Pro Thinking, GPT-5.4 Thinking) exposed a rules gap: the original 'trim ETF wrappers above 60% AI complex effective look-through, never single-name winners' had NO governing trigger for NVDA 11.2% / MU 10.5% / AVGO 4.0% concentration. Trim band only governed cosmetic wrappers; actual concentration was ungoverned.

New rule P-AI-MOMENTUM-EXIT operates as companion to P-AI-MOMENTUM (which still stands; ATH ≠ sell signal). Trigger: any TWO of four conditions in 60-day window:

1. NVDA DIO >120 days for 2 consecutive quarters (current 110; threshold tightened from Council's 130 given rising trend 76→110 over 12 months)
2. Hyperscaler capex/revenue ratio widens in 2 consecutive quarterly prints (capex growth - revenue growth ≥20pp gap both quarters)
3. Post-earnings price reaction negative ≥3 of last 4 hyperscaler prints (MSFT/META/GOOGL/AMZN closed -2% or worse next session despite EPS beat)
4. NVDA forward gross margin guide cut ≥150 bps QoQ at any single print

Mechanic: 2-of-4 → ETF wrapper trim toward 45-50% (SMH first, then MAGS, then GRNY). 3-of-4 → add single-name semis trim. Hard cap: do not exceed 50% effective look-through within 30 days of trigger.

Monitoring cadence: monthly minimum; weekly during NVDA earnings + hyperscaler earnings clusters. None firing currently. Rule pre-commits the response when triggers fire, removing discretionary lag.

Source: Council Phase 1 May 4 2026; verdict converged across 3 models via different mechanisms (genuine edge, no Phase 2 needed).

text.content:
Cross-pollinated from Life OS v8.1 (May 3, 2026), which added a 'no phantom knowledge' guardrail after a documented drift flag — a Life OS instance claimed absence of Belle Insights without performing the search that would have grounded it. State claim, no read.

Same failure mode applies in investing at higher stakes: confidently saying 'you don't hold X', quoting a cost basis from memory, estimating sleeve concentration without aggregating, or recommending an add to a position without reading the live thesis. v10's existing principles cover this only in narrow slices: P-NO-FILL-NO-FACT (fills only) and the user-prefs price-accuracy rule (live quotes only). The general case — any state claim made without a read — was uncovered.

P-READ-OR-PUNT closes the gap. Behavior gate: never claim absence/presence/value of stored portfolio state from memory. If a read hasn't happened in this turn, fetch-and-report or say 'I haven't checked.' Applies to direct recall AND indirect state-aware framing. Hard guardrail (not soft protocol) — soft = judgment-mediated = exactly the leak that produced the Life OS failure. Moves the read decision out of judgment-space into a deterministic 8-trigger list (ticker mention, trade-decision language, sleeve/concentration question, recall question, drafting, principle/goal touching, cash-relevant rec, trigger-distance claim).

Orthogonal to read tier: tier governs surrounding state volume, trigger governs whether the specific grounding read is mandatory. P-NO-FILL-NO-FACT and user-prefs price rule retained as named subcases — P-NO-FILL-NO-FACT for the [FILL CONFIRMED] tagging mechanic, the user-prefs rule for redundancy at the user-prefs layer.

Principal count goes 8 → 9. Passes P-SIMPLICITY gates: gates behavior (forces read before composition), guards a documented failure mode (Life OS Belle Insights drift flag, generalizable to investing).

System-prompt version of the guardrail runs in parallel for one version cycle, then retires if CI version proves durable.

Critical for BMNR thesis because staking yield is the structural cash-flow differentiator vs MSTR (~72% of ~3.5M ETH staked at 3-4% APR ≈ $250-350M annualized real income). Cross-reference: parent BMNR Generational Lane DL entry (5/3/26) at

Critical for BMNR thesis because staking yield is the structural cash-flow differentiator vs MSTR (~72% of ~3.5M ETH staked at 3-4% APR ≈ $250-350M annualized real income). Cross-reference: parent BMNR Generational Lane DL entry (5/3/26) at
BMNR — Lane Reclassification: Buy-and-Hold → Generational + Thesis Lock

text.content:
FINDING: ETH staking is EXPLICITLY PERMITTED. The Tillis-Alsobrooks compromise prohibition is narrowly scoped to stablecoin yield ONLY. Three independent layers of evidence:

1) HOUSE BILL TEXT (H.R. 3633, passed July 2025, 294-134) explicitly defines and protects two staking modes: 'Self Staking' (owner of staked digital commodity = operator of node/validator) and 'Self-Custodial Staking with a Third Party' (operator does not maintain custody/control of the staked asset). Both are recognized as legitimate consensus-mechanism participation. BMNR's corporate-treasury staking model fits 'Self Staking' definition cleanly. Source:

FINDING: ETH staking is EXPLICITLY PERMITTED. The Tillis-Alsobrooks compromise prohibition is narrowly scoped to stablecoin yield ONLY. Three independent layers of evidence:

1) HOUSE BILL TEXT (H.R. 3633, passed July 2025, 294-134) explicitly defines and protects two staking modes: 'Self Staking' (owner of staked digital commodity = operator of node/validator) and 'Self-Custodial Staking with a Third Party' (operator does not maintain custody/control of the staked asset). Both are recognized as legitimate consensus-mechanism participation. BMNR's corporate-treasury staking model fits 'Self Staking' definition cleanly. Source:

text.content: Congress.gov

rich_text.plain_text: Congress.gov

text.content:
H.R. 3633 text.

2) SENATE BANKING DRAFT (Tillis-Alsobrooks compromise released 5/1/26): The yield prohibition is narrowly scoped to stablecoin balances. The bill explicitly ALLOWS activity-based rewards including 'transacting, staking, providing liquidity or posting collateral' (The Block, January 13 2026 coverage of Sen. Tim Scott bipartisan draft, language preserved through May Tillis/Alsobrooks compromise). CoinDesk 5/1/26: prohibition limited to yield 'on stablecoin deposits if that yield is the functional or economic equivalent to banks' offerings.' Coinbase Chief Legal Officer Paul Grewal: 'language preserves activity-based rewards tied to real participation on crypto platforms' (CoinDesk 5/2/26).

3) SEC + CFTC JOINT TAXONOMY (3/17/26): Interim policy explicitly excluded staking, mining, and airdrops from securities law—a reversal from the Gensler era. ETH was among the 16 digital assets classified as digital commodities. This puts a regulatory floor under BMNR's staking model independent of CLARITY passage.

4) DEFI/INFRASTRUCTURE CARVE-OUT: Bill explicitly excludes transaction validation, node operation, and similar blockchain infrastructure activities from regulatory requirements. Miners, validators, stakers do not need to register with CFTC or SEC merely for participating in network consensus. BMNR is a corporate holder staking its own balance sheet (not a digital-asset-service-provider offering staking-as-a-service to retail), placing it even further from any prohibition than Coinbase's customer-facing staking products.

IMPLICATIONS FOR BMNR THESIS:

- Pillar #4 of Lee's six-pillar ETH thesis (staking yield as productive income stream) is legally protected under the bill draft as written.
- The structural differentiator vs MSTR—real, recurring cash income from staked ETH—is preserved, not threatened.
- The Tillis-Alsobrooks stablecoin yield prohibition is bullish-by-selection for BMNR: it constrains a competitor cash-flow stream (USDC/COIN platform rewards) while leaving BMNR's stream untouched.
- Invalidation Trigger #3 ('CLARITY Act dies in this Congress AND no successor bill in 2027') from the parent DL entry is now the only material policy-risk vector—the previously-unstated risk ('CLARITY passes but prohibits staking') is effectively zero given the explicit drafting.
- Even if bill stalls, SEC+CFTC interim policy from March already legitimized staking, providing an alternative regulatory floor.

PROBABILITY DECOMPOSITION (operator's mental model):
- P(CLARITY passes this Congress) ~ 62% (Polymarket via Farrell 5/4/26)
- P(staking permitted | CLARITY passes) ~ >95% (House and Senate drafts aligned; staking is one of the LESS contentious provisions; the political fight has been bank-vs-crypto on stablecoin yield, not on staking)
- P(staking permitted | CLARITY fails) ~ high, given SEC+CFTC interim taxonomy already excludes staking from securities law
- Combined: BMNR's staking model is structurally protected in nearly all scenarios.

LEGISLATIVE STATUS CAVEATS:
- Bill not yet law—Senate Banking Committee markup still pending as of 5/4/26
- Reconciliation between House and Senate versions still required after Senate markup
- Final text could shift in markup or reconciliation, though the staking provisions have been stable across draft iterations and have NOT been a contention point

LOGGING CONTEXT: This finding was triggered by Sean Farrell's May 4 morning notes on the Tillis-Alsobrooks stablecoin-yield compromise. Initial read could have been read as a threat to BMNR's staking thesis if the prohibition were broader. Operator surfaced the precise question; web research confirmed the prohibition is narrowly scoped and BMNR's model is preserved. Per source-independence rule, primary sources here (

text.content: BMNR / ETH / Generational Lane

rich_text.plain_text: BMNR / ETH / Generational Lane

text.content:
Tom Lee is a principal/founding chairman of BitMine Immersion (BMNR). FS analysts (Lee, Newton, Farrell, Fung) are walled off from naming BMNR in their published research due to the people-overlap conflict. Operator surfaced this rule on May 4, 2026.

IMPLICATIONS — change in how FS coverage is read:

1. FS silence on BMNR is NOT a neutral signal. It's structural. Do not infer anything (positive or negative) from absence of BMNR ticker mentions in FS notes.

2. FS ETH / stablecoin / Clarity Act / crypto market structure commentary should be read as the closest-to-explicit BMNR signal Lee is allowed to publish. When Lee is bullish ETH, treat as bullish BMNR by extension — even with no ticker.
