text.content:
Premarket Mon May 4 — operator pulled FS digest mid-session and asked for careful read. Tom Lee silent today; analyst contributions from Sean Farrell (digital assets) and Mark Newton (technical strategy).

FARRELL — CLARITY ACT COMPROMISE (10:33am ET):
- Tillis-Alsobrooks finalized stablecoin yield/rewards prohibition compromise. Senate moving toward crypto markup in May.
- Brian Armstrong (COIN) implicit support on X — notable given he opposed earlier drafts with similar yield restrictions.
- Polymarket: 62% probability of Clarity Act passage. Independent stream relative to FS.
- Read-through to BMNR (per FS-can't-name rule logged separately): directly reduces probability of one of BMNR's DL invalidation triggers. Bullish.

FARRELL — CRCL re-add to FS crypto equities portfolio (10:57am ET):
- Thesis: Clarity yield prohibition shifts USDC distribution bargaining → CRCL leverage to renegotiate >50% reserve income passthrough to COIN. Lower forward rate expectations supportive of multiple. Agentic commerce narrative for upcoming earnings.
- Caveat: lower rates directly reduce CRCL reserve income (the float math); Farrell appears to argue multiple/sentiment offset > NII compression. Worth verifying before any sizing.
- Not held. Deepdive candidate, not action today.

NEWTON — 'rally getting tired' (12:07pm ET):
- Breadth negative 3/2. SPX in negative territory.
- ^TNX nearing 4.50% (highest since last summer). USD bouncing. Metals down sharply (his framing: real yield channel).
- Crude bounced 3% today but under last week's peak; expects peaking + larger selloff approaching.
- QQQ within 1 day of TD Sequential '13 Countdown' sell. Per digest preamble, DeMark signals are the reliable Newton call.
- Explicit: 'consolidation in the rally before this rally continues' — pause, not reversal. Expects SPX won't exceed 7300, QQQ won't exceed 680 in the near term.
- Direct portfolio touch: GDX (metals down on rates rising). Broad tech beta (SMH, NVDA, AVGO, MAGS, GRNY, CLS, AMZN, MSFT, GOOGL) sees tactical headwind if 13 Countdown confirms tomorrow.
- Note: 13 Countdown is 'within 1 day of potentially' — has not yet printed. Watch for confirmation.

NEWTON — Consumer Services weakness (12:14pm ET):
- Index weakness; watch April lows for trendline break. NCLH cited (cruise, fuel + bookings).
- No direct portfolio touch (no cruise/casino/booking exposure; AMZN is e-commerce/cloud, not the leisure subsector).

PORTFOLIO REALITY CHECKS (from May 4 11:53am PDFs across 7 accounts, $1.832M total, $47.2K cash = 2.58%):

1. GEV TRAILING STOPS — SCOPE CORRECTION. Memory said 'across six accounts'. Actual GEV holdings: Parents Fidelity Joint 10 sh ($10,748), SKB RH Indiv 2 sh ($2,152). TWO accounts, 12 shares total, ~$12.9K = 0.7% of portfolio. Standing rec is real but materially smaller in $ exposure than memory implied. This corrects a known framing error.

2. LEU TIER 2/3 BOUNDARY — RESOLVED. 511 sh across 8 accounts = $105,836 = 5.78% of portfolio. Comfortably Tier 2. No P-SIZE15 override needed before May 5 AMC print. Open item from prior session can be closed.

3. CONCENTRATION FLAG (catalyst-driven only, not momentum): LEU at 21.2% of SKB RH Traditional IRA going into May 5 AMC binary. Per CI guidance not surfacing AI-momentum concentration as passive warning, but this one is binary-event concentration and worth operator awareness.

4. CASH 2.58% — below 5–10% target, doesn't block per CI; new buys ideally fund from a sell, not by drawing cash to ~zero. Newton's tactical caution argues better entries may come this week.

FS TRIAGE LOG BUG (logged separately to System Improvements Backlog): Workflow B preserves email metadata but body content is blank in resulting Notion pages. Today's two FS auto-ingested emails (Apple/Ternus, Golden Week) cannot be read from Notion — only the subject lines were captured.

text.content:
Operator-conviction Generational entry per Trading Lanes v10 entry criterion #4. Reference price $21.88 (5/3/26 close).

Long-duration thesis: ETH becomes core settlement layer for tokenized finance, stablecoins, RWAs, and AI-agent payments. BMNR is the public-equity vehicle for leveraged ETH exposure + staking yield + accretive issuance flywheel.

Multi-source convergence: (1) Lee's six-pillar ETH thesis — stablecoin settlement, RWA tokenization, neutrality, staking yield, '1971 moment', ETH/BTC ratio reversion. Price ladder: $7-9K near-term, $12-20K mid, $60K+ bull, $250K supercycle. (2) Standard Chartered (Geoff Kendrick, independent of Fundstrat): ETH $7,500 by EOY 2026, $30K by 2029, $40K by 2030 — directionally aligned near-term, more conservative long-term. (3) Policy momentum: CLARITY Act stablecoin yield compromise released 5/1-5/2/26 (Tillis/Alsobrooks); Polymarket signed-into-law-2026 odds repriced ~47%→70%. (4) Institutional flows: BlackRock BUIDL, DTCC Treasury tokenization plans, Robinhood tokenized equities all ETH-primary.

Mechanism uniqueness: Only major US-listed pure ETH treasury (vs MSTR for BTC). Productive yield on ~72% of stack (3.5M+ ETH staked, ~3-4% APR ≈ $250-350M annualized at current ETH). Lee = aligned principal-strategist (chairman + Fundstrat thesis owner). Accretion flywheel (issue at premium → buy ETH → ETH-per-share rises) — currently paused given mNAV at/near 1x; flywheel resumption is itself an up-leg.

Sizing: Tier 2 (up to 10% portfolio) until Council Phase 1 review clears Tier 3 (up to 15%).

Adds — green light if any:
• ETH breaks $2,000 without thesis-break news → P-WAKE-UP single-name capitulation
• BMNR mNAV discount widens >20% on Senate Banking markup news → anomaly
• Third independent stream confirms thesis materially

Adds — yellow light:
• BMNR holds $20-22 base while ETH consolidates above $2,200
• CLARITY Banking markup happens cleanly (week of May 11 or later)

Invalidation (any one triggers trim/exit review, NOT auto-exit):

2. RWA tokenization migrates off ETH at scale (>30% TVL share lost to competitor chain)
3. CLARITY Act dies in this Congress AND no successor bill in 2027 — regulatory limbo extends materially
4. Tom Lee departs as Chairman OR sells material personal stake (>20% of his BMNR holdings)
5. BMNR mNAV premium >2x sustained → speculative blowoff, mechanical trim
6. Material dilution at discount-to-mNAV → management discipline lost (value-destructive issuance)

• Trim only on thesis break, not on price
• Adds during weakness explicitly encouraged
• Hold through earnings cycles minimum

(2) Standard Chartered (Geoff Kendrick, independent of Fundstrat): ETH $7,500 by EOY 2026, $30K by 2029, $40K by 2030 — directionally aligned near-term, more conservative long-term. (3) Policy momentum: CLARITY Act stablecoin yield compromise released 5/1-5/2/26 (Tillis/Alsobrooks); Polymarket signed-into-law-2026 odds repriced ~47%→70%. Lee = aligned principal-strategist (chairman + Fundstrat thesis owner). Accretion flywheel (issue at premium → buy ETH → ETH-per-share rises) — currently paused given mNAV at/near 1x; flywheel resumption is itself an up-leg.

text.content:
— Cron reviews Active Rationales DB: auto-expires past Valid Until, surfaces nearing-expiry, re-prices against current market for stale ones.

=== ARCHITECTURE TEMPLATE ===

Follows Workflow B pattern (deployed Sess #33):
- Cron trigger
- Pull context from Notion DBs
- Pull market data from FMP
- Send to Claude API with structured prompt
- Parse JSON judgment
- Write to designated Notion page
- Pushover only if urgent action implied
- Heartbeat row

=== COST ===

Claude Sonnet 4.5 API ~$0.03-0.10 per deep evaluation. With 20x Max plan and ~30 evaluations/week = ~$1-3/week API cost.

=== DEFERRED REASON ===

Workflow A + B just deployed; first operational data needed before adding more autonomous workflows. After 1-2 weeks of operating data on A + B, evaluate whether async deep research adds value beyond what sprint + update provides.

=== TRIGGER FOR REVIVAL ===

After Workflow B has processed 20+ Fundstrat emails AND operator has manually done 1-2 trigger-value reviews and felt the friction, schedule a design session to scope Workflow D (async research orchestrator).

text.content:
(i.e. creating a row in a database)

During Workflow B (Fundstrat Triage) build Sess #33, used 'Create Page' against the Fundstrat Triage Log database's data-source ID. Errored with 'Could not find page with ID' — misleading error message that suggested an OAuth scope problem when the real issue was wrong action selected.

Wasted ~30 min debugging OAuth permissions, reconnecting Notion, etc. Operator (correctly) identified the action mismatch and noted: 'i used create page notion function in pipedream instead of the create page from data source function.'

=== RULE ===

For future Pipedream Notion writes:
• If target is a DATABASE ROW → 'Create Page from Data Source' (parent = data_source_id)
• If target is a SUBPAGE under a page → 'Create Page' (parent = page_id)
• Default to 'Create Page from Data Source' since most Investing 2026 writes target databases (Trade Journal, Decisions Log, Active Rationales, Triage Log, etc.)

If operator has API/tool access to Notion: bypass entirely with raw axios POST to

=== SOURCE ===

Council Phase 1 executed in parallel chat May 2, 2026: GPT-5.5 Thinking + Claude Opus 4.7 Thinking + Nemotron 3 Super. Different-mechanism convergence achieved per P-CONVERGE — the kind of signal governance is supposed to capture.

=== TWO OPERATOR-STATED FRICTIONS ===

(1) Notion read/writes in heavy investing sessions take materially long. Validated bottleneck: append-only Session State growth + multi-DB rehydration on sprint/update consumes tool-call budget. P-CONFIG-CACHE (Sess #26) already documents 30-90s reads from artifact context for >5KB JSON.

(2) Operator instinct toward 'complicated system of connections' for cross-cutting insight generation. NOT addressed by this Council — flagged for separate evaluation. Initial pushback stands: P-SIMPLICITY likely vetoes connection-density additions; if pursued, the right answer is a 'patterns' command on existing data, not new structure.

=== COUNCIL UNANIMOUS FINDINGS (high confidence) ===

1. Kill full Session State rehydration — single biggest tool-call sink, 5-15 calls saved per session.

2. Cap stale-briefing fallback — eliminate or hard-cap the 14+ FMP quote cascade when Pipedream briefing is stale.

3. Ban routine update_content — DB-row appends as default write pattern (matches operational memory: update_content is fragile re: whitespace/markdown matching).

4. Cache UUID registry in

=== ALIGNMENT NOTE FOR V9.1 ===

Operational Reference's write-destinations table currently lists Session State as a write target. v9.1's Step 1 patch also references Session State reads. Both are subject to v9.x Notion-protocol revision. Do not optimize against current pattern in the meantime.

=== ONE COUNCIL FINDING TO PUSH BACK ON LATER ===

Finding #6 ('skip low-frequency DBs in routine sprints — FDF, Scan Log, Snapshots, Prompt Library on-demand only') is too coarse. FDF DB just gained meaningful active flags this session (FDF #10 superseded, P-AUTOMATE-DEFAULT). Skipping FDF reads in routine sprints would have hidden FDF #10 from operator view today. Right rule is probably 'skip FDF if no Active Severity≥High flags', not 'skip on-demand only'. Worth pushing back on Council in the v9.x design pass that incorporates this work.

=== TIMING ===

Whether this Council package becomes v9.2, v9.3, or its own dedicated workstream is OPEN. Most v9.x updates are expected to be quick iterative improvements due to time limitations; this Council package is bigger and may warrant its own focused pass rather than competing for v9.2 with smaller fixes.

text.content:
=== THE RULE ===

Claude AUTOMATES AND EXECUTES by default. The bar for asking the operator to do something Claude could do is HIGH: input must be decision-relevant, preference-relevant, or risk-relevant.

=== AUTOMATE BY DEFAULT (do these without asking) ===

• Creating Notion DB rows from clear specifications
• Populating obvious config values
• Writing standard markdown content (build specs, runbooks, technical docs)
• Setting up infrastructure (pages, databases, properties) when schema is specified
• Filling in templated entries (DL entries, FDF entries) where the content is mechanical
• Renaming, restructuring, organizing existing content per operator direction
• Calculating, aggregating, transforming data (sleeve allocations, trigger distances, etc.)
• Applying naming conventions, formatting, schema migrations
• Writing seed data for DBs Claude has just created

=== DEFER ONLY FOR (these warrant asking) ===

1. POSITION-LEVEL TRADING DECISIONS — buy/sell/trim/add specific tickers at specific prices/sizes. P-NO-FILL-NO-FACT and v9 trade-recommendation discipline govern. Asking is correct.

2. PREFERENCE/STYLE CHOICES with no obvious right answer — 'do you want this in alphabetical order or by size?' / 'should we use red or blue for the highest priority flag?' Only ask if the choice meaningfully affects how operator uses the output.

3. RISK-RELEVANT ACTIONS — anything that would cost real money, send actual external messages (email, Slack, push notifications) to non-self destinations, modify shared/external systems beyond Notion+Pipedream, or be hard to undo.

4. SCOPE BOUNDARIES UNCLEAR — when operator's request could mean two materially different things and the wrong interpretation wastes significant effort.

5. FRAMEWORK PRINCIPLE CHANGES — additions to CI principles, deletions of existing principles, or major behavioral shifts. These are operator-authored.

=== WHAT THIS REPLACES ===

FDF #10 (Sess #33 May 1) flagged 'position-level writes during system-improvement sessions' as a drift mode. The FDF was over-corrected. The actual problem was specifically (1) above — pre-deciding a GEV trim framework. Mechanical writes (DB row population, config setup, schema creation) are NOT the same category and were incorrectly grouped together.

=== EXAMPLES (concrete) ===

GOOD AUTOMATIONS (Claude executes without asking):
• Operator creates a DB and asks Claude what rows to seed → Claude writes the rows directly
• Operator says 'add an Index option to that select column' → Claude does it (or tells operator the limit and does as much as possible)
• Operator asks for a build spec → Claude writes the full spec including all real IDs, page links, code blocks
• Operator asks Claude to log a decision → Claude writes the DL entry with full content
• Sleeve target rebalancing math, factor correlation calculations, drift computations → Claude computes and presents

GOOD DEFERRALS (Claude asks):
• 'Should I trim MU?' → Claude asks operator to confirm size/price (P-NO-FILL-NO-FACT)
• 'Convert this watchlist into Active Rationales?' → Claude asks because the rationale fields require operator-original thesis content
• 'Apply the new principle retroactively to last quarter's decisions?' → Claude asks because the scope is ambiguous and rework is real

=== RELATIONSHIP TO P-SIMPLICITY ===

P-SIMPLICITY says simplicity beats sophistication when they conflict; P-AUTOMATE-DEFAULT operationalizes that by removing friction from mechanical work. Asking 'do you want me to populate this DB?' fails the P-SIMPLICITY usability test — the operator cannot benefit from the answer; they just want the work done.

=== CANDIDATE FOR CI PROMOTION ===

If this rule proves stable across 5+ sessions without recurrence of (a)-style violations, propose for CI v9.x as a Process-cluster principle: P-AUTOMATE-DEFAULT.

text.content:
=== PREFERENCE 1: INLINE-WITH-NOTION-COMPANION DELIVERY FOR COMPLEX BUILDS ===

When delivering multi-step technical build instructions (Pipedream workflows, code-step builds, multi-phase setups, anything with a checklist of operator actions), use the dual-delivery pattern:

(a) Full content rendered INLINE in chat for follow-along
(b) Same content as a Notion companion page with checkboxes, copy-paste-ready code blocks, all real page IDs pre-filled

Both contain the same content; either is sufficient on its own. Operator chooses which surface to follow.

Applies to: build specs, runbooks, setup guides, multi-phase technical procedures.

Does NOT apply to: routine sprint output (single-turn delivery), single-step operator actions, conversational discussion.

Validation example: Sess #33 build spec for B1 Pipedream Workflow A. Operator confirmed format works well.

=== PREFERENCE 2: NEVER ASSUME OPERATOR-SIDE SYSTEM CHANGES ARE IMPLEMENTED ===

When Claude hands operator a system change to execute (CI patch, Pipedream workflow deployment, Notion DB schema change, browser extension install, automation setup, anything that requires operator hands), Claude does NOT assume in subsequent sessions that the change is live.

Default status: PROPOSED until operator confirms execution.

Examples of incorrect inference patterns Claude must avoid:
• 'Now that CI v9.1 is pasted...' → wrong; CI v9.1 is patched only when operator confirms paste
• 'With Workflow A live, sprint Step 1 reads briefing-first...' → wrong; Workflow A is built only when operator confirms a successful cron tick wrote to production page
• 'Pushover is wired so Newton Flash alerts will fire...' → wrong; Pushover is wired only when operator confirms a test push received

Correct inference pattern: 'Once CI v9.1 is pasted (operator action remaining), sprint Step 1 will read briefing-first.' Tense matters: future-conditional, not present-perfect.

This is structurally identical to P-NO-FILL-NO-FACT for trades — plans aren't reality until confirmed. The principle generalizes: framework state is operator-confirmed only.

Forcing function: at the start of each session, verify status of any system change handed off in prior sessions before referencing it. Look for explicit operator confirmation in chat history or DB writes (e.g., a heartbeat row would confirm Workflow A is live; a Decisions Log entry titled 'CI v9.1 Patch — Pipedream Live' with the operator-pasted text would confirm CI v9.1).

Proposed promotion path: if this pattern recurs reliably, propose principle P-OPERATOR-CONFIRMS for v9.x or v10. Sister principle to P-NO-FILL-NO-FACT but for system state instead of trade state.

=== RELATIONSHIP TO EXISTING PRINCIPLES ===

Both preferences are P-SIMPLICITY-aligned (operator-friendly, reduces re-explanation burden) and reinforce P-NAMING-CLARITY (explicit status flags reduce ambiguity).

Preference 2 specifically resolves a subtle drift mode: Claude treating its own outputs as state changes. The output is a proposal; the state change requires operator action. Conflating them is the same category of error as P-NO-FILL-NO-FACT addresses for trades.

• 'Pushover is wired so Newton Flash alerts will fire...' → wrong; Pushover is wired only when operator confirms a test push received

Correct inference pattern: 'Once CI v9.1 is pasted (operator action remaining), sprint Step 1 will read briefing-first.' Tense matters: future-conditional, not present-perfect.

This is structurally identical to P-NO-FILL-NO-FACT for trades — plans aren't reality until confirmed. The principle generalizes: framework state is operator-confirmed only.

Forcing function: at the start of each session, verify status of any system change handed off in prior sessions before referencing it. Look for explicit operator confirmation in chat history or DB writes (e.g., a heartbeat row would confirm Workflow A is live; a Decisions Log entry titled 'CI v9.1 Patch — Pipedream Live' with the operator-pasted text would confirm CI v9.1).

Proposed promotion path: if this pattern recurs reliably, propose principle P-OPERATOR-CONFIRMS for v9.x or v10. Sister principle to P-NO-FILL-NO-FACT but for system state instead of trade state.

=== RELATIONSHIP TO EXISTING PRINCIPLES ===

Both preferences are P-SIMPLICITY-aligned (operator-friendly, reduces re-explanation burden) and reinforce P-NAMING-CLARITY (explicit status flags reduce ambiguity).

Preference 2 specifically resolves a subtle drift mode: Claude treating its own outputs as state changes. The output is a proposal; the state change requires operator action. Conflating them is the same category of error as P-NO-FILL-NO-FACT addresses for trades.

Addresses documented April execution-realism failure (Plan A Reallocation, ~$75K target → ~$20K executed; framework treated unexecuted portion as live for 2 sessions). Delta-only entry per CI standing rule (patch-only is default; full CI text backup queued separately as Tasks DB action item per major-bump rule, never blocking deploy).

=== NEW PRINCIPLES ===

• P-NO-FILL-NO-FACT (only confirmed fills update portfolio reality) — Plans, recommendations, and queued limit orders are NOT positions. Portfolio state math, sleeve allocations, concentration calculations all reference confirmed fills only. Mandatory end-of-trading-session fill confirmation prompt: 'Mark each: filled / partial fill (note shares) / not placed / cancelled.' Subsequent sessions cannot inherit 'executed' status by inference. Forcing function: Session State narrative may not contain phrase 'we executed' without adjacent [FILL CONFIRMED date] tag tied to an operator response. Violations are FDF-grade Severity High/Workflow.

• P-RATIONALE-PERSIST (the durable asset is the WHY, not the trade list) — When a high-conviction trade rec isn't placed in-session, framework remembers the RATIONALE (thesis + source + conviction tier + timing context + price target), not a 'pending trade' record. text.content:
only — not on quick questions. Auto-expiry past Valid Until (default Recommended Date + 14 days). No re-derivation without re-pricing. Skip-reason taxonomy feeds learning loop.

• P-SIMPLICITY (simplicity beats sophistication when they conflict) — A framework component the operator won't use has zero value, regardless of analytical merit. Every new principle/command/tracker/workflow step must pass: (1) 20-min usability test, (2) forcing-function check, (3) bypass cost. Meta-rule that gates all other framework additions.

=== REVISED PRINCIPLES ===

• P-CONVICTION-EXECUTION — Buildout deployment math RETIRED. Multi-week tranching language ('deploy 40-60% within 10 trading days', 'build to X over 4 weeks') removed. v9 deployment rule: trade NOW at full target size, OR set named trigger price valid for ≤14 days, OR skip. Override-permission concept survives; staged-deployment mechanism dies. Convergence without pre-logged DL + Active Trade Rationales entry CANNOT invoke this principle.

• P-DECIDE-EXECUTE — Three v9 mandates added: (a) Single-account-first execution: get exposure where it's easiest in current session; cross-account funding sequencing (sells in Account A fund buys in Account B) BANNED by default. (b) Sell-first funding mandate: every BUY rec names the funding sell explicitly OR confirms cash availability. (c) No tight limits as default: marketable limits (last × 1.005 buys / × 0.995 sells) for liquid names in regular hours. Tight limits at -1% to -2% from reference RETIRED as default.

=== STANDING RULE CHANGES ===

• Write boundary updated: portfolio/ticker rationales now route to 🧠 Active Trade Rationales DB (NOT Session State Open Pending Actions, which is retired). Meta/system tasks still route to ✅ Tasks DB with Domain=Investing.

• NUCLEAR CLEANUP: All existing Session State Open Pending Actions wiped. Anything still relevant must be explicitly re-recommended at current prices into Active Trade Rationales DB.

=== NEW COMMAND ===

text.content:
redefined — heavyweight quarterly session for strategic reviews, P-REEVAL execution, framework changes spanning >1 sleeve. Should run ~4x/year, not weekly.

=== NEW DB ===

• 🧠 Active Trade Rationales (a76caa96-5795-4ec9-b68e-3a64bda0b29b) — replaces Session State Open Pending Actions for trade-rationale persistence. Schema verified this session: ticker/theme, action, recommended date, valid-until date, rationale, target price, reference price, source, lane, status, skip reason, account hint, approx size.

=== NEW FORMAT ===

• Two-tier trade recommendation — top line (action + size + price + 1-sentence why) always visible; expandable detail section holds 11+ data points from operator preferences (52w high/low+dates, ATH+date, consensus PT, analyst actions, PE/fwd PE/mkt cap/last-Q rev, catalyst proximity, insider activity 90d, Trump-admin tie, factor-correlation, target purchase price, trailing stop %, 30-day test answer). For multi-trade recs, render as summary table.

=== EXPLICITLY KILLED ===

• Pending Execution Tracker (Council split — GPT yes / Gemini no / Nemotron yes-with-conditions; operator simplicity rule killed it).
• Conviction Buildout Tracker (operator killed buildout concept entirely).
• Scale-in bracket orders 30/30/40 (complexity, traps cash).
• Hard 3-trade-per-session cap (replaced with ranked priority guideline; top 3 marked 'do now', operator can execute more if motivated).

=== DEFERRED ===

• Options playbook → dedicated Sess #34 design session per operator request. Existing standing rule (no options in Speed Lane, options gated by buy-and-hold posture) intact.

=== COMPANION FILE STATUS ===

• Recommendation_Method_-

text.content:
Major version bump v8.3 → v9.0 same day. Driven by Council Phase 1 (GPT-5.5 Thinking + Gemini 3.1 Pro Thinking + Nemotron 3 Super) plus operator simplicity-first directive. Addresses documented April execution-realism failure (Plan A Reallocation $75K target → $20K executed; framework treated unexecuted portion as live for 2 sessions).

=== NEW PRINCIPLES ===

1. P-NO-FILL-NO-FACT (only confirmed fills update portfolio reality) — plans/recommendations/queued limits are NOT positions. Portfolio state math references confirmed fills only. Mandatory closing prompt on every trading-session response: 'Mark each: filled / partial / not placed / cancelled.' Subsequent sessions cannot inherit 'executed' status by inference. Forcing function: Session State narrative may not contain 'we executed' without adjacent [FILL CONFIRMED date] tag tied to operator response. Violations are FDF-grade Severity High/Workflow.

2. P-RATIONALE-PERSIST (the durable asset is the WHY, not the trade list) — unfilled high-conviction recs are stored as RATIONALES (thesis + source + conviction + timing + target) in new 🧠 Active Trade Rationales DB (a76caa96-5795-4ec9-b68e-3a64bda0b29b). Auto-load: sprint and update commands ONLY (NOT quick questions). Auto-expire: Status=Active rows past Valid Until date auto-expired at next sprint/update. Default Valid Until = Recommended Date + 14 days. Skip-reason taxonomy when rationales archive without execution: time / cash / spread / conviction dropped / price moved away / replaced by better / funding sequencing failed.

3. P-SIMPLICITY (simplicity beats sophistication when they conflict) — META-RULE governing all framework additions. Every new principle/command/tracker/workflow step must pass: (a) 20-min usability test, (b) forcing-function check, (c) bypass-cost test. Apply BEFORE adding components, not as post-hoc rationalization for skipping ones already added.

=== REVISED PRINCIPLES ===

P-CONVICTION-EXECUTION — buildout deployment math RETIRED. Multi-week tranching language removed. v9 deployment rule: trade NOW at full target size, OR set named trigger price valid ≤14 days, OR skip. The 'deploy 40-60% within 10 trading days' staged-deployment mechanism dies; the override-permission concept survives. Pre-logged DL + Active Rationales entry still required.

P-DECIDE-EXECUTE — added (a) single-account-first execution mandate (cross-account funding sequencing banned by default; operator must explicitly request multi-account orchestration), (b) sell-first funding mandate (every BUY rec names funding sell explicitly OR confirms cash availability), (c) tight limits at -1% to -2% from reference price RETIRED as default (use marketable limits last × 1.005 for buys / × 0.995 for sells; tight limits only with specific named price target + rationale + ≤14 days validity).

=== NEW COMMAND ===

sprint — 5-step lightweight weekly workflow (20-30 min, ≤30 tool calls, opens with strategic 'as-if-all-cash' view, top-3 ranked actions). Replaces routine update calls. update redefined as quarterly/heavyweight (~4x/year) for P-REEVAL, post-major-event recalibration, framework changes spanning >1 sleeve.

=== NEW INFRASTRUCTURE ===

🧠 Active Trade Rationales DB (a76caa96-5795-4ec9-b68e-3a64bda0b29b) replaces Session State Open Pending Actions for trade-rationale persistence. Schema: Ticker/Theme · Action (BUY/ADD/TRIM/SELL/HOLD/BUILD-UP) · Lane · Source · Approx Size · Target Price · Reference Price · Account Hint · Recommended Date · Valid Until · Status (Active/Executed/Expired/Cancelled/Renewed) · Skip Reason · Rationale (free text).

Two-tier trade rec format made mandatory — top line always visible (action + size + price + 1-sentence why), detail expansion (collapsed) holds the 11+ operator-preference data points. Multi-trade recs render as summary tables.

=== NUCLEAR CLEANUP ===

All existing Session State Open Pending Actions wiped per v9 directive. Anything still relevant must be explicitly re-recommended at current prices into Active Trade Rationales.

=== EXPLICITLY KILLED ===

Pending Execution Tracker (Council split, operator simplicity rule). Conviction Buildout Tracker (operator killed buildout concept). Scale-in bracket orders (complexity). Hard 3-trade cap (replaced with ranked priority guideline).

=== DEFERRED ===

Options playbook — dedicated Sess #34 design session per operator request. No options framework changes in v9; existing standing rules intact (no options in Speed Lane, options gated by buy-and-hold).

=== COMPANION FILES ===

Recommendation_Method_-

text.content:
Sess #33 review session: operator asked Claude to sweep old Notion logs + chat memory for unimplemented ideas, prioritize by ease-of-use, address Fundstrat Direct real-time integration, and design push-notification architecture. Fundstrat clarification (operator): flash insights push is APP-ONLY (iOS Fundstrat app), end-of-day summary emails are the email channel. Real-time intra-day flash capture is therefore not fully autonomous — best paths are iOS Shortcut share-sheet (~3s tap) or manual chat-paste.

=== TIER A — QUICK WINS (≤1hr each, high ease-of-use payoff) ===

scrape works. Build into Pipedream weekly cron once B1 operational.

=== PUSH NOTIFICATION DESIGN ===

Channels: iOS Apple Reminders (already integrated), Pushover (~$5 one-time, Pipedream native), Email, Notion mobile push.

Notification matrix:
- Fundstrat Flash with held/watchlist ticker → Pushover urgent + Notion row
- Fundstrat daily Newton/Tom Lee, no held tickers → Notion row only
- Exit trigger proximity (held position within 5% of trigger) → Pushover urgent
- Insider Form 4 cluster on held name → Pushover medium
- Senate trade on held name → Email
- Catalyst day T-1 (e.g., LEU Q1 May 6) → Apple Reminder 9am day-of
- MU stop verification → Apple Reminder one-off + recurring until done
- Speed Lane 48h post-hoc review due → Apple Reminder
- Briefing staleness >24h during market hours → Notion row only

Filtering rule: ticker is in held positions OR active watchlist OR has Exit Trigger row, AND (source = Fundstrat Flash OR Form 4 cluster ≥3 insiders 30d OR price within 5% of active trigger). Everything else = silent Notion row.

=== BUILD SEQUENCE (operator-confirmed Sess #33) ===

1. B1 FIRST (Pipedream pre-flight briefing) — operator confirmed top priority
2. Pushover account + Pipedream Pushover step (~15 min)
3. B2 layered onto same Pipedream stack (~30-90 min depending on Fundstrat email body depth)
4. Wire 3 highest-value notification triggers (Fundstrat Flash, exit-trigger proximity, MU stop reminder)
5. A1

text.content: All — framework architecture change

rich_text.plain_text: All — framework architecture change

text.content:
Council-driven redesign (Phase 1: GPT-5.5 Thinking + Gemini 3.1 Pro Thinking + Nemotron 3 Super; convergent diagnosis on 4 of 5 points). Replaces v8.3 with v9 to address documented failure mode: framework conflated 'planned' with 'executed', generated multi-trade plans operationally incompatible with 20-30 min trading windows + ~1.3% cash, and corrupted subsequent-session context with assumed-executed plans. Apr 28-30 Reallocation Plan = canonical example: 10-trade plan, 1 fill (~$20K of ~$75K target), framework treated as executed for two sessions before operator correction. Estimated upside missed 8-15% on unexecuted portion.

KEY CHANGES (delta from v8.3):

1. NEW PRINCIPLE P-NO-FILL-NO-FACT (always-on): Only confirmed fills update portfolio state. Plans never enter exposure math. End of every trading-session response, explicit fill confirmation per trade required before next-session context inherits anything.

2. NEW PRINCIPLE P-RATIONALE-PERSIST (always-on): The durable asset of a trade recommendation is the WHY (thesis + source + conviction + timing rationale), not the trade list. Stored in 🧠 Active Trade Rationales DB (a76caa96-5795-4ec9-b68e-3a64bda0b29b) with 14-day default auto-expiry. Rationales auto-load only on

text.content:
becomes the heavyweight quarterly session.

5. UPDATED P-DECIDE-EXECUTE: now mandates single-account-first execution + sell-first funding. Cross-account dependencies banned without explicit operator request.

6. UPDATED Trade Recommendation Format: TWO-TIER. Top line = action + size + price + 1-sentence why. Detail expansion = the 11+ data points operator preferences specify (rationale, insider buys, analyst targets, target buy price, trailing stop, PE, fwd PE, revenue, market cap, ATH, ATL, current price). Honors operator's 'always include' preferences without burying every rec in walls of text.

7. NUCLEAR CLEANUP: All existing 'Open Pending Actions' from prior sessions wiped. Anything still relevant must be explicitly re-recommended at current prices into Active Trade Rationales DB.

8. KILLED: Pending Execution Tracker concept (Council split, simplicity rule applied). Conviction Buildout Tracker (operator killed buildout concept). Scale-in bracket orders (too complex). Hard 3-trade cap (replaced with ranked priority guideline).

GOVERNING RULE: Simplicity-first. Every framework addition must pass usability test: 'will operator actually do this in a 20-min window without re-reading instructions?' If no, reject even if logically sound.

Full v9 CI text deferred to action item per major-bump backup rule (canonical version = .md file in project knowledge + chat transcript). Companion .md files: Custom_Instructions_

text.content:
Operator request: deploy ~$35K cash today unless dip expected next week. Newton Apr 30 daily explicitly forecasts mid-May MAG7 dips: AMZN to $200-190, GOOGL to $324/$314, MSFT to $406-395. Therefore split: deploy ~$15-20K today on names where Newton calls bottom IS in (GDX) or whose dip-zones aren't in play (GS, partial MSFT, AAPL post-breakout). Reserve ~$15-20K for mid-May MAG7 dips. 

DEPLOY TODAY:
(1) GDX NEW position $5-7K @ ~$87.33 — Newton Apr 30: 'pullback in Gold Mining ETFs near attractive trendline support; DeMark TD Buy Setups in place; selloff should be nearly complete.' Sleeve = NEW Precious Metals. Initial target $104. Trailing stop 8-10%. Zero current GDX exposure across portfolio.
(2) GS add $3-5K to existing $7.4K position. Fundstrat Apr 30 Top 5 Core LC pick @ 14.2x P/E Yr+1; Newton Apr 30 Financials broadening leadership thesis. Current $926.37; 52w high $984.70.
(3) MSFT partial add $3-5K. Newton Apr 30: 'breakout above multi-month downtrend; targets $454 then $474; dip-zone $406-$395 buyable.' Currently $415.91 (just above dip-zone). Already underwater -11.7% on existing position; partial here, partial on dip-zone retest.
(4) AAPL NEW $3-5K. Newton Apr 30 setup played out — earnings beat overnight, +4.92% to $284.70 today, decisively cleared $275 breakout level. 52w high $288.62 (1.4% away). Zero current AAPL exposure across portfolio (gap flagged for review). Smaller initial size given proximity to ATH.

HOLD CASH for mid-May DIPS:
(1) AMZN dip to $200-$190 (currently $269.50) — Newton's explicit pullback zone for better entry
(2) GOOGL dip to $324/$314 (currently $385.08) — broken out but RSI 80 daily; Newton calls dips before further extension
(3) MSFT dip to $406-$395 — second tranche

NOT DEPLOYING TODAY:
- IGV (Tom Lee May 1 add) — already 30%+ effective software exposure via MAGS 9.04%, GRNY 9.45%, plus direct MSFT/GOOGL/AMZN/AVGO. Adding IGV not additive.
- LEU additional — already at-or-above Tier 2 P-CONVICTION-TIER ceiling (10.33% on SKB side, 5.51% portfolio). Hold.
- BMNR — operator override flagged, sleeve at limits.

LANE ASSIGNMENT: All four deployment names assigned to Buy-and-Hold Core (P-BUYHOLD 30-day test passes). Speed Lane explicitly NOT triggered — per CI v8.3, Speed Lane is DEFENSIVE only (concentration breach / exit trigger / mandatory rebalance); none active today. No Generational Tier 2 promotions today (no new 6-filter qualifiers from current research).

FRESH INFRASTRUCTURE STATE: April 23 PDF re-upload blocker resolved this session — all 7 broker PDFs ingested via portfolio-pdf-extractor, all validated PASS (sum positions+cash matches reported total within $1 across every file). Portfolio total: $1,809,866. Cash: $37,579 (parents $20,917 + SKB $10,702 main + $5,963 brokerage cash). Sleeve allocation rebuilt bottom-up; PCRA Trust map now feasible.

CONSENSUS-PT pending action: Pull consensus PTs for NVDA, LEU, OKE, BWXT, UUUU, MP next session for P-CONSENSUS-GAP eligibility check (>2% portfolio AND sustained >10% above consensus PT for 30 days = paired-condition trim rule required).

MU below 2% portfolio threshold (1.91%) — no longer P-CONSENSUS-GAP eligible by sizing alone. FDF candidate: does at-threshold drift still warrant monitoring? Recurrence count 1.

Sources cited THIS session via project_knowledge_search: Custom_Instructions_v8_

text.content:
Sess #33 had three consecutive Notion timeouts on a small Session State priority-stack edit. This is the third session in three weeks with a Session State write failure (Sess #19 first flagged the budget killer pattern, Sess #32 made it explicit, Sess #33 made it modal). The framework's continuity guarantee — that operator context, ranked priorities, and active thesis pointers survive session boundaries — is silently corrupting whenever these writes fail.

Fix A spec proposes splitting Session State into:
1. Front page (existing page ID, slimmed to <8K chars): header metadata + NEXT SESSION block + Open Pending Actions + Acknowledged Flags only.
2. 📚 Session Log Archive (new): all historical session blocks, append-only, never read at session start.
3. 🌉 Recent History Capsule (new, key innovation): rotating buffer of last 3-5 sessions collapsed to 5-10 lines each, read at Step 1.5 of update workflow. Bridges the 'recent process' gap between formal Decisions Log entries and current-state Session State front page.
4. 🧠 Active Thesis Frameworks (already exists): per-position thesis docs, on-demand reads only.

Migration phased: Phase 1 (Claude-only, additive, ~15 min) creates new pages and populates them; Phase 2 (operator-present, the risky write, ~10 min) replaces Session State front-page content via full replace_content; Phase 3 (validation runs over 1 session); Phase 4 (CI v8.3 patch, ~5 min) updates Step 1 and Step 8 of update workflow.

Validation criteria: 5 consecutive surgical edits to slimmed Session State complete in <3 sec each across 5 different sessions, zero timeouts. Combined Step 1 fetch <12K chars total.

Full spec at 🛠️ Fix A — Session State Decomposition Spec (page 353c50314bb681fe9bd6ea867d793f66, child of Session State).

Operator decision points before execution: (1) naming for Archive and Capsule pages — lockable in Phase 1 prep, (2) Capsule depth (recommended 3-5 sessions), (3) migration session timing — best in a low-stakes session, NOT one with concurrent capital deployment or earnings processing. Recommended Sess #34+ if Sess #33 absorbs GDX/RSP/MSFT/KRE/TLN deployment.

This decision documents that the spec is execution-ready. Actual execution requires operator green-light on the three decision points and appropriate session timing.

text.content:
Operator flagged that today's Fundstrat digest (Apr 30 PM Newton + Apr 29 Tom Lee + flashes throughout Apr 30 trading day) was not in framework state when planning, and asked for an aggressive action plan considering missed-opportunity context. Operator noted abundant losses available = tax-loss harvesting on the table.

NEW signals from Apr 30 digest not previously in framework:
1. Newton Apr 30 7:31 PM — GDX (gold miners ETF) buyable setup with DeMark TD Buy Setups in place, intermediate uptrend intact, target $104 then year-highs. NEW signal.
2. Newton Apr 30 7:31 PM — broadening leadership trade: RSP/IWM/DJIA expected to follow SPX/QQQ to new ATHs in next 3-5 trading days. 7 sectors broadening.
3. Newton Apr 29 PM — MSFT in $395-406 buy zone, targets $454 then $474. Currently $408.60 AH, at top of buy zone.
4. Tom Lee Apr 29 — favored sectors include MAG7+Crypto, Industrials, Financials (XLF + KRE), Small-caps, Energy/Materials.
5. Newton Apr 30 12:46 PM flash — GOOGL is BEST of MAG7 technically; first target $377 hit, path to $402.
6. Newton Apr 30 12:46 PM flash — AMZN: wait for $200-190 dip into mid-May (DEFER, not chase).
7. Apr 22 Top 5 / Bottom 5 — already in framework but Bottom 5 contains GLXY (which is in GRNJ at 1.86%) — conflict flag, look-through impact only.

Ranked plan (Tier 1 fills real gaps + fresh setup, Tier 2 high-conviction single-name + sleeve fit, Tier 3 smaller bets):
Tier 1: GDX (closes non-correlated factor gap from 0.5% toward 5% target, fresh DeMark setup), RSP (broadening trade + fills diversified core lane).
Tier 2: MSFT (only direct MAG7 gap, 65 Buy/16 Hold/0 Sell, consensus PT $551.75 = +35% upside, in Newton buy zone), KRE (Tom Lee favored, complements XLF/JPM/GS Financials buildout).
Tier 3: TLN (Tom Lee SMID Top 5 Utilities, 12 Buy/0 Hold consensus PT $472 = +27% upside, different mechanism from existing nuclear basket).

Defer: AAPL (broke $275 Newton breakout but already extended at $276.41 AH — set $268-272 limit, don't chase), AMZN (Newton said wait for dip), META (lower conviction per Newton's own ranking), AMD/ANET (extended; wait for watchlist triggers per existing framework), CRS/IESC/FIX/STRL (redundant with GRNJ holdings unless conviction-driven).

Funding: $80K cash covers Tier 1 + most Tier 2. Tier 3 + remaining funded via tax-loss harvest of taxable accounts (Parents Joint, SKB Fid TOD, SKB Schwab, RH Indiv, HSA = ~$928K taxable). Specific harvest candidates already on radar: ASTS (decay candidate per Sess #31), POET (already partially exited), CIBR remaining 22sh in PCRA (retirement, no tax benefit but ETF-only reallocation). Don't sell: LEU (mechanism stops INTACT, single-entry binding, May 6 Q1 in 6 days), BMNR (operator override on thesis Apr 30), Plan A names just bought (wash sale risk), MAG7/NVDA/AVGO/MU (profitable + structural).

P-DECIDE-EXECUTE action read — GDX: $88.88 AH, target ≤$89, ~$22K = 28% of cash. RSP: ~$202 AH, target ≤$204, ~$18K = 23%. MSFT: $408.60 AH, staged ≤$408 / ≤$400 / ≤$395, ~$25K = 31%. KRE: $70.14 AH, target ≤$70, ~$12K (sell-funded preferred). TLN: $371.78 AH, target ≤$370, ~$7K (sell-funded). Total ~$84K vs $80K cash — either skip TLN until harvest funds it, or right-size to $75K.

Commitment subset if operator wants only 2-3 trades: GDX + RSP + MSFT = ~$60K. Captures everything genuinely new from today's digest.

Operator will commit (full / subset / specific names) in next session. This entry documents the recommendation; execution is operator-side.

text.content:
Operator inspecting the v8.2 dashboard flagged the Semis/Data-Center sleeve actual_pct of 11.5% as suspect (operator's stated intent: most overweight in semis). Path A executed (fix measurement first, no capital deployment). 

Findings:
1. Sleeve Targets DB Semis/Data-Center/Optical row (last reviewed 2026-04-20) lists Core ETFs = SMH, SOXX and Single-Name Examples = NVDA, ASML, MU, AVGO, LITE, CLS, POET. Target 18%. SMH is unambiguously a member of this sleeve.
2. Dashboard JSON build dropped SMH from the sleeve and ignored ETF look-through entirely.
3. Bottom-up corrected math (look-through inclusive):
   - Direct: SMH $156,205 + NVDA $125,247 + MU $33,700 + AVGO $28,354 = $343,506 = 19.21%
   - GRNY look-through (AMD, KLAC, AVGO, NVDA): ~$19,000 = 1.06%
   - GRNJ look-through (LITE, ON, FN, SANM, LSCC): ~$10,000 = 0.56%
   - MAGS look-through (NVDA component ~14% weight): ~$22,700 = 1.27%
   - Total: ~$395,200 = ~22.1% of $1.788M
4. Conclusion: ~22.1% actual vs 18% target = ~4pp OVERWEIGHT. Operator's perceived underweight was a 10pp measurement error.

Decision: NO BUY. The chase instinct (perceived gap + price advance) fired against an artifact. Adding semis here would be P-ALPHA violation (gap-filling not a buy thesis) plus chasing names at/near ATH (NVDA, AVGO, SMH) or already extended above consensus (MU +13.7% above PT).

Secondary decision: Path B (proposed in same chat turn) of conditional AMD <$320 / ANET <$160 entries is also REJECTED — same logic, no real gap to close, semis already overweight.

Follow-on: New FDF logged (Methodology, High severity) for the dashboard sleeve classifier bug. All 11 sleeve actual_pct values currently flagged 'estimate pending bottom-up rebuild' should be treated as suspect until classifier fix lands. Top 10 concentration table + Look-Through page remain reliable for ground-truth exposure questions.

P-DECIDE-EXECUTE action read: implied action = none. Cash binding: $80K available cash unaffected. Drift: NVDA/AVGO/SMH near ATH, MU above consensus — adding here is the chase.

   - GRNY look-through (AMD, KLAC, AVGO, NVDA): ~$19,000 = 1.06%
   - GRNJ look-through (LITE, ON, FN, SANM, LSCC): ~$10,000 = 0.56%
   - MAGS look-through (NVDA component ~14% weight): ~$22,700 = 1.27%
   - Total: ~$395,200 = ~22.1% of $1.788M
4. Conclusion: ~22.1% actual vs 18% target = ~4pp OVERWEIGHT. Operator's perceived underweight was a 10pp measurement error.

Decision: NO BUY. The chase instinct (perceived gap + price advance) fired against an artifact. Adding semis here would be P-ALPHA violation (gap-filling not a buy thesis) plus chasing names at/near ATH (NVDA, AVGO, SMH) or already extended above consensus (MU +13.7% above PT).

Secondary decision: Path B (proposed in same chat turn) of conditional AMD <$320 / ANET <$160 entries is also REJECTED — same logic, no real gap to close, semis already overweight.

Follow-on: New FDF logged (Methodology, High severity) for the dashboard sleeve classifier bug. All 11 sleeve actual_pct values currently flagged 'estimate pending bottom-up rebuild' should be treated as suspect until classifier fix lands. Top 10 concentration table + Look-Through page remain reliable for ground-truth exposure questions.

P-DECIDE-EXECUTE action read: implied action = none. Cash binding: $80K available cash unaffected. Drift: NVDA/AVGO/SMH near ATH, MU above consensus — adding here is the chase.

NOT live until Council Phase 1+2 review passes.

Policy Tape source stream page (352c50314bb681848a7fcbb5f91ed1a6) - 3rd durable research source stream alongside Meridian (Critical Minerals) and Fundstrat (macro/sectors). 4 sections: Implementation Log / Sector-Ticker Mapping / Midterm Dynamics / Tail-Risk Catalogue. Tail-risk catalogue is hedge-instrument planning surface, NOT trade authorization.

3. BMNR/ETH Thesis subpage (352c5031-4bb6-8154-87d4-d930f112bcba) under ACT - first thesis candidate. Source stream #1 of 3+ absorbed (operator ChatGPT extracts). Policy Tape framework recommends 1-2% portfolio max for political/tail hedges + 5-10% cash/SGOV (currently 1.3%) + GLD/GDX as non-political hedge sleeve. With midterm dynamics 4-5 months away, hedge book design is a Sess #33 high-leverage priority. Logged as separate FDF Sess #32.

text.content:
command spec (build target first per P-ADOPTION) and Dashboard v0.4 schema spec (reference doc, not CI rule).

WHY EACH NEW PRINCIPLE:
• P-CONVICTION-TIER (generational lane sizing tier) — operator executed full LEU Tier 2 sizing in one move (~$102K = 5.7% portfolio post-fills, was supposed to be staged $25K pre-Q1 + ramp post-Q1). Default $15-30K Tier 1 sizing was inadequate for genuinely high-conviction multi-source-validated entries. Three tiers now: T1 default $15-30K / 10-15% sleeve cap; T2 $50-150K / up to 10% per name (requires 2+ source streams + mechanism stops + Decisions Log override + earnings-cycle hold pre-commit); T3 conviction override up to 15% portfolio cap (4-source convergence + Council Phase 1+2). Funding hierarchy explicit: trim factor exposures > trim ETF satellites > trim lower-conviction singles > cash. Single-entry rule explicit override permitted with Decisions Log entry.
• P-DECIDE-EXECUTE (document ≠ execute) — recurring failure mode: framework decision documented (sleeve target raise, factor reclassification, lane mode change, position re-tier) but capital deployment not surfaced as actionable until later turn. Operator should not have to remember to act on decisions after system documents them. Every framework decision now triggers parallel action read covering current price vs target entry / recent drift / jump risk if delayed / actionable amount remaining (cash binding check). Apply in Step 5 of Update Workflow and to ranked-priority responses outside

text.content: Sess #30 attempted twice (v0.2, v0.3) to implement the v8.0 spec promise of a persistent Update Dashboard React JSX artifact reading from 🔄 Dashboard Scratch via Notion MCP on artifact load. Both implementations failed in mobile artifact context with 'Network error: Invalid response format' — request rejected upstream before reaching the parser. The Sess #26 POC v0.2 that allegedly validated 'Notion MCP works from artifact context' was never tested on mobile. P-ADOPTION says: when a component's forcing function fails, defer or retire — don't keep building. Decision: deprecate the Notion-fetch architecture; canonical pattern is now embedded-render. Chat session composes JSON, writes to 🔄 Dashboard Scratch (canonical cross-session persistence layer, unchanged), AND renders artifact inline with JSON embedded as a constant. Operator gets a fresh artifact each

text.content: Operator clarified Apr 29 that P-DUAL-SRC (Rules live in BOTH project-knowledge Framework .docx AND Custom Instructions, by design) can be released. Permission granted for ground-up redesign of what should exist in project files vs CI, with no need for redundancy unless there is a true need. v7.5 incremental patch approach (1A after-hours staleness, 1B refresh existing .docx, 1C split into operational reference) superseded same-day by v8 architecture. v8 DESIGN. CI scope: behavioral/operational only (~250-400 lines). What Claude needs to know WITHOUT searching to do the right thing: command recognition, update workflow skeleton, source-citation discipline (so re-reads trigger), critical safety principles (P-SECRETS, P-TASKS-BOUNDARY, P-EXTRACTOR), data hierarchy (P-FMP, FMP authoritative, Twelve Data dormant), after-hours staleness check in Step 1, pointer to where principles + posture + recommendation method + operational + governance live. CI lists all principle IDs with one-line descriptions so Claude knows they exist; elaboration is in project knowledge. Project knowledge scope: 5 focused .docx files, single-source canonical, loaded on-demand via project_knowledge_search: (1) Principles_v8.docx - all principles with stable IDs full text; (2) Trading_Lanes_v8.docx - 4 lanes Buy-and-Hold/Generational/Speed/Tactical, qualification tests, Tactical Lane Mode A/B with locked v7.3 parameters table, lane-specific exit triggers; (3) Recommendation_Method_v8.docx - 12 data points, source discipline mechanics, factor-correlation, false-diversification, opportunity ranking, trade card format; (4) Operational_Reference_v8.docx - data sources (full FMP endpoint catalog, Twelve Data dormant cold-standby, AV retired, web_search, portfolio-pdf-extractor), Notion Resources tables with full IDs, write destinations, account structure & constraints, external references, conflict resolution; (5) Governance_v8.docx - Model Council Phase 1-3 execution, FDF auto-evolution dual-counter ladder, periodic reevaluation, Trade Journal Outcome Protocol fields, cross-project coordination, year-1 Tactical Lane probationary measurement. P-DUAL-SRC RETIRED. Single source per concern. Trade-off accepted: divergence-detection between CI and project knowledge no longer fires because there is nothing to compare; mitigation = changelog-driven explicit version bumps and CI references project knowledge by filename so search-on-CI-read produces the canonical source. After-hours staleness check (1A from prior plan) PRESERVED into v8 CI Step 1. Investment_Rules

text.content:
Sess #28 update authorized full Plan A execution per Sess #28 Pre-Stage Reallocation Opportunity Map (351c50314bb681248534f92126e64530), built autonomously overnight Apr 28→29. Operator confirmed 3 tier choices: Plan A ($75K total), all definite sells only, BK→XLF swap.

FDF #1 RESOLUTION: Financials at 4.20% vs Fundstrat Apr 9 directive 12.5% — 13 days standing. Plan A closes the gap to ~7.0% (mid of revised 5-9% target band). Funded entirely from cash + cleanup; ZERO held-conviction trims; preserves P-AI-MOMENTUM ~50% AI concentration.

BK→XLF SWAP RATIONALE (Sess #28 fresh insider pull): BK shows 4 insider sells Apr 17, 2026 totaling ~$6.6M and ZERO insider buys in 90 days. Sells: McCarthy (SEVP & GC) 30,000 sh @ $136.50; Perez (SEVP) 12,504 sh @ $137.01; Kurimsky (Corp Controller) 5,290 sh @ $136.02; Hobbs (SEVP) 297 sh @ $137.05. Plus 11 director DSU awards (mechanical/disclosed exception). All discretionary sells clustered post-Q1 print. Per P-INSIDER ('insider selling + no insider buying in 90 days = don't chase up'), even though current price $133.54 is below the insider exit prices, the absence of any buy + size of the GC's $4.1M sale + clustering = meaningful negative signal. XLF gets BK exposure inside its basket without the single-name signal.

PLAN A FINAL TARGETS (with BK→XLF swap):
• XLF +$30K (was $20K + $10K BK swap): 386 sh Parents Rollover IRA + 193 sh SKB RH Trad IRA = 579 sh total
• JPM +$15K: 24 sh Parents Rollover IRA + 24 sh SKB Schwab Indiv = 48 sh
• GS +$5K: 5 sh Parents Joint (already 8 sh → 13 sh)
• IBB +$15K: 60 sh SKB RH Trad IRA + 30 sh Parents Rollover IRA = 90 sh
• TLT +$10K: 60 sh Parents Rollover IRA + 50 sh SKB RH Roth IRA = 110 sh (NEW position)
• BK: SKIPPED (insider revision)

DEFINITE SELLS (~$17.5K freed):
• Parents Joint: NMRK 211.87 sh + WMT 15.06 sh
• Parents Rollover IRA: CIBR 100 sh + ASTS 30 sh + SOFI 50 sh + POET 100 sh
• SKB Schwab Indiv: CIBR 20 sh
• SKB RH Indiv: crypto residue (TRUMP/AAVE/ETH/SOL ~$157)
• PROBABLY-SELLS DEFERRED: COST 6 sh, HOOD 2 calls, IVES 4 calls, GRNY 5 calls — operator chose definite sells only.

FDF status implications:
• FDF #1 (Financials 3% vs Fundstrat 12.5%) → Patched once trades execute; 90-day retention monitor Apr 29 → Jul 28, 2026.
• Sleeve Targets DB Financials target NOT yet revised from 3% to 5-9% in this session — separate operator confirmation required for that DB write. Plan A execution implicitly assumes the revised target.
• NEW FDF created Sess #28: BK insider cluster Apr 17 (Severity=Medium, Source Discipline) — documented for ongoing monitoring even though position skipped.

P-CITE source tags:
• Fundstrat Apr 9 Sector Allocation Update (project knowledge .pdf, re-read fresh in Pre-Stage build Apr 28→29) — Financials tactical Top 3 +2%, +4.1% MoM portfolio weight to 12.5%; Top 5 Apr 22 includes BK + GS
• Account PDFs (7/7 PASS extractor Apr 28 4:43 PM ET) — $1,772,376.85 grand total, sleeve % source of truth
• Own Analysis — 12-data-points refresh on each candidate this session via FMP batch quote + grades + insider trade pulls

30-day robustness test: ALL Plan A positions pass — diversified or large-cap with mature businesses; no monitoring burden; trailing stops in trade cards catch tail risk.

Follow-up items at fill (operator action required):
• Log each trade as Trade Journal entry with Lane=Buy-and-Hold, Generational=No, Speed Lane Used=FALSE, Tactical Lane Mode=N/A, Comparator ETF=XLF (for Financials adds) or IBB/TLT (for non-correlated)
• Operator confirms FDF #1 Patched after fills logged
• Sleeve Targets DB Financials revision (3% → 7% target) needs separate operator confirmation

text.content: Sess #28 confirmation pass on autonomous overnight Reallocation Opportunity Map (351c50314bb681248534f92126e64530). Plan A approved, BK→XLF swap applied per insider finding (4 sells $6.6M Apr 17, zero buys 90d), cleanup constrained to 'no options + no positions <$200' per operator. Closes FDF #1 (Financials 3% target vs Fundstrat Apr 9 12.5% directive, 13 days standing). Funding from cash + 7 zombie/no-thesis sells. NO held-conviction trims required per P-AI-MOMENTUM (Apr 23 directive). RATIONALE: Financials sleeve was the only big sleeve deviation with strong Fundstrat conviction backing (tactical Top 3 +2%, +4.1% MoM portfolio weight increase, 69.7% sector members above 20-day MA). Materials and CritMin had bigger %-deviations but weaker conviction signals. Non-correlated infill (IBB biotech beta 0.90 + TLT long-duration ~0 beta) genuinely lowers portfolio variance while keeping ~50% AI concentration. The 13-day delay on FDF #1 had likely already cost some upside (XLF flat-to-up over period). Acting now is better than acting more. EXECUTION (operator places at brokers, refresh quotes at 9:30 AM ET cash open before placing): SELLS $17,333: Parents Joint NMRK 212sh + WMT 15sh; Parents Rollover IRA CIBR 100sh + ASTS 30sh + SOFI 50sh + POET 100sh; SKB Schwab Indiv CIBR 20sh. BUYS $73,776: XLF $30,021 (386sh Parents Rollover + 193sh SKB RH Trad IRA — incl $10K BK swap), JPM $14,950 (24sh Parents Rollover + 24sh SKB Schwab Indiv), GS $4,633 (5sh Parents Joint adds to existing 8sh), IBB $15,103 (30sh SKB Schwab Indiv + 60sh SKB RH Trad IRA), TLT $9,069 (25sh Parents Rollover + 30sh SKB RH Trad IRA + 50sh SKB RH Roth IRA). Net cash drawdown ~$56K (gross $80K → ~$24K post-deployment). All 5 buy positions pass 30-day buy-and-hold robustness test. BK skipped per insider cluster — McCarthy SEVP&GC sold 30,000sh @$136.50, Perez SEVP 12,504sh @$137.01, Kurimsky CC 5,290sh @$136.02, Hobbs SEVP 297sh @$137.05, all Apr 17 post-Q1. Zero insider buys 90d. Per P-INSIDER, signal warranted swap to XLF (BK is held inside XLF basket). Sleeve Targets DB revision deferred (Financials 3% → 7% mid of revised 5-9% band) until trades execute. FDF #1 marked Patched with retention monitor Apr 29 → Jul 28, 2026.

text.content:
OPERATOR-CALIBRATED LOCK (Sess #25 Apr 28 PM, post-close). v7.2 CI had locked Council-default parameters as a placeholder pending operator selection (FDF 350c5031-4bb6-811e-9995-f61706cafcd0). Operator requested walk-through-each-parameter discussion before commit (rejected: locking v7.2 defaults; rejected: Council Phase 1 review — explicitly 'we can decide ourselves; Council adds noise here'). Sequential discussion yielded the following spec, with 5 of 7 parameters diverging from v7.2 defaults.

=== LOCKED SPEC ===

P1 TIME STOP: T+45 default + T+90 override (HYBRID — DIVERGENCE)
• v7.2 default was: T+45 hard, no extensions; T+90 only if setup data justifies (deferred)
• Locked: T+45 default. T+90 override available IF Catalyst Calendar entry has dated event >45d AND operator writes Decisions Log entry at trade entry citing the dated catalyst as override justification
• Forcing function: DL entry citing the dated event. Without DL entry, T+45 hard applies. P-ADOPTION compliant.
• Rationale: hybrid resolves v7.2's 'deferred' T+90 question. Allows Mode B Catalyst-Ride trades structured around 60-90d catalysts (DOE announcements, Senate votes) without forcing T+45 close that defeats trade thesis.

P2 STOP-LOSS: -10% close-basis broker GTC (matches v7.2)
• R:R 1.5x vs +15% Mode A profit-take; breakeven ~50% win rate after fees + ST tax in taxable accounts
• -$2,700 max loss per trade at 1.5% sizing (P4)
• Locked as broker GTC OCO leg — enforced regardless of Mode A vs Mode B

P3 PROFIT-TAKE: TWO-MODE SETUP-TYPED ARCHITECTURE (STRUCTURAL DIVERGENCE)
• v7.2 default was: +15% hard (full exit) for all Tactical Lane trades
• Locked: TWO MODES, declared at qualification:
  ○ Mode A (Mechanical, DEFAULT): broker OCO with both legs +15% / -10% · T+45 calendar entry · NO Decisions Log entry required at qualification · most trades sit here
  ○ Mode B (Catalyst-Ride, OPT-IN): broker GTC -10% stop only (NO +15% leg) · Catalyst Calendar Trade Review entry at catalyst-resolution-minus-1 · T+45/T+90 hybrid time stop as outer bound
• Mode B activation requires ONE Decisions Log entry at trade entry with 4 mandatory fields: (1) specific dated catalyst with date, (2) catalyst-as-thesis articulation ('This trade's payoff thesis is X happens at [date]'), (3) source authority justification (which of #2/#3/#4 from hierarchy), (4) hold-through OR exit-pre-resolution declaration
• NO minimum days-to-catalyst threshold — any specific dated catalyst qualifies. The DL articulation IS the discipline; if operator can't articulate catalyst-as-thesis, trade defaults to Mode A.
• Polymarket binary catalysts: exit-pre-resolution MANDATORY (no hold-through option, hard rule preserved from v7.2)
• Non-binary catalysts (DOE/earnings/FDA/Senate vote): operator's choice between hold-through or exit-pre-resolution at entry, declaration immutable post-entry
• Rationale: operator pushed back on pure-mechanical +15% hard — exiting at +15% on a trade structured to ride into a 60-day-out catalyst defeats the trade thesis itself. Two-mode architecture resolves this by making mode declaration explicit at qualification rather than forcing a single rule across heterogeneous setup types.
• Selectivity discipline: Mode A is default; Mode B is the deliberate exception. Year-1 review reports the Mode A/B split as a self-audit signal; no auto-FDF threshold (operator preference: 'don't overcomplicate').
• Year-1 measurement: realized return measured SEPARATELY for Mode A vs Mode B vs comparator ETF + SPY. If one mode is alpha-positive and the other isn't, the loser retires (granular retire decision, not lane-wide).

P4 PER-TRADE SIZING: 1.5% portfolio ~$27K (DIVERGENCE from v7.2 default 1.0%)
• At $1.8M portfolio: $27K per trade · -$2,700 loss at -10% stop · +$4,050 win at +15% Mode A profit · 0.15% portfolio drag per loss
• Concurrency cap binding effect: 3 concurrent trades max at 1.5% sizing × 5% aggregate cap (vs 5 concurrent at 1.0% sizing)
• Annual alpha math at 50% Mode A win rate / 12 trades: +0.225% portfolio = ~$4,050/year (small absolute, but Tactical Lane purpose isn't pure alpha; it's structural permission to act on short-term opportunities without violating buy-and-hold default)
• Rationale: operator explicitly chose 'wins/losses more meaningful' over lower stakes. Year-1 alpha signal stronger with 1.5% sizing (each trade moves needle more), making retire/continue decision cleaner.

P5 AGGREGATE CAP: 5% portfolio (matches v7.2)
• At 1.5% sizing × 5% cap = 3 concurrent trades max
• Worst-case portfolio drag if all 3 hit -10% stop same week: -0.45% (vs -0.50% at v7.2 default 1.0%/5%/5)
• Slightly safer worst-case despite larger per-trade size (cap bites harder at 1.5% sizing)
• Rationale: 3% cap with 1.5% sizing too binding (max 2 concurrent); higher caps compromise quarantine principle

P6 SOURCE-AUTHORITY HIERARCHY: 4 SOURCES (STRUCTURAL DIVERGENCE — v7.2 had 5)
• v7.2 had: 1. Fundstrat Top 3 sector picks · 2. Newton + FMP corroboration · 3. Catalyst-driven w/ explicit dated catalyst · 4. Polymarket-driven catalyst plays (w/ hard constraint) · 5. Operator intuition
• Locked: 4 SOURCES — 1. Fundstrat Top 3 · 2. Newton + FMP corroboration · 3. Catalyst-driven (any dated event) · 4. Operator intuition
• Polymarket binary plays fold UNDER Catalyst-driven authority as a subtype with two added rules: (a) P-POLY internal-inconsistency check fires whenever Polymarket data is cited as rationale, regardless of which authority frames the trade; (b) Polymarket binary catalysts force exit-pre-resolution declaration at entry (no hold-through option, v7.2 hard rule preserved)
• Rationale: Mode B Catalyst-Ride architecture (P3) already absorbs Polymarket binary plays as 'Catalyst-Ride with binary subtype'. Listing Polymarket as a separate top-level authority was redundant once P3 was structured. Simplification, not loosening.

P6.5 SOURCE CONCENTRATION GUARD: 80% threshold (DIVERGENCE from v7.2 60%)
• v7.2 had: any source >60% of Tactical Lane qualifications over trailing 6 months → FDF
• Locked: 80% threshold (loosened)
• Rationale: Fundstrat dominance in operator's research stack means Fundstrat-driven qualifications likely approach 60% in normal operation, generating noise FDFs. 80% reserved for truly extreme single-source concentration (e.g., Fundstrat is the ONLY source for Tactical Lane qualifications over 6 months — that's a real signal). Self-audit value preserved at 80%; noise reduced.

=== RELATIONSHIP TO PRIOR DECISIONS ===
• Tactical Lane formalization decision (350c5031-4bb6-81af-9a12-f6220645cd8a, Apr 28 PM): remains ACTIVE. This entry is ADDITIVE — specifies the parameters that decision left as 'Council convergence on structure but divergence on parameters; operator decision required.'
• P-ADOPTION principle (350c5031-4bb6-810f-ba9f-ed1e3dd1f93a, Apr 28 PM): forcing functions for Mode B activation (DL entry + Catalyst Calendar entry + Trade Journal Mode field) demonstrate compliance.
• FDF 350c5031-4bb6-811e-9995-f61706cafcd0 (Tactical Lane parameter selection): marked Patched Sess #25; 90-day retention monitor window Apr 28 → Jul 27, 2026.

=== v7.3 CI PATCH SCOPE (operator-side paste required) ===
1. Header: v7.2 → v7.3, Apr 28, 2026 PM
2. Tactical Lane 'Locked Parameters' table: revised values per P1-P6.5
3. NEW subsection under Tactical Lane: Mode A / Mode B architecture (~250 words)
4. Source-Authority Hierarchy: 5 → 4 sources, Polymarket subtype rules added
5. Trade Journal Outcome Protocol: NEW field 'Tactical Lane Mode' (Mechanical / Catalyst-Ride)
6. Failure-Mode Guard #5: 60% → 80% threshold
7. v7.3 changelog block at top of CI

v7.3 CI patch text drafted Sess #25 in conversation; operator-side paste pending.

text.content:
Today's LEU -7.38% drop to $205.59 brought price to the upper edge of the CI 'BUY opportunity zone' ($180-205) at the same time mechanism stops remained intact. Strict 6-filter test passes 6/6: F0 anchor (acknowledged 10% nuclear concentration; LEU is anchor not deepening bet) ✓ · F1 NRC HALEU monopoly ✓ · F2 $3.8B backlog through 2040 ✓ · F3 -55.7% off ATH $464.25, +34.6% to consensus PT $276.67 ✓ · F4 $1.96B cash floor + thesis-2027-2035 ✓ · F5 24mo+ comfort ✓.

However, the framework-correct response is HOLD WITH CONVICTION, not ADD. Three constraints bind:
1. Single-entry rule satisfied (Apr 17 + Apr 23 swap = the entry; adding violates discipline)
2. Position already oversized at 4.6% portfolio (~$83K) vs $15-30K target; uses ~31-46% of generational lane budget on a single name
3. Cash binding at ~$1,200 portfolio-wide real cash (some Fidelity sub-accounts negative pending settlement)

30-day test for 'ADD': fails — answer to 'if Suraj does nothing for 30 days what specifically goes wrong?' is 'nothing — May 6 catalyst will resolve thesis direction, position is already held.'

Key insight to document: today's drop with mechanism stops intact validates the Apr 17 + Apr 23 single-entry sizing — the position IS the answer to 'buy LEU on weakness.' This pattern is the framework working as designed: having entered at full size means dip-add-temptation is structurally unavailable. Trailing-stops also explicitly inappropriate for generational positions per Apr 28 PM Council Phase 1 GEV finding (high-beta + high-IV → trailing stops produce whipsaw without thesis change).

Insider activity 90d: ALL mechanical RSU vesting + tax withhold (Brown SVP Apr 21 @ $191.55, Donelson SVP Mar 15 @ $209.64). No discretionary buys or sells. P-INSIDER disclosed-mechanical exception applies → NEUTRAL signal, neither buy signal nor 'don't chase up' signal.

Analyst sentiment: B. Riley Apr 24 Buy maintained (PT lowered $315→$295). UBS/Citi/JPM/Roth Cap all Neutral maintain in 2026. No downgrades since Aug 2025 (BofA Buy→Neutral). Range $137-$390 with avg $276.67 / median $265.50.

May 6 Q1 is the gate: TENEX subsequent-event language is the watch item; B. Riley Apr 24 PT-cut full-text read still pending operator action. If TENEX disclosure neutral-or-better → mechanism stops continue intact → drift normalizes. If TENEX halt language appears → reassess via mechanism stops, not panic-sell into already-oversized weakness.

This decision documents the pattern for future generational-position drift events: oversized + single-entry + mechanism stops intact + catalyst ahead = HOLD WITH CONVICTION. Not a missed opportunity; the original sizing IS the response.

rich_text.plain_text: Framework / governance

text.content: Phase 2 Council Nemotron meta-finding: all three Phase 1 architectural proposals (Thesis Ledger, CI decomposition, FMP cold-standby) added new systems WITHOUT addressing the adoption-mechanism problem. The operator's existing framework already shows this pattern — Trade Journal Outcome Protocol almost suffered the graveyard problem and was saved only by explicit Catalyst Calendar entries (Oct 14 LEU Review Date being gold-standard example). Without a forcing function, 'it will get used because it's useful' is wishful thinking. PROPOSED PRINCIPLE: P-ADOPTION — Every new framework component must specify an explicit forcing function (mandatory invocation trigger) before being approved for build. 'It will get used because it's useful' is not a forcing function. Adoption mechanisms include: mandatory invocation in command flows (e.g., update Step 1 mandatory read), entry-creation linkage requirements (e.g., Trade Journal entry MUST link to X), automated Catalyst Calendar entry generation, formula-column visual surfacing (FDF DB pattern). Without at least one forcing function, the component must be deferred or rejected. STATUS: candidate principle, awaiting operator confirmation for v7.2 inclusion. If approved, joins Governance principles cluster alongside P-LADDER and P-DUAL-SRC. Applied retroactively to Thesis Ledger (deferred per adoption-mechanism gap) and prospectively to all future framework additions.

text.content: Framework architecture (cross-cutting)

rich_text.plain_text: Framework architecture (cross-cutting)

text.content: Phase 1 Council (GPT-5.4 / Claude Opus 4.7 / Gemini 3.1 Pro Thinking) converged on Constitution + Operating Parameters split (CI + Notion). Phase 2 disconfirmation (Nemotron 3 Super, two runs, identical verdicts) successfully attacked the convergence on two grounds: (1) PRIVILEGE-LEVEL MISMATCH — CI in Project Instructions carries system-prompt weight; Notion page read via tool call carries user-content weight. Substitution is not privilege-neutral; user-content rules are demonstrably easier for the model to override or deprioritize, defeating the enforcement-decay protection decomposition claimed to provide. (2) P-DUAL-SRC CONTRADICTION — operator's framework explicitly treats divergence between two sources of rules as a tripwire (dangerous, not safe). Decomposition intentionally creates exactly this divergence. Phase 2 also flagged this as signal-laundered training-data convergence: three LLMs all reaching for 'Constitution + Config DB' pattern because that's what LLMs reach for when asked about long instruction sets, not because it fits this operator's specific framework. OUTCOME: monolithic v7.1 form factor stays. v7.2 and beyond will be full re-paste. 'No patches, no fragment assembly, full copy-pasteable output every time' rule remains correct. Future Claude in future sessions should NOT re-litigate this without new evidence.

text.content: Data infrastructure

rich_text.plain_text: Data infrastructure

text.content: Phase 1 Council 3-model convergence: retain Twelve Data as cold standby with degradation protocol. Phase 2 Nemotron (2 runs, verdict=Survives with modification): finding survives but Phase 1 spec was insufficient. RISK INSURED: low-probability/high-consequence FMP vendor outage during market hours; web_search alone can't reliably handle structured data needs in those scenarios. MODIFICATIONS REQUIRED to prevent drift-into-active-use: (1) Hard suppression of Twelve Data outside verified FMP outage — actively blocked, not 'available as fallback'; (2) Verified outage definition required — HTTP 5xx threshold, consecutive timeout count, FMP status page confirmation, exact threshold to be specified; (3) NO reconciliation between FMP and Twelve Data during normal operation — FMP authoritative, Twelve Data dormant, cross-checking creates noise floor (timestamp precision, last-trade aggregation differences) that violates P-FMP hierarchy; (4) Every Twelve Data invocation auto-creates Framework Drift Flag (Category=Data Quality, Severity=Medium) requiring verified-outage justification or escalates as drift. SUPERSEDES: Apr 17 'Data infrastructure redesign — FMP Starter primary' (now FMP Ultimate); Apr 17 'FMP data-routing overhaul — AV demoted' (AV now fully retired); Apr 24 'Council Round 4 — pivot preflight to Pipedream + FMP Starter' (Twelve Data role narrowed from preflight quote source to dormant outage standby). IMPLEMENTATION: build suppression protocol before re-activating Twelve Data integration; until protocol built, Twelve Data stays effectively retired.

text.content: OPERATOR CLARIFICATION 2026-04-28: posture is buy-and-hold default WITH selective short-term tactical opportunities, not pure buy-and-hold. v7.1 CI Trading Posture section is incomplete by omission. Phase 1 Council (3-model unanimous on 1a — formalize; explicit rejection of 1b expand-Speed-Lane and 1c exclude-from-framework). Convergence framing: 'quarantine + audit', NOT endorsement of more tactical trading. Speed Lane is defensive (concentration trims, exit-trigger execution); Tactical Lane is offensive (new exposure for alpha) — semantic mixing corrupts both. Excluding tactical from framework creates shadow-unaudited portfolio (worsens drift, doesn't solve it). CROSS-MODEL AGREED STRUCTURE: (a) exit-first design — precommitted mechanical exits (time stop + price stop + profit-take rule) before entry; no exits, no trade; (b) weekly-monitoring-compatible — rules out fast-decay tactics (most pure momentum bursts, post-earnings reactions, intuition-only setups); setups requiring daily attention disqualified; (c) hard aggregate cap (probationary year-1); (d) mandatory broker-level OCO orders at entry per Gemini's GTC OCO Test ('if you disappeared 30 days, would the position still be governed?'); (e) simplified Thesis Ledger schema when ledger exists — not full generational apparatus; (f) disqualifications: pure momentum, uncorroborated intuition, options/leveraged. CROSS-MODEL PARAMETER DIVERGENCE — operator decision required, not Council-resolvable: time stop T+45 (Claude Opus, Gemini) vs T+90 (GPT-5.5); stop-loss -8% (GPT-5.5) / -10% (Gemini) / -12% (Claude Opus); profit-take +15% hard (Gemini) / +15% with +10% scale (GPT-5.5) / +20% scale + trailing (Claude Opus); per-trade sizing 0.5% (GPT-5.5) / 1.0% (Claude Opus) / 1.5% (Gemini); aggregate cap 3-5%. 30-DAY TEST TREATMENT: Phase 1 unanimous on analogous test, not exemption — 'must be robust to neglect'. YEAR-1 PROBATIONARY: if comparator alpha vs sector ETF + SPY negative at ~12 months, retire the lane rather than iterate. Outcome tracking via Trade Journal Outcome Protocol with simplified schema. STATUS: principle-level decision logged. Specific parameter selection pending operator decision (FDF row created). v7.2 CI patch will integrate Tactical Lane spec once parameters chosen.

text.content: Sess #24 (Apr 28). MU Council Phase 1 (3 architecturally distinct models) returned. Current $503.22 = +10.4% above consensus $455.86 (median $450; range $310-$1,000). Gap NARROWING (Sess #22 +22.4% -> #23 +11.3% -> #24 +10.4%). Last MU grade Apr 8 UBS Buy maintained; NO post-ATH refreshes — Street simply hasn't updated PTs since $285->$531 run. Council validated HOLD posture: gap contains BOTH Street-lag AND extension; sequential ASP trajectory in late June Q3 = decisive variable; FY27+ SCA pricing structure (5-year SCA disclosed first time per Council) = key evidence to track. Gemini's >15% portfolio claim FALSE per PDF-verified Session State (MU = 4.0% portfolio-wide post-Apr-22 trim) — likely conflated with AI-urgency factor concentration ~48%. Council surfaced new data: Melius Apr 27 initiated MU at $700 PT (FMP not yet showing — verify next session). FORMALIZED CONDITIONAL TRIM RULE: If MU sequential ASP growth decelerates QoQ in late June Q3 print AND price >+15% above consensus PT at that time -> Trim 25% toward ~8% portfolio target. Single condition firing = no automatic action. Pairs price-level signal with fundamental-event confirmation (Council critique of magnitude-only rules accepted). text.content: 8 consecutive update budget-ceiling sessions (#11-#18) triggered P-REEVAL. Analysis: v7.0 briefing-first architecture only collapses Step 1 cost when preflight has run; when briefing is stale (as in Session #18), v6.3 multi-read fallback fires AND reads an ever-growing Session State on top. Root cause: no incremental mode in update + Session State has become append-only log (contains What Changed blocks for #9-#18 inline). ADOPT Fix A (decompose Session State, move historical session blocks to dated subpages, target Session State under 3K tokens) and Fix B (rename Sessions Cited to Recurrence Signals 60d, add touch_count column, verify formula columns, populate PCRA map, add Trade Review category). ADOPT Fix C (briefing freshness as explicit Step 1 gate) after A lands. DEFER Fix D (update-lite command split) — not worth scope if A works. DISCARD Fix E (preload FDF summary in Session State) — wrong layer; contradicts the goal of a lean Session State. Bundle A+B as one focused Session #20 stabilization session. Positive data: v7.0 mechanics are working where exercised — MU Speed Lane ran clean end-to-end (trim, 48h review, gating validated, post-trade drift +1.31%), post-paste verification 9/9 PASS, FDF active count stayed at 4. Budget problem is upstream of v7.0, not caused by it.
